You can either choose to select a protocol from the protocol drop-down list, or fill in the scheme in the address part of the profile. Certificates that are sent by servers using secure traffic (FTP over SSL, Webdav over SSL, and SSH) are silently accepted. The scheme identifiers and default port numbers of the five supported protocols are listed in the table below.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>The table below lists Scheme Identifier and Default Port Numbers of Five Supported Protocols</p><table><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th colspan="1">Protocol Name</th><th colspan="1"> Scheme</th><th colspan="1">Default Port Number</th><th colspan="1">Traffic Mode</th></tr><tr><td>ftp</td><td> ftp://</td><td> 21</td><td>Plain text </td></tr><tr><td>webdav </td><td> http:// </td><td> 80</td><td>Plain text </td></tr><tr><td>ftp + ssl </td><td> ftps://</td><td>990 </td><td>Encrypted &amp; Secure </td></tr><tr><td>webdav + ssl </td><td> https://</td><td>443 </td><td>Encrypted &amp; Secure</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">ssh</td><td colspan="1">ssh:// or sftp://</td><td colspan="1">22</td><td colspan="1">Encrypted &amp; Secure</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><ul><li><strong>Address</strong></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Address field is the central part of a profile. It can set other fields, except the Password field. An Address contains five different parts: scheme, username, hostname, port (number), and (remote) path. Except hostname, every part is optional. Depending on the circumstances, you might need to add a special port number and/or the remote path where you have write access. Even though an address is valid without the path part, most servers will require a path, and will not let you write to the root directory. In every case, scheme and username parts will take precedence over the selection in the Protocol Field or the content in the Username field. The following is the basic pattern for the address field:  </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">[scheme://][username@]hostname[:port][path]</p><ol><li><strong>scheme</strong>: <br />The supported schemes are ftp, ftps, http, https, ssh, or sftp. A scheme must be followed by “://”.</li><li><strong>username</strong>: <br />A username is needed for user authentication. It must be followed by “@”. </li><li><strong>hostname</strong>: <br />Must be either an IP address (for example, 10.1.1.195) or a human readable URL (for example, <a class="external-link" href="http://www.ftpserver.com/" rel="nofollow">www.ftpserver.com</a>), and a valid address.</li><li><strong>port</strong>: <br />A port number where the server is listed. It is only necessary if the port number is different from the default port for well-known services. It must be preceded by &quot;:&quot;. </li><li><strong>path</strong>: <br />The predefined path will start from the home directory of the specified username. Therefore, the report will be generated at the home directory of the username, e.g., if the address is <em><a href="ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish" rel="nofollow">ssh://10.1.1.97:22/mymodel/publish</a></em>, and the username is <em>developer</em>, the report will be generated at the home directory of the <em>developer</em> username. The designated path will be <em>/home/developer/mymodel/publish</em>.</li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Examples of valid addresses:</p><ol><li>10.1.1.195 (hostname only)</li><li>10.1.1.195/companyDav (hostname and path)</li><li>10.1.1.195:80/companyDav (hostname, port number, and path)</li><li><a href="ftp://www.ftpserver.com/" class="external-link" rel="nofollow">ftp://www.ftpserver.com</a> (scheme and hostname)</li><li><a class="external-link" href="mailto:john@10.1.1.195" rel="nofollow">john@10.1.1.195</a> (username and hostname)</li><li><a class="external-link" href="mailto:john@www.ftpserver.com" rel="nofollow">john@www.ftpserver.com</a>/users/john/reports (username, hostname, and path)</li><li><a href="ssh://john@10.1.2.35:22/reports" rel="nofollow">ssh://john@10.1.2.35:22/reports</a> (all parts)</li></ol><ul><li><strong>User Name</strong></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">This field specifies a username that will be used for authentication. If the address contains a username part, the content of the Username field will be ignored. It will be either filled in or overwritten with the username from the Address. A Profile with an empty username field is valid, but you will be prompted for a username and password every time you upload to the server using this profile.</p><ul><li><strong>Password</strong></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">A profile with a password must always be accompanied by a username. A Profile with a password, but not a username, is invalid and, therefore, cannot be saved. The password field uses an '*' to represent a character in a password. Saving a profile with a password will save the password in an encrypted form. For example, if the password is 'test', it will be encrypted to '0013BCA5590DE'. A Profile with an empty password field is valid, but you will be prompted for a password every time you upload a report to the server in this profile.</p><p><br /></p><p><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142480 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00859: Profile Migration Transformation

- page_id: `189142480`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142480/Profile+Migration+Transformation
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:16.815+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Transformation provides a way to migrate your model according specified mapping rules.

Profile migration transformation search-and-replaces the following elements:

- Applied stereotypes
- Tagged values
- Usages of one type with another type

Element symbols are updated according to model changes.

Before starting transformation you have to define transformation mapping.

true

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>Transformation provides a way to migrate your model according specified mapping rules.</p><p>Profile migration transformation search-and-replaces the following elements:</p><ul><li>Applied stereotypes</li><li>Tagged values</li><li><span style="line-height: 1.42857;">Usages of one type with another type</span></li></ul><p>Element symbols are updated according to model changes.</p><p>Before starting transformation you have to define transformation mapping.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1559d803-b9a2-44d9-ba79-24b69de5050a"><ac:parameter ac:name="all">true</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142481 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00860: Profile Migration Transformation mapping

- page_id: `189142481`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142481/Profile+Migration+Transformation+mapping
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:17.136+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools > Model Transformation Wizard > Profile Migration Transformation
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Before starting transformation you have to create transformation mapping rules.

To create mapping rules you need to create Dependency relationship between elements you want to transform.

Mapping rules can be created (dependency relationship can be created) between the following elements:

1. Stereotypes
2. Tags
3. Types

###### Creating mapping rules for Stereotype transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace Stereotype.

To create Stereotype transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Stereotypes which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceStereotype stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

Tag values of old stereotype are preserved when tag name and type of tag value is the same. For tag values with different names create mapping rule for tag transformation.

*ReplaceStereotype* stereotype has the following tags:

- disableNewTypeCreation tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want to perform transformation when target and source metaclasses are not compatible. For example, if you do not want that Class would be changed to Use Case.
- disableReplaceWhereSaveAsElementValue tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want that stereotype would be changed where it is used as Tag value (tag value is stereotype itself, for which you perform transformation).

###### Creating mapping rules for Tag transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace Tag (when tag names differs). For example, source stereotype has author Tag and target stereotype has name Tag.

To create Tag transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Tags which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceTaggedValue stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

To create mapping rule correctly, you have to create Dependency relationship not only between Tags, but also between Stereotypes of these tags (see mapping between stereotypes, which is described above).

###### Creating mapping rules for types transformation

This type of transformation is used to replace type. For example, to replace type of Attribute.

To create types transformation mapping rule

1. Create Dependency relationship between Types which you want to transform.
2. Apply ReplaceType stereotype to Dependency.
3. Perform transformation.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Before starting transformation you have to create transformation mapping rules.</p><p>To create mapping rules you need to create Dependency relationship between elements you want to transform.</p><p>Mapping rules can be created (dependency relationship can be created) between the following elements:</p><ol><li>Stereotypes</li><li>Tags</li><li>Types</li></ol><h4>Creating mapping rules for Stereotype transformation</h4><p>This type of transformation is used to replace Stereotype.</p><p> </p><p>To create Stereotype transformation mapping rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Dependency relationship between Stereotypes which you want to transform.</li><li>Apply<em> ReplaceStereotype</em> stereotype to Dependency.</li><li>Perform transformation.</li></ol><p> </p><p>Tag values of old stereotype are preserved when tag name and type of tag value is the same. For tag values with different names create mapping rule for tag transformation.</p><p><em>ReplaceStereotype</em> stereotype has the following tags:</p><ul><li><strong>disableNewTypeCreation</strong> tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want to perform transformation when target and source metaclasses are not compatible. For example, if you do not want that Class would be changed to Use Case. </li><li><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">disableReplaceWhereSaveAsElementValue</strong> tag. By default false. Set this tag to true if you do not want that stereotype would be changed where it is used as Tag value (tag value is stereotype itself, for which you perform transformation).</li></ul><h4>Creating mapping rules for Tag transformation</h4><p>This type of transformation is used to replace Tag (when tag names differs). For example, source stereotype has author Tag and target stereotype has name Tag.</p><p> </p><p>To create Tag transformation mapping rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Dependency relationship between Tags which you want to transform.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceTaggedValue</em> stereotype to Dependency.</li><li>Perform transformation.</li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1e2a42d0-cd17-4429-80d5-f568021f30ef"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To create mapping rule correctly, you have to create Dependency relationship not only between Tags, but also between Stereotypes of these tags (see mapping between stereotypes, which is described above).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p> </p><h4>Creating mapping rules for types transformation</h4><p>This type of transformation is used to replace type. For example, to replace type of Attribute.</p><p> </p><p>To create types transformation mapping rule</p><hr /><ol><li>Create Dependency relationship between Types which you want to transform.</li><li>Apply <em>ReplaceType</em> stereotype to Dependency.</li><li>Perform transformation.</li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145986 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00861: Profiling changes

- page_id: `189145986`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145986/Profiling+changes
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:08.934+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > UML 2.5.1 support
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The previously implemented UML profiling metamodelused Instance Specifications, Slots and Value Specificationsto store the tagged values ofappliedstereotypes.Since that approach required two model elements (Slot and Value Specification) to store each tagged value, it has been optimized.

The InstanceSpecifications, Slots, and ValueSpecifications are no longer used to store profiling data.Instead,theElementdirectlyreferences the TaggedValue that is used to specify the Boolean, Integer, Real, String, or Elementtaggedvalues of the applied stereotype.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Profiling changes in the UML metamodel.

As a consequence, expressions based on the previously implemented profiling data storing metamodel no longer work; they have to be updated manually.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

## PAGE 00862: Profiling tool

- page_id: `189144540`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`

2049646004

2049646007

2049646000

**On this page**

43

2049646006

You can use $profiling to access meta-model information.

****

###### **$profiling.getGeneralizationName(modelName)**

Return a generalization model of modelName.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A list of generalization model names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getDeclaringElementName (modelName, propertyName)**

Retrieve the meta-model name which is the declared property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getPropertyTypeName (modelName, propertyName)**

Retrieve property types from meta-model names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A property type name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getPropertyTypeName (element, propertyName)**

Retrieve property types from an element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A property type name. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperties(modelName)**

Retrieve a collection of element property names from meta-model names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | modelName | java.lang.String | A meta-model name. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A collection of element property names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperties(element)**

Retrieve a collection of element property names from elements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection<String> | A collection of element property names. |

****

###### **$profiling.getElementProperty(element, propertyName)**

Retrieve property values of specified elements and property names.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
|  | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
| Return | - | java.lang.Object | A property object. |

****

###### **$profiling. getHumanPropertyName(element, propertyName)**

Return text representing a property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |
|  | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | Text representing a property name. |

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144915 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00863: Project characteristics

- page_id: `189144915`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144915/Project+characteristics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:21.499+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Use Case driven
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The Technical and Environmental factors are associated with the weight of the project. You have to assign a value to each factor. The value assigned to a particular factor depends on the degree of influence a factor has. The relevance values range from 0 to 5, where 0 means no influence, 3 is the average level of influence, and 5 means a strong influence.

#### Technical Factors

The table below lists Technical Factors

| Factor | Technical Factors | Weight | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| T1 | Distributed system | 2.0 | The system distributed architecture or centralized architecture. |
| T2 | Response adjectives | 1.0 | The response time is one of the important criteria. |
| T3 | End-user efficiency | 1.0 | The end-user efficiency. |
| T4 | Complex processing | 1.0 | The business process is very complex. |
| T5 | Reusable code | 1.0 | The project maintains high reusability. The design is complex. |
| T6 | Easy to install | 0.5 | The project requires simple installation. |
| T7 | Easy to use | 0.5 | User-friendly is one of the important criteria. |
| T8 | Portable | 2.0 | The project requires cross-platform implementation. |
| T9 | Easy to change | 1.0 | The project is highly customizable in the future. The architectural design is complex. |
| T10 | Concurrent | 1.0 | The project has a large numbers of users working with locking support. The architecture increases the project complexity. |
| T11 | Security features | 1.0 | The project has heavy security. |
| T12 | Access for third parties | 1.0 | The project is dependent on the third party’s control. Studying and understanding the third party is required. |
| T13 | Special training required | 1.0 | The application is so complex for the user that training must be provided. |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven Model Data E3.

##### Technical Factor Value

Technical Factor Value (TFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Technical factor by its weight.

```text
TFactor = value x weight
```

##### Technical Complexity Factor

Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) is obtained with the addition of 0.6 to the sum of TFactor multiplied by 0.01.

```text
TCF = 0.6 + (0.01 * TFactor)
```

#### Environmental Factors

The Table below lists Environmental Factors

| Factor | Environmental Factors | Weight | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| E1 | Familiar with RUP | 1.5 | Staff in the project are familiar with domain and technical details of the project. |
| E2 | Application experience | 0.5 | The application experience level. |
| E3 | Object-oriented experience | 1.0 | Staff in the project have basic knowledge of the OOP concept. The project is implemented on the object oriented design. |
| E4 | Lead analyst capability | 0.5 | The analyst who is leading the project has enough domain knowledge. |
| E5 | Motivation | 1.0 | The project motivates staff to work including how the software industry is going on. |
| E6 | Stable requirements | 2.0 | The requirements are clear, stable, and unlikely to change in the future. |
| E7 | Part-time workers | -1.0 | Part-time staff are working on the project. |
| E8 | Difficult programming language | -1.0 | Complexity of a programming language. |

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Use Case Driven Model Data E3.

##### Environmental Factor Value

Environmental Factor Value (EFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Environmental factor by its weight.

##### Environmental Factor

Environmental Factor (EF) is obtained with the addition of 1.4 to the sum of EFactor multiplied by -0.03.

```text
EF = 1.4 + (-0.03 * EFactor)
```

#### Project Estimation

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Variables of Use Case Project Estimation.

##### Adjusted Use Case Points

The Adjusted Use Case Point (UCP) is determined with the multiplication of Unadjusted Use Case Point (UUCP) by Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) and Environmental Factor (EF).

```text
UCP = UUCP * TCF * EF
```

##### Estimated Effort in Person Hours

The person hours multiplier or X hours is a ratio of the number of man hours (PHM) per Use Case Point based on past projects. If no historical data has been collected, industry experts suggest using a figure between 15 and 30. A typical value is 20.

```text
X hours = UCP * PHM
```

##### Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time

Divide X hours by the number of developers working on the project and working hours per day to determine Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time or Y days. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Y days to complete the project.

```text
Y days = X / nDev / hay
```

##### Estimated Effort in Working Days

Divide Y days by working days per month to get the estimated effort in working days or Z months. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Z month to complete the project.

```text
Z months = Y / d Month
```

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The Technical and Environmental factors are associated with the weight of the project. You have to assign a value to each factor. The value assigned to a particular factor depends on the degree of influence a factor has. The relevance values range from 0 to 5, where 0 means no influence, 3 is the average level of influence, and 5 means a strong influence.</p><h2>Technical Factors</h2><p>The table below lists Technical Factors</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 41.0032%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 11.5108%;" /><col style="width: 38.2554%;" /><col style="width: 12.0803%;" /><col style="width: 38.2674%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Factor</th><th colspan="1"><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">Technical Factors</span></th><th>Weight</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>T1</td><td colspan="1"><span>Distributed <br />system</span></td><td>2.0</td><td>The system distributed <br />architecture or centralized <br />architecture.</td></tr><tr><td>T2</td><td colspan="1"><span>Response <br />adjectives</span></td><td>1.0</td><td>The response time is one <br />of the important criteria.</td></tr><tr><td>T3</td><td colspan="1"><span>End-user <br />efficiency</span></td><td>1.0</td><td>The end-user efficiency.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T4</td><td colspan="1">Complex <br />processing</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The business process <br />is very complex.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T5</td><td colspan="1">Reusable <br />code</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The project maintains <br />high reusability. The <br />design is complex.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T6</td><td colspan="1">Easy to <br />install</td><td colspan="1">0.5</td><td colspan="1">The project requires <br />simple installation.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T7</td><td colspan="1">Easy to <br />use</td><td colspan="1">0.5</td><td colspan="1">User-friendly is one of <br />the important criteria.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T8</td><td colspan="1">Portable</td><td colspan="1">2.0</td><td colspan="1">The project requires <br />cross-platform implementation.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T9</td><td colspan="1">Easy to <br />change</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The project is highly <br />customizable in the future. <br />The architectural design is <br />complex.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T10</td><td colspan="1">Concurrent</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The project has a large <br />numbers of users working <br />with locking support. The <br />architecture increases the <br />project complexity.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T11</td><td colspan="1">Security <br />features</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The project has heavy security.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T12</td><td colspan="1">Access for <br />third parties</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The project is dependent on <br />the third party’s control. <br />Studying and understanding <br />the third party is required.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">T13</td><td colspan="1">Special training <br />required</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The application is so complex <br />for the user that training must <br />be provided.</td></tr></tbody></table><p> </p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Use Case Driven Model Data E3" ac:alt="Use Case Driven Model Data E3"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model-properties.png" /></ac:image><br />Use Case Driven Model Data E3.</h6><h3>Technical Factor Value</h3><p>Technical Factor Value (TFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Technical factor by its weight.</p><pre>TFactor = value x weight</pre><h3>Technical Complexity Factor</h3><p>Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) is obtained with the addition of 0.6 to the sum of TFactor multiplied by 0.01.</p><pre>TCF = 0.6 + (0.01 * TFactor)</pre><h2>Environmental Factors</h2><p>The Table below lists Environmental Factors</p><table class="relative-table" style="width: 52.8501%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.92608%;" /><col style="width: 31.7782%;" /><col style="width: 9.36774%;" /><col style="width: 50.0232%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Factor</th><th>Environmental Factors</th><th>Weight</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>E1</td><td>Familiar <br />with RUP</td><td>1.5</td><td>Staff in the project are familiar <br />with domain and technical <br />details of the project.</td></tr><tr><td>E2</td><td>Application <br />experience</td><td>0.5</td><td>The application experience level.</td></tr><tr><td>E3</td><td>Object-oriented <br />experience</td><td>1.0</td><td>Staff in the project have basic <br />knowledge of the OOP concept. <br />The project is implemented <br />on the object oriented design.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E4</td><td colspan="1">Lead analyst <br />capability</td><td colspan="1">0.5</td><td colspan="1">The analyst who is leading the <br />project has enough domain <br />knowledge.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E5</td><td colspan="1">Motivation</td><td colspan="1">1.0</td><td colspan="1">The project motivates staff to work <br />including how the software industry <br />is going on.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E6</td><td colspan="1">Stable <br />requirements</td><td colspan="1">2.0</td><td colspan="1">The requirements are clear, <br />stable, and unlikely to change <br />in the future.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E7</td><td colspan="1">Part-time <br />workers</td><td colspan="1">-1.0</td><td colspan="1">Part-time staff are working on <br />the project.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">E8</td><td colspan="1">Difficult <br />programming <br />language</td><td colspan="1">-1.0</td><td colspan="1">Complexity of a programming <br />language.</td></tr></tbody></table><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="model-properties-b.png" /></ac:image><br />Use Case Driven Model Data E3.</h6><h3>Environmental Factor Value</h3><p>Environmental Factor Value (EFactor) is obtained with the multiplication of the value of each Environmental factor by its weight.</p><h3>Environmental Factor</h3><p>Environmental Factor (EF) is obtained with the addition of 1.4 to the sum of EFactor multiplied by -0.03.</p><pre>EF = 1.4 + (-0.03 * EFactor)</pre><h2>Project Estimation</h2><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Variables of Use Case Project Estimation" ac:alt="Variables of Use Case Project Estimation"><ri:attachment ri:filename="template-vars.png" /></ac:image><br />Variables of Use Case Project Estimation.</h6><h3>Adjusted Use Case Points</h3><p>The Adjusted Use Case Point (UCP) is determined with the multiplication of Unadjusted Use Case Point (UUCP) by Technical Complexity Factor (TCF) and Environmental Factor (EF).</p><pre>UCP = UUCP * TCF * EF</pre><h3>Estimated Effort in Person Hours</h3><p>The person hours multiplier or X hours is a ratio of the number of man hours (PHM) per Use Case Point based on past projects. If no historical data has been collected, industry experts suggest using a figure between 15 and 30. A typical value is 20.</p><pre>X hours = UCP * PHM</pre><h3>Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time</h3><p>Divide X hours by the number of developers working on the project and working hours per day to determine Estimated Effort in Scheduled Time or Y days. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Y days to complete the project.</p><pre>Y days = X / nDev / hay</pre><h3>Estimated Effort in Working Days</h3><p>Divide Y days by working days per month to get the estimated effort in working days or Z months. This means that with nDev developers, it would take Z month to complete the project.</p><pre>Z months = Y / d Month</pre>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145818 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00864: Project Constraints

- page_id: `189145818`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145818/Project+Constraints
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:02.219+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Code Engineering > C++ Code Engineering
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This chapter describes how to setinclude pathsfor an includesystem file.

###### Include system file example

If you include any system file, you must set the system file path as well.

###### System file paths

To set the include path

1. From the Options menu choose Project .
2. Expand Code Engineering , and choose C++ Language Options . Select the Include path check-box. Click the Use include paths button (...) appearing on the right side of the Project options dialog box.
3. The Set Include Path dialog appears.

###### Set Include Path dialog box

| Button | Action |
| --- | --- |
| Add | The Open dialog box appears for the select directory path. |

| Remove | Remove the selected Include path. |
| --- | --- |
| Up | Move the selected Include path upward. |
| Down | Move the selected Include path downward. |

Select the code-engineering set and then select**Properties**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

Finally, set the “Parse includes” property to “true”.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Parse Includes option

Parse Includes option

If the user sets the “Parse Includes” property to “false”, during reverse engineering of the C++ code, the modeling tool will not parse inside the header file; all unknown types will be created in the “Default” package of the model. The user can then solve keyword-specific problems in the library, speed up the reverse process, and remove unnecessary models inside the header file.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html

Select the Include path check-box. Click the <strong>Use include paths</strong> button (...) appearing on the right side of the <strong>Project</strong> options dialog box.</li><li>The Set Include Path dialog appears.</li></ol><h6>Set Include Path dialog box</h6><table><colgroup><col style="width: 31.174475%;" /><col style="width: 68.82552%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><th><p><span>Button </span></p></th><th><p><span>Action </span></p></th></tr><tr><td><p><span>Add </span></p></td><td><p><span>The </span><strong>Open </strong><span>dialog box appears for the select directory path. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><table><colgroup><col style="width: 31.174475%;" /><col style="width: 68.82552%;" /> </colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Remove </strong></p></td><td><p><span>Remove the selected Include path. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Up </strong></p></td><td><p><span>Move the selected Include path upward. </span></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Down </strong></p></td><td><p><span>Move the selected Include path downward. </span></p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><span>Select the code-engineering set and then select </span><strong>Properties</strong><span>.</span></p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-24_17-32-58.png" /></ac:image></p><p>Finally, set the “Parse includes” property to “true”.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="image2018-1-24_17-29-24.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9ed922b7-b8db-419b-a8b8-5f71d700fc5b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Parse Includes option</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>If the user sets the “Parse Includes” property to “false”, during reverse engineering of the C++ code, the modeling tool will not parse inside the header file; all unknown types will be created in the “Default” package of the model. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139880 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00865: Project disclaimer configuration

- page_id: `189139880`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139880/Project+disclaimer+configuration
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:31.048+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can create a project disclaimer configuration to display custom disclaimers upon project opening. The disclaimers can contain any information you want the project users to be aware of before opening the project. The project can only be opened once all of the specified disclaimers have been read.

#### NOTE: Disclaimers in used projects

Disclaimers in used projects

- If the disclaimer configuration is created in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space=''] , it must be located in a [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Sharing project data' space=''] of the used project for the disclaimer to be shown when opening the main project.
- If you are using multiple projects that contain disclaimers, the order in which they are displayed is determined by the order in which the projects were used (however, disclaimers created in the main project are shown first). The same order applies if you create multiple classes with the «disclaimerConfiguration» stereotype in the same project; the disclaimers of a class that was created first are also displayed first.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

##### Creating project disclaimer configuration

To create a project disclaimer configuration

1. Create a Class element and name it (e.g. 'Project Disclaimer Configuration').
2. Right-click the Class element. In the shortcut menu, click Stereotype > select «disclaimerConfiguration» from the menu, and click Apply . [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_1.png']
3. Right-click the Class element and open its Specification window.
4. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
5. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text and click the Create Value button. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_2.png']
6. In the Value box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer. [ATTACHMENT filename='Creating_project_disclaimer_configurations_3.png']
7. (Optional) To add more disclaimer messages, click the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button.
8. Click **Close**.

##### Adding more messages to the project disclaimer configuration

To add more messages to the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text, and on the right side of the window, click the [ATTACHMENT filename='add_button.png'] button.
4. In the Value box, type the message you want to have displayed in the disclaimer.
5. Click Close .

##### Removing messages from the project disclaimer configuration

To remove messages from the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to remove, and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_button.png'] button.
5. Click Close .

##### Editing messages in the project disclaimer configuration

To edit messages in the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to edit, and click the [ATTACHMENT filename='edit_button.png'] button.
5. Click Close .

##### Reordering messages in the project disclaimer configuration

The order of the messages displayed in the disclaimer depends on the order specified for the text values in the configuration's **text**tag.

To reorder messages in the project disclaimer configuration

1. Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.
2. In the Specification window, select the Tags property group.
3. Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select text .
4. On the right side of the window select the text value you want to move, and do one of the following:
  1. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_up_button.png'] button to move the text value up.
  2. Click the [ATTACHMENT filename='move_down_button.png'] button to move the text value down.
5. Click Close .

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can create a project disclaimer configuration to display custom disclaimers upon project opening. The disclaimers can contain any information you want the project users to be aware of before opening the project. The project can only be opened once all of the specified disclaimers have been read.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d4b99cb0-cac5-4f66-ae31-2d5a8739f549"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Disclaimers in used projects</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>If the disclaimer configuration is created in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Using other projects in a project" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[used local project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, it must be located in a <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Sharing project data" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[shared package]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> of the used project for the disclaimer to be shown when opening the main project.</li><li>If you are using multiple projects that contain disclaimers, the order in which they are displayed is determined by the order in which the projects were used (however, disclaimers created in the main project are shown first). The same order applies if you create multiple classes with the «disclaimerConfiguration» stereotype in the same project; the disclaimers of a class that was created first are also displayed first. </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(255,0,0);"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="disclaimers_example.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h3>Creating project disclaimer configuration</h3><p>To create a project disclaimer configuration</p><hr /><ol><li>Create a <strong>Class </strong>element and name it (e.g. 'Project Disclaimer Configuration').</li><li>Right-click the Class element. /><ol><li>Right-click the project disclaimer configuration Class element and open its Specification window.</li><li>In the Specification window, select the <strong>Tags </strong>property group.</li><li>Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select <strong>text</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the window select the text value you want to remove, and click the <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="19"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_button.png" /></ac:image> button. </li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Editing messages in the project disclaimer window.</li><li>In the Specification window, select the <strong>Tags </strong>property group.</li><li>Under «disclaimerConfiguration» select <strong>text</strong>.</li><li>On the right side of the window select the text value you want to edit, and click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit_button.png" /></ac:image> button. </li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol><h3>Reordering messages in the project disclaimer configuration</h3><p>The order of the messages displayed in the disclaimer depends on the order specified for the text values in the configuration's <strong>text window select the text value you want to move, and do one of the following:<ol><li>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_up_button.png" /></ac:image> button to move the text value up. </li><li>Click the <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="move_down_button.png" /></ac:image> button to move the text value down. </li></ol></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong>.</li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139980 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00866: Project partitioning

- page_id: `189139980`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139980/Project+partitioning
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:35.782+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

INLINE

In life, all large things are built from smaller parts. This also applies to large projects. For large models with several weakly dependent parts, it is advisable to split them into several separate project files (or project resources in a collaborative environment), also called used projects.

Project partitioning opens up additional possibilities, such as:

- Reusing the same model part (e.g. library) in several projects.
- Separate administration of different project parts. You can define different access rights in the collaborative environment for each used project. Separate developers or teams of developers can be assigned for each resource, clearly establishing responsibility boundaries and preventing inadvertent modifications when these projects are used in the read-only mode.
- Separate versioning. Each used project can have its own version history with its own tagging and branching, life cycle of development, feature-freezes, and stable releases.
- Increased performance on very large projects. Whenever used projects are marked as loaded manually, a user can selectively load only the necessary parts of the project. He/she can also open the isolated used project separately.

A model can be decomposed into separate projects. A used project is a part that can be separated from the main project and can be used for a specific purpose.

The modeling tool supports the following used project accessibility modes:

- Read-only
- Read-write

The decision to use a project as read-only or read-write depends on the maturity of a project and the organization ownership or responsibility rules for projects using other projects. If the library in the used project is complete (changes to it are not expected / likely / possible) it should be used in read-only mode. If a project is developed by a separate team and this team is responsible for this project and the project is reused in another project, it is recommended to use this project as read-only. This prevents inadvertent changes in the project by another team.

If a project is actively developed and evolves together with the projects that are using it, this project can be used in read-write accessibility mode. In this case you must be careful and remember that your changes to the used project will be reflected in other projects. Using Teamwork Cloud might be advisable in this case. And, of course, there can be mixed usage situations - when a project is used as read-only in some projects and as read-write in others.

Instructions on how to partition your project can be found in the following pages:

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="529a846e-6ed2-4434-b5aa-45c0a35dec83"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>In life, all large things are built from smaller parts. This also applies to large projects. For large models with several weakly dependent parts, it is advisable to split them into several separate project files (or project resources in a collaborative environment), also called used projects.</p><p>Project partitioning opens up additional possibilities, such as:</p><ul><li><span>Reusing the same model part (e.g. library) in several projects.</span></li><li>Separate administration of different project parts. You can define different access rights in the collaborative environment for each used project. Separate developers or teams of developers can be assigned for each resource, clearly establishing responsibility boundaries and preventing inadvertent modifications when these projects are used in the read-only mode.</li><li>Separate versioning. Each used project can have its own version history with its own tagging and branching, life cycle of development, feature-freezes, and stable releases.</li><li>Increased performance on very large projects. Whenever used projects are marked as loaded manually, a user can selectively load only the necessary parts of the project. He/she can also open the isolated used project separately.</li></ul><p>A model can be decomposed into separate projects. A used project is a part that can be separated from the main project and can be used for a specific purpose.</p><p>The modeling tool supports the following used project accessibility modes:</p><ul><li>Read-only</li><li>Read-write</li></ul><p>The decision to use a project as read-only or read-write depends on the maturity of a project and the organization ownership or responsibility rules for projects using other projects. If the library in the used project is complete (changes to it are not expected / likely / possible) it should be used in read-only mode. If a project is developed by a separate team and this team is responsible for this project and the project is reused in another project, it is recommended to use this project as read-only. This prevents inadvertent changes in the project by another team.</p><p>If a project is actively developed and evolves together with the projects that are using it, this project can be used in read-write accessibility mode. In this case you must be careful and remember that your changes to the used project will be reflected in other projects. Using Teamwork Cloud might be advisable in this case. And, of course, there can be mixed usage situations - when a project is used as read-only in some projects and as read-write in others.</p><p>Instructions on how to partition your project can be found in the following pages:</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="83a571da-de4a-4433-a85f-360eea3a5425" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139724 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00867: Project Properties dialog

- page_id: `189139724`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139724/Project+Properties+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:21.810+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Creating projects
- labels: ['forward-compatibility', 'compatibility-range', 'count-elements', 'project-statistics', 'detailed-statistics']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1428371638

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1428371640

1228009252

**On this page**

**53**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1428371639

#### EXCERPT: Note

BLOCK

The **Project** **Properties** dialog provides general information about a project. You can add a title, author, version, and project description using this dialog. 
 
 To open the **Project Properties**dialog

1. Open the project.
2. Select the Project Properties command from the File men u.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Project properties dialog.

#### TIP: Title

Title

The project title is assigned as a value of the project [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Documentation tab' space=''].

##### Compatibility range

To specify compatibility range

1. Enter the Version value.
2. Enter the Compatible with. The value must be less or equal to the Version value.
3. Click OK after you are done.

#### NOTE: Compatible With

Compatible With

**Compatible With** defines the compatibility range between two Standard/System profile versions. Once specified, it helps to avoid read-only constraints in TWCloud projects when you use earlier profile versions locally rather than those used in the server project.

When the **Version** of the Standard/System profile on your computer is earlier (e.g. 19.0 SP1) than the **Version** of this profile on TWCloud (e.g. 19.0 SP3), but higher or equal to the value of **Compatible with** (e.g. 19.0), you will still be able to edit TWCloud projects using the profile.

##### Advanced options

To use advanced options

1. Click More to see more options:

- 
  - See the list of shared packages in the Shared Packages tab. In this tab you can:
    - Define a preferred path to the particular package. Click the ... button [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Selecting elements' space=''] in the Preferred Path value box.
    - Set the project as a standard/system profile by selecting the **Standard/System Profile** check box. NoteThe **Shared Packages** tab is available for local projects only.
  - The Used Projects tab shows the projects and standard/system profiles used in the open project. Click the Open Project Usages button to open the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']**Used Projects** dialog , where you can modify project usages.
  - The Advanced tab shows project meta information. In this tab you can:
    - Reset all project element IDs. All project elements have their own ID (a unique identifier). While working with several related projects, you may receive a message about duplicated element IDs. After clicking the **Reset IDs** button, new IDs will be created for all project elements. NoteMake sure the project you are resetting IDs for is not used in other projects.
    - Reset the project ID. After clicking the **Reset Project ID** button, new IDs are created for the project. You cannot reset the project ID for server projects.

1428371636

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating projects' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with multiple projects' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142551 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00868: Project Statistics

- page_id: `189142551`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142551/Project+Statistics
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:22.060+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

##### Detailed project statistics

The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help us solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects.

#### NOTE: Confidential Information

Confidential Information

The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select **Confidential**to keep their names hidden.

To export detailed statistics

1. In the main menu, select Tools > Project Statistics . [ATTACHMENT filename='project_statistics_tools.png']
2. In the Project Statistics dialog, click the Export Detailed Statistics button. [ATTACHMENT filename='project_statistics_dialog.png']
3. Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default. [ATTACHMENT filename='export_detailed_statistics_options.png']
4. Specify other export options:
  - Select Include Used Project Statistics to generate and export used project statistics as well.
  - Select Confidential to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report.
5. Click Export .
6. When the statistics file is generated, click Close .

The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:

**Diagrams**

- Diagram dimensions
- Diagram count by type
- Diagram count by kind
- Symbol count by diagram

**Elements**

- Element count by UML type
- Element count by DSL type
- Derived properties count
- Property count by size
- Elements used for CDH mapping count
- Elements used for transformation mapping count
- Longest flat list count (longest flat list of elements under one element's root, such as under a package or a classifier)
- Smart package count
- Number of stereotype applications by stereotype name
- Number of stereotype tagged values by defining feature name and tagged value size
- Number of strings in the model by size and metaProperty
- Number of multi-references by size and metaProperty
- Number of profiling elements (stereotype instance, slots, and value specifications)

**Project/Environment options**

- Active/passive validation options
- Number of active validation rules by implementation language
- Number of code engineering options by language
- Symbol styles count
- Symbol styles properties count

**Hardware & system**

- Hardware (RAM, OS, disks size and type) and Java info (heap)
- Restricted dir size

**Teamwork Cloud projects**

- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache size in a binary blob
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision
- Teamwork Cloud project history depth
- Locked elements count
- Used projects count
- Root object count by type

**Performance**

- Time to build diagrams

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<h3>Detailed project statistics</h3><p>The project statistics feature calculates detailed statistics for the entire project and exports the results to a separate file. The exported statistical report will help us solve performance problems you may be experiencing. Project statistics are calculated in the main project and, if selected, in used projects.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="811f00dd-6f47-4a6a-99ee-daa68a80650c"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Confidential Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The exported file contains the names of the main project, used projects (if selected), and used elements. If they are confidential, you should select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep their names hidden.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To export detailed statistics</p><hr /><ol><li>In the main menu, select <strong>Tools </strong>&gt; <strong>Project Statistics</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_statistics_tools.png" /></ac:image></li><li>In the <strong>Project Statistics</strong> dialog, click the <strong>Export Detailed Statistics</strong> button. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_statistics_dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select data to export along with the OS and hardware, diagrams/elements by types, and project options statistics that are included by default.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="export_detailed_statistics_options.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Specify other export options:<ul><li>Select <strong>Include Used Project Statistics</strong> to generate and export used project statistics as well.</li><li>Select <strong>Confidential </strong>to keep the project element names hidden in the exported statistics report. </li></ul></li><li>Click <strong>Export</strong>.</li><li>When the statistics file is generated, click <strong>Close</strong>.     </li></ol><p><br /></p><p>The generated detailed project statistics file includes the following quantitative data:</p><p><strong>Diagrams</strong></p><ul><li>Diagram dimensions</li><li>Diagram count by type </li><li>Diagram count by kind</li><li>Symbol count by diagram</li></ul><p><strong>Elements</strong></p><ul><li>Element count by UML type</li><li>Element count by DSL type</li><li>Derived properties count</li><li>Property count by size</li><li>Elements used for CDH mapping count</li><li>Elements used for transformation mapping count</li><li>Longest flat list count (longest flat list of elements under one element's root, such as under a package or a classifier)</li><li>Smart package count</li><li>Number of stereotype applications by stereotype name </li><li>Number of stereotype tagged values by defining feature name and tagged value size</li><li>Number of strings in the model by size and metaProperty</li><li>Number of multi-references by size and metaProperty</li><li>Number of profiling elements (stereotype instance, slots, and value specifications)</li></ul><p><strong>Project/Environment options</strong></p><ul><li>Active/passive validation options</li><li>Number of active validation rules by implementation language</li><li>Number of code engineering options by language</li><li>Symbol styles count</li><li>Symbol styles properties count</li></ul><p><strong>Hardware &amp; system</strong></p><ul><li>Hardware (RAM, OS, disks size and type) and Java info (heap)</li><li>Restricted dir size</li></ul><p><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Teamwork Cloud projects</strong></p><ul><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache size on disk</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache size in a binary blob</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache latest blob size</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob size by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project local cache blob count by revision</li><li>Teamwork Cloud project history depth</li><li>Locked elements count</li><li>Used projects count</li><li>Root object count by type</li></ul><p><strong>Performance</strong></p><ul><li>Time to build diagrams</li></ul>

## PAGE 00869: Project tool

$project is a project reference enabling a template to return the project information.

##### **$project.getName()**

Returns a project name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project name. |

##### **$project.getTitle()**

Gets a project title.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project title. |

##### **$project.getFileName()**

Gets a project filename.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project filename. |

##### **$project.getExtension()**

Gets a project filename extension.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

This function is only for local projects.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project filename extension. |

##### **$project.getDirectory()**

Get a project directory name.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

This function is only for local projects.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A project directory name. |

##### **$project.getVersionList()**

Returns a list of version information from an open Server project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | - | - | - |
| Return | - | java.util.List | A list of com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion. |

##### **$project.getDiagrams()**

Returns all existing diagrams stored in a particular project, including diagrams in auxiliary resources.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram instances. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

$project.getDiagrams() returns all the diagrams in the selected scope, including diagrams in auxiliary resources.

##### **$project.getDiagrams(type)**

Returns existing diagrams of a given type stored in a particular project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A diagram type. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram instances. |

```text

```

- “Class Diagram” is a diagram type.

##### **$project.getPresentationDiagrams()**

Returns all existing presentation diagrams stored in a particular project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram views. |

##### **$project.getPresentationDiagrams(type)**

Returns all existing presentation diagrams of a given type stored in a particular project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A diagram type. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram views. |

```text

```

- “Class Diagram” is a diagram type.

##### **$project.isRemote()**

Returns the remote or non-remote state of a project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.lang.Boolean | Return true if a project is a remote project, otherwise false. |

##### **$project.isDirty()**

Returns true if that particular project was modified after it had been saved or loaded.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.lang.Boolean | Return true if a project was modified after it had been saved/loaded, otherwise false. |

##### **$project.getElementByID(id)**

Returns an element with a given ID.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | ID | java.lang.String | An element ID. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | An element with a given ID or null if the element with such ID is not registered in the project. |

```text

```

Returns a collection of all element IDs in a project.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | includeAuxiliary | boolean | Returns server modules including auxiliary resources if this value is true (false by default). |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of . |

##### **$project.getLocalModule()**

Returns a list of used local modules.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of . |

##### **$project.getLocalModule(includeAuxiliary)**

Returns a list of used local modules as well as auxiliary resources.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | includeAuxiliary | boolean | Returns local modules including auxiliary resources if this value is true (false by default). |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of . |

##### **$project.getAuxiliaryResource()**

Returns a list of auxiliary resources.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of . |

#### Project Version

The full class name is com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion. 
ProjectVersion is a class that represents some version of the project or module. It contains date, user, number, etc.

ProjectVersion contains the following properties:

| Property | Return type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| comment | java.lang.String | Returns a version committed comment. |
| date | java.util.Date | Returns a committed date. |
| dateAsString | java.lang.String | Returns a committed date as text. |
| number | long | Returns a committed version. |
| numberAsString | java.lang.String | Returns a committed version as text. |
| user | java.lang.String | Returns a committer’s name. |
| version | java.lang.Object | Returns a version object com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.IEsiVersionDescriptor for a Teamwork Cloud project. |
| tags | java.util.List<java.lang.String> | Returns all tags. |

```text

```

#### Project Module

The full class name is com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule. 
ProjectModule is a wrapper Class of an attached project (com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject) that represents a special kind of the project that is used as a component in the current project.

The following are some properties and methods of ProjectModule:

| Property / Method | Return type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| representationString | java.lang.String | Returns user-friendly representation string about the module. |
| description | java.lang.String | Returns project description of the module. |
| version | long | Returns a version number of the module. |
| remoteID | java.lang.String | Returns a remote ID for the module. |
| URI | java.net.URI | Converts EMF URI to java.net.URI. |
| versionList | java.util.List<> | Returns a list of version descriptors of the module. |
| isRemote() | boolean | Checks if the module is remote - from Teamwork Cloud. |

```text

```

/></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getLocalModule(includeAuxiliary)</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of used local modules as well as auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 91.46%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 8.33862%;" /><col style="width: 9.36508%;" /><col style="width: 13.9643%;" /><col style="width: 68.3664%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>includeAuxiliary</td><td>boolean</td><td>Returns local modules including auxiliary resources if this value is true (false by default).</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><h3><strong>$project.getAuxiliaryResource()</strong></h3><p>Returns a list of auxiliary resources.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 79.8574%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 5.9197%;" /><col style="width: 5.45455%;" /><col style="width: 18.8985%;" /><col style="width: 69.7667%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectModule"><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h2>Project Version</h2><p>The full class name is <span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectVersion</span>.<br />ProjectVersion is a class that represents some version of the project or module. It contains date, user, number, etc.</p><p><br />ProjectVersion contains the following properties:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property</th><th>Return type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>comment</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a version committed comment.</td></tr><tr><td>date</td><td>java.util.Date</td><td>Returns a committed date.</td></tr><tr><td>dateAsString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committed date as text.</td></tr><tr><td>number</td><td>long</td><td>Returns a committed version.</td></tr><tr><td>numberAsString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committed version as text.</td></tr><tr><td>user</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a committer’s name.</td></tr><tr><td>version</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>Returns a version object com.nomagic.magicdraw.esi.project.project.IEsiVersionDescriptor for a Teamwork Cloud project.</td></tr><tr><td>tags</td><td>java.util.List&lt;java.lang.String&gt;</td><td>Returns all tags.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="472aa08a-04cb-4ed8-92f4-c062256dd26d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Current version : $project.version
All version:
#foreach ($version in $project.getVersionList())
	Date : 				$version.date
	Number : 			$version.number
	Number As String : 	$version.numberAsString
	User : 				$version.user
	Comment : 			$version.comment
	Tags:
#foreach($tag in $version.tags) 
		- tag
#end
---
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Project Module</h2><p>The full class name is <span>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.tools.ProjectModule</span>.<br />ProjectModule is a wrapper Class of an attached project (com.nomagic.ci.persistence.IAttachedProject) that represents a special kind of the project that is used as a component in the current project.</p><p><br />The following are some properties and methods of ProjectModule:</p><table class="wrapped relative-table" style="width: 99.9444%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 10.6754%;" /><col style="width: 20.6899%;" /><col style="width: 68.6662%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Property / Method</th><th>Return type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td>representationString</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns user-friendly representation string about the module.</td></tr><tr><td>description</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns project description of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>version</td><td>long</td><td>Returns a version number of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>remoteID</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>Returns a remote ID for the module.</td></tr><tr><td>URI</td><td>java.net.URI</td><td>Converts EMF URI to java.net.URI.</td></tr><tr><td>versionList</td><td>java.util.List&lt;<ac:link ac:anchor="ProjectVersion" />&gt;</td><td>Returns a list of version descriptors of the module.</td></tr><tr><td>isRemote()</td><td>boolean</td><td>Checks if the module is remote - from Teamwork Cloud.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0c8dad29-d010-40c1-b029-6a38467dc0fe"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($module in $project.getModuleList())
	Name : $module.representationString
	Description : $module.description
	Version : $module.version
	RemoteID : $module.remoteID
	URI : $module.URI 
#if($module.isRemote())
#set($version = $module.projectVersion)
	Current module version :
		date : $version.date
		number : $version.number 
		numberAsString : $version.numberAsString
		user : $version.user
		comment : $version.comment
		tags: 
#foreach($tag in $version.tags)
- $tag
#end
	All module versions :
#foreach($version in $module.versionList)
		date : $version.date
		number : $version.number
		numberAsString : $version.numberAsString
		user : $version.usercomment : $version.comment 
		tags:
#foreach($tag in $version.tags)
- $tag
#end
----------
#end
#end 
	Shared module:
#foreach ($child in $project.getSharedModule($module))
		- $child.representationString
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139873 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00870: Projects with file attachments

- page_id: `189139873`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139873/Projects+with+file+attachments
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:29.661+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305056268

305056271

305056270

You can attach any type of file from the local file system to your project.

Attached files are bundled with the project and remain attached after transferring the project, keeping project references to these files not broken.

The contents of the attached file can be modified and the changes are instantly saved to the project. Different versions of a server project can refer to different versions of the same attached file.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

305056267

**Related pages**

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9f99122-b323-44fd-82f9-581a72d43a80"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305056268</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="63a8065a-18c0-48d5-953e-3b5fde9e7476"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305056271</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d2d5fa54-724c-41e5-9a83-49cab25f0dad"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305056270</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can attach any type of file from the local file system to your project.</p><p>Attached files are bundled with the project and remain attached after transferring the project, keeping project references to these files not broken.</p><p>The contents of the attached file can be modified and the changes are instantly saved to the project. Different versions of a server project can refer to different versions of the same attached file.  </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Concept of Project with File Attachments " ac:alt="Concept of Project with File Attachments "><ri:attachment ri:filename="project_with_file_attachments.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="090364b5-81e8-4ee5-adb0-a9dea2351601"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">305056267</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="6c953183-7162-4bdf-9a06-c148a29501c6" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141130 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00871: Properties dialog

- page_id: `189141130`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141130/Properties+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:55.199+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Saving as image
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Image export options

768901281

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Image export options

768901283

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Image export options

768901282

In the **Properties** dialog, you can specify image export properties specific to the image format selected in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space=''][CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space=''] dialog. For example, if the selected image format is .jpg, or .jpeg, the **Properties** dialog allows you to specify JPEG compression quality, image resolution (DPI), and exported image size.

To specify image export options

1. From the File menu, select Save as Image .
2. In the Save As Image dialog, specify the desired image format, and click the Options button.
3. In the Properties dialog, specify given properties.
4. Click **OK**. Property related informationIf you need more information about the properties provided in the **Properties** dialog, make sure that the **Show Description** button is pressed (as shown in the figure below), then select the desired property, and read its description in the description area on the bottom of the dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### This Properties dialog displays the image export properties specific to the .jpg, .jpeg image format. If the highlighted button is pressed, the description of the selected property is provided in the description area on the bottom of the dialog.

#### NOTE: Image export options

Image export options

Default image export properties can be changed manually both via the **Environment Options** and the **Project Options** dialogs. To learn more, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Diagram image export' space=''] page.

1341338422

**Related pages:**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Saving as image' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Save As Image dialog' space='']
