## PAGE 00907: Relation Map

- page_id: `189140355`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140355/Relation+Map
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:18.082+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagrams > Analysis diagrams
- labels: ['relation-map']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

393725676

393725679

393725677

The main purpose of all relation maps is to review and analyze relations among the elements and create new elements directly in the relation map. All modeling tools allow you to create the Relation Map Diagram, which provides you with a rapid review, analysis, and creation of relationships among the elements of the entire model. See the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The example of Relation Map Diagram. It is created by using the *CoffeeMachine sample model* that comes only with SysML Plugin.

You can create other predefined relation maps (Activity Decomposition, Structure Decomposition, Instance, Requirement Containment, Requirement Derivation Maps) only if you have the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='SysML Plugin Documentation' space='SYSMLP2024xR2'] installed. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Predefined Relation Maps' space='SYSMLP2024xR2']>]]>

INLINE

The following procedures explain how to work with relation maps.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="aba4ec33-dd2a-4dad-917f-75f6fd5018a4"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393725676</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="041523a8-3982-4331-bacc-505de5aa0c69"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393725679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a1ad3c7a-a129-4ded-aaf2-6271b2ce5133"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">393725677</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The main purpose of all relation maps is to review and analyze relations among the elements and create new elements directly in the relation map. All modeling tools allow you to create the Relation Map Diagram, which provides you with a rapid review, analysis, and creation of relationships among the elements of the entire model. See the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="relation_map_diagram.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The example of Relation Map Diagram. It is created by using the <em>CoffeeMachine sample model </em> that comes only with SysML Plugin.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5079db6a-df2d-4b77-a4dd-de769b02cc52"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can create other predefined relation maps (Activity Decomposition, Structure Decomposition, Instance, Requirement Containment, Requirement Derivation Maps) only if you have the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="SysML Plugin Documentation" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[SysML Plugin]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> installed. <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="SYSMLP2024xR2" ri:content-title="Predefined Relation Maps" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Learn more about predefined relation maps in SysML Plugin >>]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d860df6c-f473-4ecf-9f6a-02f8ae1f2e0b"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>The following procedures explain how to work with relation maps.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="1e98e6fd-8026-4f8b-aad2-02b974ba6e4c" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139996 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00908: Reloading used projects in a project

- page_id: `189139996`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139996/Reloading+used+projects+in+a+project
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:40.907+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Managing used projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The best way to access the latest changes in a used project is to reload it. All modifications made in the used project become visible after it is reloaded in the open project. Alternatively, used projects are reloaded automatically when the open project is reloaded, or the project is updated on the server.

To reload the used project

1. In the Containment tree, right-click the package of the used project.
2. From the shortcut menu, select **Project Usages** > **Reload**. If you open a used project for editing, be sure to save any changes you make. The modifications made appear after reloading the used project in the main project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>The best way to access the latest changes in a used project is to reload it. All modifications made in the used project become visible after it is reloaded in the open project. Alternatively, used projects are reloaded automatically when the open project is reloaded, or the project is updated on the server.</p><p>To reload the used project</p><hr /><ol><li>In the Containment tree, right-click the package of the used project.</li><li><p>From the shortcut menu, select <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Reload</strong>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88863e70-f636-4ffc-9b27-ad2ba1b806cf"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you open a used project for editing, be sure to save any changes you make. The modifications made appear after reloading the used project in the main project.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ol><p> </p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141066 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00909: Removing a relationship

- page_id: `189141066`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141066/Removing+a+relationship
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:45.348+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Working with paths and relationships
- labels: ['delete-relationship', 'delete-path']

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a relationship from the diagram pane

1. Select a relationship and click [ATTACHMENT filename='remove_from_diagram.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.

To remove a relationship from the model

1. Select a relationship and click [ATTACHMENT filename='delete.png'] in the Diagram toolbar.
2. In the Question dialog, click Yes to confirm the deletion.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To remove a relationship from the diagram pane</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a relationship and click <ac:image ac:title="Remove From Diagram (Delete)" ac:alt="Remove From Diagram (Delete)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_from_diagram.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.<br /><br /></li></ol><p>To remove a relationship from the model</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a relationship and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete.png"><ri:page ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image> in the Diagram toolbar.</li><li>In the <strong>Question</strong> dialog, click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm the deletion.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141165 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00910: Removing applied layout template from shape

- page_id: `189141165`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141165/Removing+applied+layout+template+from+shape
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:37:58.162+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Diagramming > Layout templates
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can remove the applied layout template from the Part, Class, or classifier shape, if you want to see their predefined style notations.

To remove an applied layout template from a shape

- Revert the Apply Template command by using the Undo button from the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Understanding the user interface' space=''] , or press Ctrl+Z.
- Delete the shape and display it again by using the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying parts and ports' space='']**Display Parts/Ports** dialog. Ensure that the Use Layout Template check box is clear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can remove the applied layout template from the Part, Class, or classifier shape, if you want to see their predefined style notations.</p><p><br /></p><p>To remove an applied layout template from a shape</p><hr /><ul><li>Revert the Apply Template command by using the <strong>Undo</strong> button from the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Understanding the user interface" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[main toolbar]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, or press Ctrl+Z.</li><li>Delete the shape and display it again by using the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Displaying parts and ports" /><ac:link-body><strong>Display Parts/Ports</strong> dialog.</ac:link-body></ac:link> Ensure that the <strong>Use Layout Template</strong> check box is clear.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189145955 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00911: Removing keyboard shortcuts from Macro

- page_id: `189145955`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189145955/Removing+keyboard+shortcuts+from+Macro
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:42:05.901+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > Macro Engine Documentation > Macro keyboard shortcuts
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a keyboard shortcut

1. Open either the (i) Environment Options or (ii) Macro Information dialog.
2. Select a keyboard shortcut from the Current keys box.
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . The selected keyboard shortcut will be removed from the Current keys box.

You can also remove a keyboard shortcut through the **Environment Options** dialog by clicking **Options** > **Environment** > **Keyboard** on the main menu.

To remove all keyboard shortcuts

1. Open either the (i) Environment Options or (ii) Macro Information dialog.
2. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] . All keyboard shortcuts will be deleted from the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box.

- The [IMAGE alt='' src=''] , [IMAGE alt='' src=''] , and [IMAGE alt='' src=''] buttons in the Macro Information or Environment Options dialog will be disabled if there is no keyboard shortcut either in the [IMAGE alt='' src=''] or [IMAGE alt='' src=''] box.

- The [IMAGE alt='' src=''] button will be enabled if the new keyboard shortcut entered has not been assigned to any other macro. You can assign more than one keyboard shortcut to a macro.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><span style="color: rgb(51,51,51);">To remove a keyboard shortcut</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open either the (i) <strong>Environment Options</strong> or (ii) <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog.</li><li>Select a keyboard shortcut from the <strong>Current keys</strong> box.</li><li>Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-btn.png" /></ac:image></strong>. The selected keyboard shortcut will be removed from the <strong>Current keys</strong> box.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3724a596-2ff5-4707-99d3-8b7e2fd983df"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can also remove a keyboard shortcut through the <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog by clicking <strong>Options</strong> &gt; <strong>Environment</strong> &gt; <strong>Keyboard</strong> on the main menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To remove all keyboard shortcuts</p><hr /><ol><li>Open either the (i) <strong>Environment Options</strong> or (ii) <strong>Macro Information</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-all-btn.png" /></ac:image></strong>. All keyboard shortcuts will be deleted from the <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current-keys-area.png" /></ac:image></strong> box.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e6fc5501-a8a8-4461-9d93-be15740a563e"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png" /></ac:image></strong>, <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-btn.png" /></ac:image></strong>, and <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove-all-btn.png" /></ac:image></strong> buttons in the<strong> Macro Information</strong> or <strong>Environment Options</strong> dialog will be disabled if there is no keyboard shortcut either in the<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="press-new-shortcut-key.png" /></ac:image></strong> or <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="current-keys-area.png" /></ac:image></strong> box.</li></ul><ul><li>The <strong><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="assign-btn.png" /></ac:image></strong> button will be enabled if the new keyboard shortcut entered has not been assigned to any other macro. You can assign more than one keyboard shortcut to a macro.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143519 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00912: Removing projects

- page_id: `189143519`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143519/Removing+projects
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:54.316+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To remove a project

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select the project you want to remove.
3. Click **Remove**. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

4. In the dialog that opens, select **Yes**. The project will be removed from Teamwork Cloud.

Data Manager

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To remove a project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Collaborate </strong>&gt; <strong>Projects</strong>. The<span> </span><strong>Manage Projects </strong>dialog opens.</li><li>Select the project you want to remove.</li><li><p>Click <strong>Remove</strong>. <br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_project.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><p><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">       4. In the dialog that opens, select </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Yes</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">. </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> </span> <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">The project will be removed from Teamwork Cloud.</span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a32fe1f2-e685-4ab7-96ba-8155e9178b41"><ac:rich-text-body>Removed projects are not deleted from the Teamwork Cloud database. To permanently delete resources from the database, use the <a href="https://docs.nomagic.com/display/TWCloud2024xR2/Data+Manager">Data Manager</a> tool.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140024 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00913: Removing recovered element symbol

- page_id: `189140024`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140024/Removing+recovered+element+symbol
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.564+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305696338

305696340

305696339

This is the case when a recovered element is depicted on diagrams of your project.

To remove a recovered element’s symbol, do one of the following

- On a diagram pane, select the symbol. On its smart manipulator toolbar click [ATTACHMENT filename='error_icon.png'] and then select Symbol of Recovered Element > Remove Symbol , as shown in the following figure.
- On the diagram pane, select the symbol and press Delete.
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the symbol and select Remove Symbol from its shortcut menu.

The symbol of the recovered element is removed from the diagram.

The same diagram can have many symbols of the recovered element. To remove all of them, repeat the same procedure for each symbol.

Remove Symbol

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Using smart manipulator toolbar to remove recovered element’s symbol

305696337

To remove all of them, repeat the same procedure for each symbol.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7ed3cd6-e601-4bf0-8745-df895523bf08"><ac:rich-text-body>You can use the <strong>Remove Symbol</strong> command for more than one symbol at a time. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=209257451 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00914: Removing server project usages in 3DEXPERIENCE platform

- page_id: `209257451`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/209257451/Removing+server+project+usages+in+3DEXPERIENCE+platform
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2025-02-04T14:54:10.584+01:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Collaboration powered by 3DEXPERIENCE platform > Managing project usages via 3DEXPERIENCE platform
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

If you no longer need to use a project, you can easily remove the usage from the main project. Project usages are removed with all references to the elements stored in the used project.

To remove a project usage

1. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Locking model for edit' space='MD2022x'] you want to stop using.
2. In the main menu, click Collaborate > Project Usages > Project Usages .
3. In the **Project Usages** dialog, do one of the following:
  - Select a used project, click [IMAGE alt='' src=''], and select **Remove with References**.
  - Right-click a used project and select Remove > Remove with References . [ATTACHMENT filename='removing_project_usages.png']
4. In the question dialog, click Yes to confirm that you want to remove the used project.
5. When the used project is removed, click OK to close the Project Usages dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>If you no longer need to use a project, you can easily remove the usage from the main project. Project usages are removed with <span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">all references to the elements stored in the used project.</span></p><p><br /></p><p>To remove a project usage</p><hr /><ol><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="Locking model for edit" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Lock the used project]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> you want to stop using.</li><li>In the main menu, click <strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Usages</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog, do one of the following:</p><ul><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Select a used project, click <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="17"><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_button.png"><ri:page ri:space-key="MD2022x" ri:content-title="_buttons" /></ri:attachment></ac:image>, and select <strong>Remove with References</strong>.</p></li><li>Right-click a used project and select <strong>Remove</strong> &gt; <strong>Remove with References</strong>.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="removing_project_usages.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul></li><li>In the question dialog, click <strong>Yes</strong> to confirm that you want to remove the used project.</li><li>When the used project is removed, click <strong>OK</strong> to close the <strong>Project Usages</strong> dialog.</li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140023 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00915: Removing unresolved reference

- page_id: `189140023`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140023/Removing+unresolved+reference
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.520+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305661011

305661013

305661012

At times, a reference to some recovered element is no longer needed and can be removed.

To remove a reference to the recovered element, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Clean Usage of Recovered Element .
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is URE. From its shortcut menu select Clean Usage of Recovered Element .

The reference to the particular recovered element is removed.

You can use the command for more than one element at a time.

If some of the referring elements are non-editable, belong to the read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked, the command will skip these elements.

You can remove all unresolved references (including all used modules) of a recovered element in one operation.

To remove all references to a recovered element, do one of the following

- In the Model Browser, right-click the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Clean Usage of Recovered Element .
- In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Clean Usage of Recovered Element .

All unresolved references to the particular recovered element are removed. As the unresolved references are cleared, the recovered element is removed as well.

Unresolved references will not be removed from the referring elements, if they are stored in a read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked.

305661010

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Replacing recovered element with another element' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing recovered element symbol' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space='']

## PAGE 00916: Renaming projects

- page_id: `189143516`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143516/Renaming+projects
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:15.075+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Managing server projects
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

To rename a project

1. From the main menu, select Collaborate > Projects . The Manage Projects dialog opens.
2. Select the project you want to rename.
3. Click Rename .
4. In the dialog that opens, type a new name for the project in the **Name:** field: [ATTACHMENT filename='renaming_project.png']

5. In this dialog, you can also change the project category by clicking the three dots menu [IMAGE alt='' src=''] and selecting the desired category.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>To rename a project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select<span> </span> <strong>Collaborate</strong> <span> </span>&gt;<span> </span> <strong>Projects</strong>. The<span> </span> <strong>Manage Projects</strong> <span> </span>dialog opens.</li><li>Select the project you want to rename.</li><li>Click <strong>Rename</strong>.</li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">In the dialog that opens, type a new name for the project in the <strong>Name:</strong> field:</p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="renaming_project.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p>       5. In this dialog, you can also change the project category by clicking the three dots menu <ac:image ac:thumbnail="true" ac:height="16"><ri:attachment ri:filename="3_dots.png" /></ac:image> and selecting the desired category.</p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142310 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00917: Reordering elements

- page_id: `189142310`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142310/Reordering+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:09.369+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can change the order of elements/properties presented in a list, tree, and Specification window, so if you need to move them, you can easily do so using one of the following methods:

To reorder elements in dialogs

1. Select one or more element(s) to move to the desired place.
2. Do one of the following:
  - Click the Up (to move up) or Down (to move down) buttons on the right side of the dialog
  - Press Ctrl + Up (to move up) or Ctrl + Down (to move down) on your keyboard [ATTACHMENT filename='customize diagram wizard dialog.png']
  - Drag-and-drop the selected element(s) [IMAGE alt='' src=''] If you need to move multiple elements, press Ctrl on your keyboard and click to select the elements to reorder.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>You can change the order of elements/properties presented in a list, tree, and Specification window, so if you need to move them, you can easily do so using one of the following methods:</p><p>To reorder elements in dialogs</p><hr /><ol><li>Select one or more element(s) to move to the desired place.</li><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>Click the <strong>Up </strong>(to move up) or<strong> Down</strong> (to move down) buttons on the right side of the dialog</li><li>Press Ctrl + Up (to move up) or Ctrl + Down (to move down) on your keyboard<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="customize diagram wizard dialog.png" /></ac:image></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target">Drag-and-drop the selected element(s) </p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="dragging_and_dropping_element_in_a_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="29652724-5bc7-4acd-add7-62624c32125c"><ac:rich-text-body><p>If you need to move multiple elements, press Ctrl on your keyboard and click to select the elements to reorder.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul></li></ol>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142017 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00918: Replacing elements

- page_id: `189142017`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142017/Replacing+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:47.320+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Refactoring
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.

###### Original element

The original element is the element that is going to be replaced. In other words, the replacement source.

###### Replacement target

The replacement target is the element with which the original element is going to be replaced.

###### Replaced element

The replaced element is the element to which the original element has been changed during the replacement.

###### Element properties

The element properties are so called the element specification properties (specified in the element Specification window) and the element symbol properties (defined in the element Symbol Properties dialog).

###### Inner elements

Inner elements are the elements stored inside the element (that is, owned by that element), for example, the class as inner package element.

You can replace one model element with another of the same type (metaclass) element. The model element replacement is useful when during the modeling process you notice that one model element needs to be replaced with another. All relations and references of the original element are transferred to the newly replaced element.

After the replacement, an original element will be replaced with a replacement target:

- The replacement target will be displayed in all diagrams instead of the original element.
- All references to the original element will be replaced by references to the replacement target.
- The replacement target after the replacement will have all the relationships of the replacement target and the original element.
- The original element will be deleted from the project.
- The original element properties and inner elements will be deleted from the project.

See the replacement example in the following figure.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

The preceding figure illustrates the replacement of the *User* class instead of the *Customer* class. After the replacement the *User* class has been removed and the *Customer* class has been created instead of it.

INLINE

To replace one element with another

1. Select a symbol on the diagram pane or an element in the Model Browser and open its shortcut menu.
2. Click Refactor > Replace With . The warning message appears:
  - Click Yes to continue the element replacement. The properties and inner elements of the original element will be lost.
  - Click No to cancel the element replacement. The selected element will not be replaced.
3. If you decided to continue the replacement, the element selection dialog opens.
4. In the element selection dialog, select the element with which you want to replace the original element.
5. Click **OK**. The original element is replaced with the target element.

#### PANEL: Related pages

Related pages

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Refactoring' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Working with model elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09ef1523-24fe-4c16-ac44-25f61f81558b"><ac:rich-text-body><p>This functionality is available in Standard, Professional, Architect, and Enterprise editions.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Original element</span></h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The original element is the element that is going to be replaced. In other words, the replacement source.</p><h4>Replacement target</h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The replacement target is the element with which the original element is going to be replaced.</p><h4>Replaced element</h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The replaced element is the element to which the original element has been changed during the replacement.</p><h4>Element properties</h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The element properties are so called the element specification properties (specified in the element Specification window) and the element symbol properties (defined in the element Symbol Properties dialog).</p><h4>Inner elements</h4><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Inner elements are the elements stored inside the element (that is, owned by that element), for example, the class as inner package element.</p><p>You can replace one model element with another of the same type (metaclass) element. The model element replacement is useful when during the modeling process you notice that one model element needs to be replaced with another. All relations and references of the original element are transferred to the newly replaced element.</p><p>After the replacement, an original element will be replaced with a replacement target:</p><ul><li>The replacement target will be displayed in all diagrams instead of the original element.</li><li>All references to the original element will be replaced by references to the replacement target.</li><li>The replacement target after the replacement will have all the relationships of the replacement target and the original element.</li><li>The original element will be deleted from the project.</li><li>The original element properties and inner elements will be deleted from the project.</li></ul><p>See the replacement example in the following figure.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Model before element replacement (at the top) and model after element replacement (at the bottom)" ac:alt="Model before element replacement (at the top) and model after element replacement (at the bottom)"><ri:attachment ri:filename="replace_with1.png" /></ac:image></p><p>The preceding figure illustrates the replacement of the <em>User</em> class instead of the <em>Customer</em> class. After the replacement the <em>User</em> class has been removed and the <em>Customer</em> class has been created instead of it.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="excerpt" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="821074d5-1fb4-480f-b806-99a36a4b8723"><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>To replace one element with another</p><hr /><ol><li>Select a symbol on the diagram pane or an element in the Model Browser and open its shortcut menu.</li><li>Click <strong>Refactor</strong> &gt; <strong>Replace With</strong>. The warning message appears:<ul><li>Click <strong>Yes</strong> to continue the element replacement. The properties and inner elements of the original element will be lost.</li><li>Click <strong>No</strong> to cancel the element replacement. The selected element will not be replaced.</li></ul></li><li>If you decided to continue the replacement, the element selection dialog opens.</li><li><p>In the element selection dialog, select the element with which you want to replace the original element.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>OK</strong>. The original element is replaced with the target element.</p></li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="panel" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bbd010ad-86a6-4404-ba30-364a13e40aa2"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Related pages</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Refactoring" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Working with model elements" /></ac:link> </li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140022 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00919: Replacing recovered element with another element

- page_id: `189140022`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140022/Replacing+recovered+element+with+another+element
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:41.470+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity > Managing unresolved references
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

305622476

305622479

305622477

At times, you may decide to reference a different element rather than the recovered one in the model.

To replace a reference from a recovered element to another one

1. Do either:
  - In the Model Browser, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element. From its shortcut menu select Validation > Usage of Recovered Element > Use Another Element .
  - In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click an element that refers to the recovered element and whose abbreviation is URE. From its shortcut menu select Use Another Element .
2. In the element Selection dialog, select the element you want to use instead of the recovered element.
3. Click OK . The reference is now pointing to the selected element. Also, it is no longer unresolved.

You can use the command for more than one element at a time.

If some of the unresolved references are read-only, belong to the read-only module, are locked by another user, or still not locked, the command will skip these elements.

Alternatively, you can replace all references from a particular recovered element to another one using one operation.

To replace a recovered element with another one in all references

1. Do either:
  - In the Model Browser, right-click the recovered element and from its shortcut menu select Validation > Recovered Element > Change Usages To .
  - In the Active Validation Results panel, right-click the recovered element and from its shortcut menu select Change Usages To .
2. In the element Selection dialog, select the element you want to use instead of the recovered element.
3. Click OK . All the references are now pointing to another element. Also, they are no longer decorated as unresolved.

305622474

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing unresolved reference' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Removing recovered element symbol' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing unresolved references' space='']

From its shortcut menu select <strong>Use Another Element</strong>.</li></ul></li><li>In the element Selection dialog, select the element you want to use instead of the recovered element.</li><li>Click <strong>OK</strong>. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144316 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00920: Report Data Management Pane

- page_id: `189144316`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144316/Report+Data+Management+Pane
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:44.626+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard dialog
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1277078685

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1277078691

1277078679

**On this page**

3

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1277078690

Report Wizard provides predefined report data templates that you can use to generate a report. The built-in report data template consists of several report data variables such as title, author, scope, and purpose that you can modify and adjust to meet your needs. You can also create a new report data template as needed, and manage the variables using the report data management pane.

This section defines report data, and how to use it in your report to represent variables that you want to include. Click the following links to navigate to the content of this page.

- 
- 
- 
- 

Reportdata

#### Report Data and Variables

A report in Report Wizard has a collection of variables which are called report data. Report Wizard provides a built-in report data for every predefined template. The built-in report data comes with predefined variables and values which you can edit or delete. You can also add more variables to the built-in report data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The built-in report data's predefined variables and values in Report Wizard.

You can create your own report data, and add variables and child variables under it if you do not want to use the built-in one. Report Wizard also allows you to create a report data as an element in the Containment tree inside a project by using the Report Profile which allows you to commit the Report Data to Teamwork Cloud, and share it with other users.

Reportdatapane

#### Report Data Management Pane

Report Wizard stores the built-in report data in the **Report Data**management pane which helps you to organize information into groups, and keep revision history created in a project.

This pane also allows you to:

- Create, edit, delete, or clone a Report Data.
- Create or organize the variables in a Report Data.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting the built-in report data in the Report Data management pane.

The following table lists the buttons and their function in the **Report Data**management pane.

| Button | Function |
| --- | --- |
|  | Creates report data. |
|  | Edits report data. |
|  | Deletes report data. |
|  | Clones report data. |
|  | Creates a report variable. |

WorkingwithReportdata

#### Working with Report Data

You can create report data either in:

- Report Wizard in which all data will be saved into an XML file.
- A modeling tool project, in which case the report data will be saved with the project itself. Saving report data in a project will enable you to commit the report to Teamwork Cloud.

The following instructions show you how to create and manage your report data in Report Wizard and in a modeling tool project.

To create report data in Report Wizard

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **New****Report Data** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a Report Data in the New Report Data dialog.
4. Enter a new report name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] .

Before creating report data in a project, you need to use a report profile,*Report Profile.mdzip*, which is located in the*<install.root>\profiles*folder.

To use a report profile (Report Profile.mdzip)

1. On the main menu, click **File******> **Use Project** > **Use Local Project**. The **Use Project** dialog will open, and the project module path *<install.root>\profiles* will be selected by default. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Use Local Project menu opens the Use Project dialog that contains a report profile you need before creating a report data in a project. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Selecting a project module path for thereport profile and Report Profile.mdzip in the Use Project dialog.
2. Select *Report Profile*and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Specify the usage options. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Usage Options.
4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The “Report Profile” profile will open in the Containment tree as a read-only profile. You can now use it in your project.

create new report data in MD project

To create a Report Data in a project

1. Use *Report Profile.mdzip* in your project.
2. Right-click a data model in the Containment tree and select **Create Element**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating Report Data in the project
3. Select **Report Data** from the menu.
4. Type the name of the Report Data element in the Containment tree.

You can right-click a data model, a package, or a profile in the Containment tree to create a Report Data.

| Tag Values | Function |
| --- | --- |
| autoImageSize | Change the image size. |
| imageFormat | Select an image format, either JPEG or PNG. |
| emptyText | Store a string value that will be replaced with an empty variable. |
| data | Contain elements that will be published by Report Wizard. |
| template | Determine which template to use in a particular Report Data. |
| generateRecursively | Determine whether or not a report will be generated recursively. |

To edit report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The**Edit Report Data** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a report data in the Edit Report Data dialog
4. Edit the report’s name and description, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].

To delete report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Confirm delete** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] The Confirm delete dialog.
4. Click either [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] to delete the selected data or [ATTACHMENT filename='No_button.png'] to cancel the operation.

To clone report data

1. Click **Tools** > **Report Wizard**.
2. Select a template and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''].
3. Click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Clone Report** dialog will appear. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Cloning a report data in the Clone Report dialog.
4. Enter the name and description of the report. The name should begin with **Copy of** (cloned report name).
5. Click[IMAGE alt='' src='']. The cloned Report Data will then be created.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Copy of My Report created in the Report Data Management pane.

When a cloned Report Data is created, a copy of the Report Data (variable) will also be created.

WorkingwithReportvariables

#### Working with Report Data Variables

Variables are created because some information is not “naturally” contained in a model, e.g. the company's name, author's name, revisions. Although you can put all of the information in a model's specification (Document/Hyperlinks in the **Specification** dialog), it is difficult and tedious to get data from the model's specification, as this results in more lines of codes and scripts in the report templates. Therefore, instead of writing lines of codes, you can simply create a variable, give it a name, and call it directly from the template. For example, you can create a variable called “Author” in Report Wizard and write **$Author** in the template. When you generate the report, you will see the value inside “Author.” Essentially, a variable is used to represent data that you want to include in the report, not in the model.

Variables contain information that you want to store in a project, such as names and dates. You can create a report variable in a project or the Report Wizard dialog.

To create a report variable in a project

1. Right-click a **Report Data** in the Containment tree and select **Create Element** > **Variable**. The variable will appear inside the **Report Data**. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] Creating a variable for Report Data in the Containment tree.

You can also create, edit, and delete variables through the **Report Variable** dialog in Report Wizard.

To create a report variable in the Report Wizard dialog

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='New_button.png'] . The New Variable dialog will open. Enter the variable name and description. You can create either a parent variable or a child variable in the Owner box.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.

To create a parent variable, type the variable name in the **Name** box, enter the description, select an empty value in the **Owner** box, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Creating MyNewVariableName as a parent variable.

To create a child variable, type the variable name in the **Name** box, enter the description, select**Parent** in the **Owner** box, and then click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Creating a child variable of MyNewVariableName.

3. The new variable will appear in the table in the **Report Variable** dialog.

4. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to save the variables in the Report Data.

To edit a report variable

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Double-click on the column next to the variable name column and click [IMAGE alt='' src='']. A dialog will open in which you can edit the variable value. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Editing a variable in the Template Variable dialog
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='OK_button.png'] . The new variable value will appear in the column next to the variable name column.

To delete a report variable

1. Open the Report Variable dialog.
2. Click a variable in the table, and then click **Delete**. A dialog will open prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Dialog to confirm the deletion of a report variable
3. Click [ATTACHMENT filename='Yes_button.png'] and the variable will be deleted.

To get the value of a top-level variable

1. Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.
2. Type: **$Parent**. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Referencing to a parent variable in the template.
3. Save the file as “*sampletemplate.rtf*”. Choose Rich Text Format (**.rtf*) as the file type.
4. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new template. The **New** **Template** dialog will open.
5. Type the name and description of the new template. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select “*sampletemplate.rtf*” as the template file.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] >**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**. The**Select Report Data** pane will open.
7. Select a report data, in this example, **My Report Data** and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure. 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### NOTE: Note

Note

When you include the variables you have created in a template, each variable value will be included in the generated report. This section will use the following **My Report Data** as an example.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### My Report Data as an example.

To get the value of the child of a variable

1. Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.
2. Type any of the following to print a child variable. (i) **$Parent.get(1)**to get a child value by index. (ii) **$Parent.get(“Child”)** to get a child value by name comparison. (iii) **$Parent.Child**to get a child value by referencing its name, in this case “Child”. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] Referencing to the child variable in the template
3. Save the file as “*sampletemplate.rtf*”. Choose **Rich Text Format** (**.rtf*) as the file type.
4. Open the **Report Wizard** dialog and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to create a new template. The **New Template** dialog will open.
5. Type the name and description of the new template. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to select “*sampletemplate.rtf*” as the template file.
6. Click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] > [IMAGE alt='' src='']. The **Select Report Data** pane will open.
7. Select a report data, in this example, **My Report Data** and click [IMAGE alt='' src=''] to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The resulting RTF file.

#### Glossary

- Clone To clone is to create an exact copy of report data.

- RTF Rich Text Format (RTF) is a file format developed by Microsoft that can be used with different word processors in different operating systems.

#### More information about working with reports in Report Wizard

To learn more about reports in Report Wizard, you can visit the following pages:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating Reports from Report Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the Containment tree' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generating reports from the command line' space='']

#### Sample model

[ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip']250

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c4db7214-d3dc-499d-90ea-77da7bd57240"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078685</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="60f93fb5-fb8b-43cd-91b4-80a0bf5b2a74"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078691</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a25830c1-cfcf-4066-856c-083cc4ce0976"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078679</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d916590-ea04-46c4-86b6-fee4fa116189"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="21d8533e-1f56-4f37-99f9-489fa8427806"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1277078690</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>Report Wizard provides predefined report data templates that you can use to generate a report. You can also add more variables to the built-in report data.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="BuiltinVariables.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The built-in report data's predefined variables and values in Report Wizard.</h6><p>You can create your own report data, and add variables and child variables under it if you do not want to use the built-in one. and share it with other users.</p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eac4b8e3-d7df-4514-80f6-25bf3295f39a"><ac:parameter Data.</li><li>Create or organize the variables in a Report Data.</li></ul><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Built-in Report Data.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 Data</h2><p>You can create report data either in:</p><ul><li>Report Wizard in which all data will be saved into an XML file.</li><li>A modeling tool project, in which case the report data will be saved with the project itself. Saving report data in a project will enable you to commit the report to Teamwork Cloud.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>The following instructions show you how to create and manage your report data in Report Wizard and in a modeling tool project.</p><p>To create report data in Report Wizard</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>New </strong><strong>Report Data</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="New Report Data Dialog" ac:alt="New Report Data Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-report-data-a.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Creating a Report Data in the New Report Data dialog.</h6></li><li>Enter a new report name and description, and then click<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image></strong>.</li></ol><p>Before <span>creating report data in a project, you <span>need to use a report profile, </span><em>Report Profile.mdzip</em><span>, which is located in the </span><em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</em><span> folder. </span></span></p><p>To use a report profile (Report Profile.mdzip)</p><hr /><ol><li><p>On the main menu, click <strong>File</strong><strong> </strong>&gt; <strong>Use Project</strong> &gt; <strong>Use Local Project</strong>. The <strong>Use Project</strong> dialog will open, and the project module path <em>&lt;install.root&gt;\profiles</em> will be selected by default.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UseLocalProject_menu.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>The Use Local Project menu opens the Use Project dialog that contains a report profile you need before creating a report data in a project.</h6><h6><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_select.png" /></ac:image><br />Selecting a project module path for the<span> report profile and Report Profile.mdzip</span> in the Use Project dialog.</h6></li><li><p>Select <em>Report Profile </em>and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Specify the usage options.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="UsageOptions_select.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Usage Options.</h6></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Finish_button.png" /></ac:image>. The “Report Profile” profile will open in the Containment tree as a read-only profile. You can now use it in your project.</p></li></ol><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="140d6f37-2e15-4467-9f5d-5bed364565bf"><ac:parameter ac:name="">create new report data in MD project</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><p>To create a Report Data in a project</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Use <em>Report Profile.mdzip</em> in your project.</p></li><li><p>Right-click a data model in the Containment tree and select <strong>Create Element</strong>.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_ReportData.png" /></ac:image></p><h6><span>Creating Report Data in the project</span></h6></li><li><p>Select <strong>Report Data</strong> from the menu.</p></li><li>Type the name of the <strong>Report Data</strong> element in the Containment tree.<br /> </li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03eb30b1-3dc1-478d-b1fe-919fdfbbc25f"><ac:rich-text-body><p>You can right-click a data model, a package, or a profile in the Containment tree to create a Report Data.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="wrapped" style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Tag Values</th><th>Function</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>autoImageSize</td><td>Change the image size.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>imageFormat</td><td>Select an image format, either JPEG or PNG.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>emptyText</td><td>Store a string value that will be replaced with an empty variable.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>data</td><td><p>Contain elements that will be published by Report Wizard.</p></td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1">template</td><td colspan="1">Determine which template to use in a particular Report Data.</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td colspan="1">generateRecursively</td><td colspan="1">Determine whether or not a report will be generated recursively.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">To edit report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Edit_button.png" /></ac:image>. The<strong> Edit Report Data</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Edit Report Data Dialog" ac:alt="Edit Report Data Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="edit-report-data-a.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Editing a report data in the Edit Report Data dialog</h6></li><li><p>Edit the report’s name and description, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Save_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li></ol><p class="auto-cursor-target">To delete report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Delete_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Confirm delete</strong> dialog will appear.    </p><p><ac:image ac:title="Confirm Delete Dialog" ac:alt="Confirm Delete Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="confirm-delete-report-data.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>The Confirm delete dialog.</h6></li><li>Click either <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png" /></ac:image> to delete the selected data or <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="No_button.png" /></ac:image> to cancel the operation.</li></ol><p>To clone report data</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>.</p></li><li><p>Select a template and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Clone Report</strong> dialog will appear.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Clone Report Dialog" ac:alt="Clone Report Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="clone-report-b.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Cloning a report data in the Clone Report dialog.</h6></li><li><p>Enter the name and description of the report. The name should begin with <strong>Copy of</strong> (cloned report name).</p></li><li><p><span>Click </span><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image><span>. The cloned Report Data will then be created.</span></p></li></ol><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Dialog Copy of My Report.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The Copy of My Report created in the Report Data Management pane.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8ccbe5a1-e65d-4424-8267-cab5e66c46a8"><ac:rich-text-body><p>When a cloned Report Data is created, a copy of the Report Data (variable) will also be created.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38ad4cab-6003-4d7d-9f6e-d9583c22eb71"><ac:parameter ac:name="">WorkingwithReportvariables</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><h2>Working with Report Data Variables</h2><p>Variables are created because some information is not “naturally” contained in a model, e.g. the company's name, author's name, revisions. Although you can put all of the information in a model's specification (Document/Hyperlinks in the <strong>Specification</strong> dialog), it is difficult and tedious to get data from the model's specification, as this results in more lines of codes and scripts in the report templates. Therefore, instead of writing lines of codes, you can simply create a variable, give it a name, and call it directly from the template. For example, you can create a variable called “Author” in Report Wizard and write <strong>$Author</strong> in the template. When you generate the report, you will see the value inside “Author.” Essentially, a variable is used to represent data that you want to include in the report, not in the model.</p><p>Variables contain information that you want to store in a project, such as names and dates. You can create a report variable in a project or the Report Wizard dialog.</p><p>To create a report variable in a project</p><hr /><ol><li><p>Right-click a <strong>Report Data</strong> in the Containment tree and select <strong>Create Element</strong> &gt; <strong>Variable</strong>. The variable will appear inside the <strong>Report Data</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportProfile_Variable.png" /></ac:image></p><h6><span>Creating a variable for Report Data in the Containment tree.</span></h6></li></ol><p>You can also create, edit, and delete variables through the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog in Report Wizard.</p><p>To create a report variable in the Report Wizard dialog</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>New</strong> <strong>Variable</strong> dialog will open. Enter the variable name and description. You can create either a parent variable or a child variable in the <strong>Owner</strong> box.</li></ol><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="New variable dialog" ac:alt="New variable dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-b.png" /></ac:image><br />Creating a new variable in the New Variable dialog.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To create a parent variable, type the variable name in the <strong>Name</strong> box, enter the description, select an empty value in the <strong>Owner</strong> box, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Creating a Parent Variable" ac:alt="Creating a Parent Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-c.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Creating MyNewVariableName as a parent variable.</h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To create a child variable, type the variable name in the <strong>Name</strong> box, enter the description, select<strong> Parent</strong> in the <strong>Owner</strong> box, and then click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image>.<br /><ac:image ac:title="Creating a Child Variable" ac:alt="Creating a Child Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-d.png" /></ac:image><br />Creating a child variable of MyNewVariableName.</p><p>     3.  The new variable will appear in the table in the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</p><p>     4.  Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image> to save the variables in the Report Data.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target">To edit a report variable</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li><p>Double-click on the column next to the variable name column and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="browse_button.png" /></ac:image>. A dialog will open in which you can edit the variable value.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Editing Variables" ac:alt="Editing Variables"><ri:attachment ri:filename="new-variable-e.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Editing a variable in the Template Variable dialog</h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="OK_button.png" /></ac:image>. The new variable value will appear in the column next to the variable name column.</li></ol><p>To delete a report variable</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Report Variable</strong> dialog.</li><li><p>Click a variable in the table, and then click <strong>Delete</strong>. A dialog will open prompting you to confirm before deleting the selected variable.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Deleting Variable" ac:alt="Deleting Variable"><ri:attachment ri:filename="delete-variable-b.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Dialog to confirm the deletion of a report variable</h6></li><li>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Yes_button.png" /></ac:image> and the variable will be deleted.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To get the value of a top-level variable<br /></span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.</li><li><p>Type: <strong>$Parent</strong>.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Referencing to Parent Variable in the Template" ac:alt="Referencing to Parent Variable in the Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MSWORD.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Referencing to a parent variable in the template.</h6></li><li><p>Save the file as “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>”. Choose Rich Text Format (<em>*.rtf</em>) as the file type.</p></li><li><p>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image> to create a new template. The <strong>New</strong> <strong>Template</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Type the name and description of the new template. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png" /></ac:image> to select “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>” as the template file.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image> &gt;<strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image></strong>. The<strong> Select Report Data</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Select a report data, in this example, <strong>My Report Data</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png" /></ac:image> to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="parent_result.png" /></ac:image></p></li></ol><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b50bd350-075c-47d5-b0ac-c193e56e5d64"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When you include the variables you have created in a template, each variable value will be included in the generated report. This section will use the following <strong>My Report Data</strong> as an example.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:title="Sample of Report Data" ac:alt="Sample of Report Data"><ri:attachment ri:filename="sample of report data.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">My Report Data as an example.</h6></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">To get the value of the child of a variable</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Open a blank document in Microsoft Word.</li><li><p>Type any of the following to print a child variable.</p><p>(i) <strong>$Parent.get(1) </strong>to get a child value by index.</p><p>(ii) <strong>$Parent.get(“Child”) </strong> to get a child value by name comparison.</p><p>(iii) <strong>$Parent.Child </strong>to get a child value by referencing its name, in this case “Child”.</p><p><ac:image ac:title="Referencing to Child Variable in the Template" ac:alt="Referencing to Child Variable in the Template"><ri:attachment ri:filename="MSWORD2.png" /></ac:image></p><h6>Referencing to the child variable in the template</h6></li><li><p>Save the file as “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>”. Choose <strong>Rich Text Format</strong> (<em>*.rtf</em>) as the file type.</p></li><li><p>Open the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="New_button.png" /></ac:image> to create a new template. The <strong>New Template</strong> dialog will open.</p></li><li><p>Type the name and description of the new template. Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Browse_bigbutton.png" /></ac:image> to select “<em>sampletemplate.rtf</em>” as the template file.</p></li><li><p>Click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Create_button.png" /></ac:image> &gt; <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Next_button.png" /></ac:image>. The <strong>Select Report Data</strong> pane will open.</p></li><li><p>Select a report data, in this example, <strong>My Report Data</strong> and click <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Generate_button.png" /></ac:image> to generate a report. The output of the generated report will be shown as in the following figure.<br /><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="child_result.png" /></ac:image><span style="color: rgb(61,61,61);"><br /></span></p></li></ol><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span>The resulting RTF file.</span></h6><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong>Clone</strong><br />To clone is to create an exact copy of report data.</li></ul><ul><li class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>RTF</strong><br />Rich Text Format (RTF) is a file format developed by Microsoft that can be used with different word processors in different operating systems.</li></ul><h2>More information about working with reports in Report Wizard</h2><p class="confluence-link">To learn more about reports in Report Wizard, you can visit the following pages:</p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating Reports from Report Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating reports from the Containment tree" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating Reports from the Containment Tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Generating reports from the command line" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Generating Reports from the Command Line]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><h2>Sample model</h2><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="view-file" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f35fc673-0b17-4051-9a7d-cf664c12b0b3"><ac:parameter ac:name="name"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip" /></ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="height">250</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p><hr /></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144397 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00921: Report Designer

- page_id: `189144397`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144397/Report+Designer
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:45.595+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1769283899

1769283902

1769283901

Report Designer is a tool for conveniently drafting your template in Microsoft Word Document (DOCX) and Microsoft Excel Spreadsheet (XLSX) formats. Without requiring knowledge in UML and expertise in Velocity template creators, this tool provides you with an easy way to access elements and properties in a few clicks.

The Report Designer diagram is for starting to create and design your report template. This diagram helps you draft the template structure or pseudocode. When you drag template diagram elements to the diagram, Report Designer will allow you to select available properties of the particular elements, view the appearance of your template, generate the drafted template that contains skeleton code, add more detail of code logic, and arrange text style and layout to the drafted template later to complete creating the template.

982760273

**Related pages**

Without requiring knowledge in UML and expertise in Velocity template creators, this tool provides you with an easy way to access elements and properties in a few clicks.</p><p>The Report Designer diagram is for starting to create and design your report template. This diagram helps you draft the template structure or pseudocode. When you drag template diagram elements to the diagram, Report Designer will allow you to select available properties of the particular elements, view the appearance of your template, generate the drafted template that contains skeleton code, add more detail of code logic, and arrange text style and layout to the drafted template later to complete creating the template.</p><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c909faa3-1543-4f51-9ae0-4ac92c125ded"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">982760273</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="4e88b85d-c02a-425c-a379-205e7a4d36b5" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
