<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141697 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00922: Report Template

- page_id: `189141697`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141697/Report+Template
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:24.893+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

846411724

846411726

846411725

This is a custom element used to store a report template file (*.mrzip*) attached to the project.

In contrast to the report templates stored in the local report templates folder (*<modeling tool [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='']>\data\reports*), a report template attached to the project as this specific type of model element is not lost when you open the project on another computer. It is safely transferred within the project (either local or server).

To attach a report template to the project

1. From the main menu, select Tools > Report Wizard . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard dialog' space='']**Report Wizard** dialog opens.
2. Select the report template you want to attach to the project and click the Attach button. The selected report template is duplicated, and the duplicate is attached to the open project. It is visible in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment tab' space=''] directly under the root package. As the following figure shows, in the Report Wizard dialog it as marked with the “[Attached to Project]” tag.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Attached to a project report template in the Report Wizard dialog.

You can generate a report from the attached report template in one of the following ways:

- Right-click the attached report template in the Containment tree. From the shortcut menu select Generate Report . The Report Wizard dialog opens with the attached report template already selected. Proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.
- Right-click the namespace (for example, a Package) in the Containment tree. From the shortcut menu select Generate Report . Then select the appropriate report templates category and click the attached report template.

The *“[Attached]”* tag helps to identify attached to project report templates on this menu.

Proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.

- From the main menu, select Tools > Report Wizard . In the Report Wizard dialog, select the report template and proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Selecting attached to project report template for report generation.

If you no longer need the attached report template, you can simply delete it from the model. Just select it in the Containment tree and press **Delete**.

If you need to modify the attached report template, do the following:

- 
  - Extract it to the local report templates folder.
  - Open the extracted template for edit and make changes in it.
  - After you finish editing, reattach the updated report template to your project.

To extract a report template to the local report templates folder

- 
  - From the main menu, select Tools > Report Wizard. The Report Wizard dialog opens.
  - Select the report template you want to extract to the local report templates folder and click the Extract button.
  - If a message asking to confirm the update of already existing report template appears, click Yes.

The report template appears in the local report templates folder and can be opened for edit. It also remains attached to the open project.

846411723

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Containment Tree' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='UML elements' space='']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="83a38efa-6e8b-4fa0-bc79-0da4fa7b2513"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">846411724</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a8d51e6-30db-4da8-9796-1660affa4f6f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">846411726</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d087491a-9b7c-4add-9e65-7aff201de547"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">846411725</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="single"><ac:layout-cell><p>This is a custom element used to store a report template file (<em>.mrzip</em>) attached to the project.</p><p><span>In contrast to the report templates stored in the local report templates folder (</span><em>&lt;modeling tool <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuration files" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[configuration files directory]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>&gt;\data\reports</em><span>), a report template attached to the project as this specific type of model element is not lost when you open the project on another computer. It is safely transferred within the project (either local or server). </span></p><p><br /></p><p>To attach a report template to the project</p><hr /><ol><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link> opens.</li><li>Select the report template you want to attach to the project and click the <strong>Attach</strong> button. The selected report template is duplicated, and the duplicate is attached to the open project. It is visible in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment tab" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Containment tree]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> directly under the root package. As the following figure shows, in the <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog it as marked with the <em>“[Attached to Project]”</em> tag.</li></ol><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_template.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Attached to a project report template in the Report Wizard dialog.</h6><p>You can generate a report from the attached report template in one of the following ways:</p><ul><li>Right-click the attached report template in the Containment tree. From the shortcut menu select <strong>Generate Report</strong>. The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog opens with the attached report template already selected. Proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.</li><li class="auto-cursor-target"><span>Right-click the namespace (for example, a Package) in the Containment tree. From the shortcut menu select </span><strong>Generate Report</strong><span>. Then select the appropriate report templates category and click the attached report template.</span></li></ul><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="79625bc9-2136-4f84-b7fd-680a06bc2552"><ac:rich-text-body><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The <em>“[Attached]”</em> tag helps to identify attached to project report templates on this menu.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target" style="margin-left: 30.0px;">Proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard.</p><ul><li><span>From the main menu, select </span><strong>Tools </strong><span>&gt; </span><strong>Report Wizard</strong><span>. In the </span><strong>Report Wizard </strong><span>dialog, select the report template and proceed to the subsequent steps of the wizard. </span></li></ul><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="generating_report.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;text-align: center;"><span>Selecting attached to project report template for report generation.</span></h6><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span>If you no longer need the attached report template, you can simply delete it from the model. Just select it in the Containment tree and press <strong>Delete</strong>. </span></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">If you need to modify the attached report template, do the following:</p><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>Extract it to the local report templates folder.</li><li>Open the extracted template for edit and make changes in it.</li><li>After you finish editing, reattach the updated report template to your project.</li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">To extract a report template to the local report templates folder</p><hr /><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>From the main menu, select <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Report Wizard.</strong> The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the report template you want to extract to the local report templates folder and click the <strong>Extract</strong> button.</li><li>If a message asking to confirm the update of already existing report template appears, click <strong>Yes.</strong></li></ul></li></ul><p><br /></p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;">The report template appears in the local report templates folder and can be opened for edit. It also remains attached to the open project. </p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><span><br /></span></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p><br /></p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cedf4174-fb8b-4171-8c27-e9a02eef671e"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">846411723</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Containment Tree" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="UML elements" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Model Elements]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

**On this page**

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Sample 3: $report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional(element, includePureOwned) : List<Element>

1210344294

$report is a utility tool enabling a template to get the modeling tool data.

##### **$report.createValueSpecificationText(specification)**

Create texts representing the ValueSpecification element.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | specification | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.ValueSpecification | A given Value Specification. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A String value for ValueSpecification. |

****

##### **$report.filterDiagram(diagramList, diagramTypes)**

Return a collection of diagrams from a list of diagrams filtered by types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | diagramList | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagrams. |
| diagramTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of diagram types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered diagrams. |

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.filterElement(elementList, humanTypes)**

Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by human types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| humanTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of human types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

You can change the human type depending on the language used in the modeling tool. For example, "Class" is the human type of the classes in the English modeling tool while "Classe" is the human type for the classes in French. The specified humanTypes needs to be changed when you change the language used in the modeling tool.

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.filterElementType(elementList, elementType)**

Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by element types.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| elementTypes | java.util.Collection | A collection of element types used as filters. |  |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

Return a collection of elements filtered by a specified property name.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | elementList | java.util.Collection | A collection of elements. |
| propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |  |
| propertyValue | java.util.Collection | A collection of property names used as filters. |  |
| Return |  | java.util.Collection | A collection of filtered elements. |

For example:

```text

```

##### **$report.getElementProperties(element)**

Return a list of property names, including UML properties and properties that are customized through DSL customization.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of property names. |

For example:

```text
$pp.value
#end]]>
```

By default, all properties are exported. They can be filtered by adding a "STANDARD" or "EXPERT" argument, e.g.*$report.getElementProperties($element, "STANDARD").*

Return a list of property names, including UML properties and properties that are customized through DSL customization.

| Return | - | java.util.Collection | A collection of property names. |

Return a DSL property.

| propertyName | java.lang.String | A property name. |  |

##### **$report.getElementComment(element)**

Return a comment attached to an element. If no comment is attached, it will return a null value.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element | An element. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Comment | A comment instance of the element. |

****

##### **$report.getElementName(element)**

Get an element’s name. If the name is empty, it will return "<unnamed>". This method performs the following procedures:

- If the element is generalized from the NamedElement metaclass, it will return $element.name
- If the element is generalized from the Slot metaclass, it will return $element.definingFeature.name
- If the element is a UML element, it will return $element.humanName
- Else returns $element.toString()

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | java.lang.Object | An element. |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | An element name. |

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(element)**

Return an image icon of an element with a default icon filename (“icon_”+element type).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling toolelement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object of the element's icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.

****

##### $report.getImageFor(element, dpi)

Return an image with specified DPI of an element.

| Name | Type | Description | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling toolelement. |
| dpi | int | DPI of the element image. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object with specified DPI. |

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- The DPI value can be 1 to 4800.
- If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI set in *Template* and *Global* will be used respectively. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Setting image DPI' space=''] .
- If more than 4800, the value will be rounded down to 4800 .

##### **$report.getIconFor(element, prefix, suffix, hashCode)**

Return an image icon of an element. The icon name depends on the ***hashCode*** value if ***hashCode*** equals true, the icon’s name is set as***”icon_”+hash*** code value of the icon. If the ***hashCode***value equals false, the icon name is set as

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | element | com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement | A modeling tool element. |
| prefix | java.lang.String | A prefix of an icon name. |  |
| suffix | java.lang.String | A suffix of an icon name. |  |
| hashCode | boolean | If true, use hash code of an icon as the icon name and exclude a prefix or suffix from the name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object of an element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.

****

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(type)**

Return an image icon for an element type with a default icon’s filename ((“icon_”+element type).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A type name. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.

****

****

##### **$report.getIconFor(type, prefix, suffix, hashCode)**

Return an image icon for an element type. The icon’s name depends on the ***hashCode*** value if ***hashCode*** equals true, the icon name is set as ***“icon_”+hash*** code value of the icon. If the ***hashCode*** value equals false, the icon name is set as ***prefix + element type + suffix***.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | type | java.lang.String | A type name. |
| prefix | java.lang.String | A prefix for an icon name. |  |
| suffix | java.lang.String | A suffix for an icon name. |  |
| hashCode | boolean | If true, use hash code ofan icon as the icon’s name and exclude a prefix or suffix from the name. |  |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for an element’s icon. |

For example:

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.

```text

```

This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.

****

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | diagram | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram | A diagram. |

##### **$report.getQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)**

Get a qualified name. A qualified name is the name that allows a ***NamedElement***to be identified within a hierarchy of nested ***Namespaces***. It is constructed from the names of the containing namespaces starting at the root of the hierarchy and ending with the name of the ***NamedElement***itself.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | namedElement | NamedElement | A NamedElement. |
| separator | java.lang.String | A separator symbol. If the value is null or an empty string, the '::' will be used. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A qualified name. |

****

##### **$report.getPackageQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)**

Get a qualified name by considering only **Packages** and a given element. **Models** and **Profiles** will not be included in the qualified name.

For example, given the element hierarchy:

```text
com : Package -> nomagic : Package -> ui -> Package -> BaseDialog : Class]]>
```

```text

```

If $class is a “BaseDialog” element, the result from the above template code will be as follows.

```text

```

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | namedElement | NamedElement | A NamedElement. |
| separator | java.lang.String | A separator symbol. If the value is null or an empty string, the '::' will be used. |  |
| Return | - | java.lang.String | A qualified name. |

****

##### **$report.getBasicFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List<FlowStep>**

Find and return a list of basic flows from a given use case. If a use case scenario is not being used, it will retrieve the value from "requirementUseCase" stereotype tags.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | usecase | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.UseCase | A targeted Use Case. |
| Return | - | List<com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep> | A list of flow steps. A Flow Step is an object representing each step of a Use Case flow. |

The <FlowStep> is a "com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep" class.

The important properties of <FlowStep> are as follows:

- name prints a name of a current step, for example: $flowStep.name.
- element returns an element associates with a current step, for example: #set ($e = $flowStep.element).
- alternativeConditions returns a list of alternative steps <AlternativeCondition> from a current step, for example: #foreach ($alter in $flowStep.alternativeConditions) #end.
- exceptionTypes returns a list of exception steps <ExceptionType> from a current step, for example: #foreach ($alter in $flowStep.exceptionTypes) #end.

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.getAlternativeFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List<FlowStep>**

Find and return all alternative flows from a given use case. The alternative flows will be listed from all possible branches of basic flows. If a use case scenario is not being used, it will retrieve the value from "requirementUseCase" stereotype tags.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | usecase | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.UseCase | A targeted Use Case. |
| Return | - | List<com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep> | A list of flow steps. A Flow Step is an object representing each step of a Use Case flow. |

For example:

```text

```

****

##### **$report.getExceptionalFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List<FlowStep>**

Find and return all exceptional flows from a given use case. The alternative flows will be listed from all possible branches of basic flows. If the use case scenario is not being used, it will retrieve the value from "requirementUseCase" stereotype tags.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | usecase | com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.UseCase | A targeted Use Case. |
| Return | - | List<com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep> | A list of flow steps. A flow step is an object representing each steps of a Use Case flow. |

For example:

```text

```

****

ac:macro-id="b1ee1faa-fec1-4ec2-b49b-df74045a43df"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1210344294</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>$report is a utility tool enabling a template to get the modeling tool data.</p><h3><strong>$report.createValueSpecificationText(specification)</strong></h3><p>Create texts representing the ValueSpecification element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>specification</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.ValueSpecification</td><td>A given Value <br />Specification.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A String value <br />for ValueSpecification.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.filterDiagram(diagramList, diagramTypes)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of diagrams from a list of diagrams filtered by types.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>diagramList</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of diagrams.</td></tr><tr><td>diagramTypes</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of diagram types <br />used as filters.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of filtered diagrams.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dc7cd3c6-1a04-42d8-aa7a-4fcd7300c684"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.filterDiagram(diagramList, diagramTypes)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $report.filterDiagram($Diagram,["Class Diagram","Communication Diagram"]))
$diagram.name : $diagram.diagramType
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.filterElement(elementList, humanTypes)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by human types.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>elementList </td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of elements.</td></tr><tr><td>humanTypes</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of human types <br />used as filters.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of filtered elements.</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06dabf73-ac30-4ea4-8726-2e02e1593514"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body>You can change the human type depending on the language used in the modeling tool. For example, &quot;Class&quot; is the human type of the classes in the English modeling tool while &quot;Classe&quot; is the human type for the classes in French. The specified humanTypes needs to be changed when you change the language used in the modeling tool.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="756985fd-adb2-42b1-b795-8f65c051634c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.filterElement(elementList, humanTypes)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $report.filterElement($elements, ["Use Case", "Actor"]))
$element.name : $element.humanType
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.filterElementType(elementList, elementType)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by element types.</p><p><br /></p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>elementList </td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of elements.</td></tr><tr><td>elementTypes</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of element types <br />used as filters.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of filtered elements.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.filter(elementList, propertyName, propertyValue)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of elements filtered by a specified property name.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 119.0px;" /><col style="width: 113.0px;" /><col style="width: 139.0px;" /><col style="width: 217.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name  </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td>elementList</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of elements. </td></tr><tr><td>propertyName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td><p>A property name.</p></td></tr><tr><td>propertyValue </td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of property names <br />used as filters.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of filtered elements.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example: </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dfed2cad-bbdf-451d-952a-eed83c2be494"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.filter(elementList, propertyName, propertyValue)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($e in $report.filter($elements, "name", ["foo", "bar"]))
   $e.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.filterElementByStereotypeName(elementList, appliedStereotypeNameList)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of elements from a list of elements filtered by applied stereotype names.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong> </strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></p></td><td><p>elementList</p></td><td><p>java.util.Collection</p></td><td><p>A collection of elements.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>appliedStereotypeNameList</p></td><td><p>java.util.Collection</p></td><td><p>A collection of applied stereotype names.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Return</strong></p></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><p>java.util.Collection</p></td><td><p>A collection of filtered elements.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="857cc3d1-3cdc-4478-9ac4-0ffce9ec3c15"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.filterElementByStereotypeName(elementList, appliedStereotypeNameList)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $report.filterElementByStereotypeName($elements, ["Stereotype A", "Stereotype B"]))
   $element.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.findElementInCollection(elementList, name)</strong></h3><p>Find an element from a collection of elements by name.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>elementList</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of elements.</td></tr><tr><td>name</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An element name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element instance. <br />If it cannot find the <br />element, it will return <br />a null value.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="dbb0f9ab-cbf0-4ce7-8bfd-3b29f6ad4c74"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($myClass = $report.findElementInCollection($Class, "MyClass"))
$myClass.humanType $myClass.name]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.findRelationship(modelPackage)</strong></h3><p>Search and return a collection of relationship elements inside a package.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>modelPackage</td><td><span>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package</span></td><td>A package <br />element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />relationships in <br />a package.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.findRelationship(modelPackage, recursive)</strong></h3><p>Search and return a collection of relationship elements inside a package.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>modelPackage</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Package</td><td>A package <br />element. </td></tr><tr><td>recursive</td><td>boolean</td><td>If true, performs <br />recursively.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of <br />relationships <br />in a package.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getAppliedStereotypeByName(element, stereotypeName)</strong></h3><p>Return a stereotype assigned to an element.</p><table class="wrapped"><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype <br />name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype</td><td>An assigned <br />stereotype with <br />a specified name.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getBaseClassAssociations(classifier)</strong></h3><p>Obtain a collection of associations of a classifier.</p><table classifier.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of associations.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getClientElement(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a client of a relationship.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br client.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getComment(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a documentation of a given element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>The documentation <br />of an element.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><strong><br /></strong></strong></p><h3><strong style="font-size: 16.0px;"><strong>$report.getCustomizationProperty(element, stereotypeName, propertyName)</strong></strong></h3><p>Get a customization property from the specified applied stereotype of the element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td colspan="1">java.lang.String</td><td colspan="1">A stereotype name.</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">propertyName</td><td colspan="1">java.lang.String</td><td colspan="1">A property name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>A property tag value.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong style="font-size: 16.0px;"><strong>$report.getCustomizationPropertyString(element, stereotypeName, propertyName)</strong></strong></h3><p>Get a customization property as a String value from the specified applied stereotype of the element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property tag value as String.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getDerivedClassifiers(parent)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of derived elements of a classifier.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>parent</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier</td><td>A parent class.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />derived elements <br />(classifiers). </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getDiagramElements(diagram)</strong></h3><p>Get a collection of elements from a diagram.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col style="width: 107.0px;" /><col style="width: 73.0px;" /><col style="width: 409.0px;" /><col style="width: 104.0px;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>diagram</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</td><td>A diagram instance.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection <br />of elements in a diagram.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getDiagramType(diagram)</strong></h3><p>Return a diagram type.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th> Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>diagram</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</td><td>A diagram instance.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A diagram type.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getElementProperties(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a list of property names, including UML properties and properties that are customized through DSL customization.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of property names.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6647c6f4-77ed-49ca-948c-2f7456883a81"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($pp in $report.getElementProperties($element))
$pp.name > $pp.value
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6f2ed9ae-4f8a-4502-8909-536441b7822a"><ac:rich-text-body><p><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">By default, all properties are exported. They can be filtered by adding a &quot;STANDARD&quot; or &quot;EXPERT&quot; argument, e.g. </span><em><span style="color: rgb(14,16,26);">$report.getElementProperties($element, &quot;STANDARD&quot;).</span></em></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3><strong>$report.getElementPropertiesName(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a list of property names, including UML properties and properties that are customized through DSL customization.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of property names.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="32491bca-cc8e-482e-b90d-9185887be999"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($name in $report.getElementPropertiesName($element))
$name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getDSLProperty(element, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Return a DSL property.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td>propertyName </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property <br />name. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>A property <br />value.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getElementComment(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a comment attached to an element. If no comment is attached, it will return a null value.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Comment</td><td><p>A comment <br />instance of <br />the element.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getElementName(element)</strong></h3><p>Get an element’s name. If the name is empty, it will return &quot;&lt;unnamed&gt;&quot;. This method performs the following procedures:</p><ul><li>If the element is generalized from the NamedElement metaclass, it will return <em>$element.name</em></li><li>If the element is generalized from the Slot metaclass, it will return<em> $<span style="line-height: 1.42857;">element.definingFeature.name</span></em></li><li>If the element is a UML element, it will return <em>$element.humanName</em></li><li>Else returns <em>$element.toString()</em></li></ul><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An element name.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getIconFor(element)</strong></h3><p>Return an image icon of an element with a default icon filename (“icon_”+element type).</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type  </th><th> Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement </td><td>A modeling tool<br />element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object <br />of the element's icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="128d8588-a024-435b-b1ef-d0c0e499c9d6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.getIconFor(element)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getIconFor($classObject)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.</p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3>$report.getImageFor(element, dpi)</h3><p>Return an image with specified DPI of an element.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 43.1345%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 17.649%;" /><col style="width: 11.4654%;" /><col style="width: 46.1192%;" /><col style="width: 24.4767%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name </th><th>Type  </th><th> Description</th><th colspan="1">Description</th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement </td><td colspan="1"><span>A modeling tool</span><br /><span>element.</span></td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">dpi</td><td colspan="1">int</td><td colspan="1">DPI of the element image.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td colspan="1"><span>An image object </span><br /><span>with specified DPI.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84b916e7-f028-496d-9974-ec0878d833a8"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The DPI value can be 1 to 4800.</li><li>If less than 1, the value is not taken, and the DPI set in *Template* and *Global* will be used respectively. See <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Setting image DPI" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[setting image DPI]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>.</li><li>If more than 4800, <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">the value will be rounded down to 4800</span>.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getIconFor(element, prefix, suffix, hashCode)</strong></h3><p>Return an image icon of an element. The icon name depends on the <em><strong>hashCode</strong></em> value if <em><strong>hashCode</strong></em> equals true, the icon’s name is set as<em><strong> ”icon_”+hash</strong></em> code value of the icon. If the <em><strong>hashCode</strong> </em>value equals false, the icon name is set as</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)   </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement</td><td>A modeling tool element.</td></tr><tr><td>prefix</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A prefix of an icon name.</td></tr><tr><td>suffix</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A suffix of an icon name.</td></tr><tr><td>hashCode</td><td>boolean</td><td>If true, use hash code of an icon <br />as the icon name and exclude a <br />prefix or suffix from the name. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object of <br />an element’s icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="7be74159-443d-47b0-a7f1-85a517d6b7d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To get an icon and set a name for hash code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getIconFor($classObject, “prefix_”, “_suffix”, true)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.</span></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c28ec2e1-6a97-4fc4-9382-f979e47ad4ff"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To get an icon and set a name for prefix and suffix</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getIconFor($classObject, “prefix_”, “_suffix”, false)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.</span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getIconFor(type)</strong></h3><p>Return an image icon for an element type with a default icon’s filename ((“icon_”+element type).</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>type</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A type name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object for the element’s icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f48f62e3-a5df-4e3e-9d77-4af01997fcf6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.getIconFor(type)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getIconFor("class")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span>This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “icon_class”.</span></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getIconFor(type, prefix, suffix, hashCode)</strong></h3><p>Return an image icon for an element type. The icon’s name depends on the <em><strong>hashCode</strong></em> value if <em><strong>hashCode</strong></em> equals true, the icon name is set as <em><strong>“icon_”+hash</strong></em> code value of the icon. If the <em><strong>hashCode</strong></em> value equals false, the icon name is set as <em><strong>prefix + element type + suffix</strong></em>.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>  Type</th><th> Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)</strong><strong> </strong><strong> </strong><strong> </strong></td><td>type</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A type name.</td></tr><tr><td>prefix</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A prefix for an icon name.</td></tr><tr><td>suffix</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A suffix for an icon name.</td></tr><tr><td>hashCode</td><td>boolean</td><td>If true, use hash code of<br />an icon as the icon’s name <br />and exclude a prefix <br />or suffix from the name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicreport.Image</td><td>An image object <br />for an element’s icon.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="95a8eee3-a1bb-4db7-939e-83cc8731e2a3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To get an icon and set a name for hash code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getIconFor(“class”, “prefix_”, “_suffix”, true)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This results in an Image object of a class whose name “icon_24676200”. The number in the icon name is hash code, which is regenerated whenever the modeling tool starts.</span></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84549cb8-ec03-4c66-a7da-71f9d7d62d6c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">To get an icon and set a name for prefix and suffix</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getIconFor(“class”, “prefix_”, “_suffix”, false)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><span>This results in an Image object of a class whose name is “prefix_class_suffix”.</span></p><p><br /></p><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3>$report.getIncludeUseCase(useCase)</h3><p>Return a collection of included elements of a UseCase.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>useCase </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.UseCase</td><td>A UseCase <br />instance.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection <br />of included <br />UseCases.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3>$report.getInnerElement(element)</h3><p>Return a collection of inner elements.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name  </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />inner elements. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getInteractionMessageType(message)</strong></h3><p>Return an interaction message type.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>message</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.interactions.mdbasicinteractions.Message</td><td>A message <br />instance. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A message <br />type.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getMetaClass(stereotype)</strong></h3><p>Return a stereotype meta class.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name  </th><th>Type </th><th> Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>stereotype</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdprofiles.Stereotype</td><td>A stereotype <br />instance.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />Meta Classes.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getMetaClass(element)</strong></h3><p>Return an element meta class.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name  </th><th>Type </th><th> Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return </strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />Meta Classes.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.isTypeOf(element, stereotypeName)</strong></h3><p>Test an element and return true if the element is subtype of the specified stereotype.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element to test.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if the specified element is subtype of the specified stereotype.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getPresentationDiagramElements(diagram)</strong></h3><p>Return presentation elements in a diagram. This method will return all presentation elements from the diagram, except non-manipulator symbols.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>diagram</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</td><td>A diagram <br />instance. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection <br />of elements. </td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b1638eec-4a83-4373-aa5f-16d8a601c05a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($symbol in $report.getPresentationDiagramElements($diagram))
#set($element = $symbol.getElement())
$element.humanType $element.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getPresentationDiagramElements(diagram, includeNonManipulator)</strong></h3><p>Return presentation elements in a diagram.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>diagram</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</td><td>A diagram. </td></tr><tr><td>include NonManipulator</td><td>boolean</td><td>True to include <br />all non-manipulator <br />elements.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />elements.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getPresentationElementBounds(diagram, element)</strong></h3><p>Return the polygonal bounds of an element. The bounds specify the component coordinates to its diagram.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>diagram</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</td><td>A target diagram.</td></tr><tr><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>A presentation element <br />on a given diagram.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>java.util.List</td><td>Returns the polygonal <br />bounds of an element. <br />If the element is not found <br />in the diagram, it will return <br />null.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getPresentationElementBounds(element)</strong></h3><p>Return the polygonal bounds of an element. The bounds specify this component's coordinate to its diagram.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.symbols.PresentationElement </td><td>A presentation element <br />on a diagram. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.magicreport.helper.Polygon</td><td>Returns the polygonal <br />bounds of an element.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getPresentationElementRectangle(diagram, element)</strong></h3><p>Return the rectangular bounds of an element. The bounds specify the component coordinates to its diagram.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong><strong> </strong></td><td>diagram</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</td><td>A target diagram. </td></tr><tr><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>A presentation element <br />on a given diagram. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.List</td><td>Returns the rectangular <br />bounds of an element. <br />If the element is not found <br />in the diagram, it will return <br />null.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)</strong></h3><p>Get a qualified name. A qualified name is the name that allows a <em><strong>NamedElement</strong> </em>to be identified within a hierarchy of nested <em><strong>Namespaces</strong></em>. It is constructed from the names of the containing namespaces starting at the root of the hierarchy and ending with the name of the <em><strong>NamedElement</strong> </em>itself.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><strong> </strong></td><td>namedElement  </td><td>NamedElement </td><td>A NamedElement.</td></tr><tr><td>separator </td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A separator symbol. <br />If the value is null or <br />an empty string, <br />the '::' will be used.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A qualified name.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getPackageQualifiedName(namedElement, separator)</strong></h3><p>Get a qualified name by considering only <strong>Packages</strong> and a given element. <strong>Models</strong> and <strong>Profiles</strong> will not be included in the qualified name.</p><p>For example, given the element hierarchy:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4cc85964-1563-4a51-a92e-f0a48fd35a2b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of element hierarchy</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Design : Model -> com : Package -> nomagic : Package -> ui -> Package -> BaseDialog : Class]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="eb8afde6-9720-47e4-8bc1-855fcfa83f96"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of a template code of the element hierarchy</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$report.getPackageQualifiedName($class, ".")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>If $class is a “BaseDialog” element, the result from the above template code will be as follows.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4b91c128-0227-494a-aba4-f088badcd8bb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">The result of the template code</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[com.nomagic.ui.BaseDialog]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 54.7695%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 16.7151%;" /><col style="width: 17.5872%;" /><col style="width: 17.8779%;" /><col style="width: 47.8198%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong><strong> </strong></td><td>namedElement </td><td>NamedElement </td><td>A NamedElement.</td></tr><tr><td>separator</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A separator symbol. If the value is null or an empty string, the '::' will be used. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A qualified name. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getReceivingOperationalNode(element)</strong></h3><p>Get the needline association ends.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>A needline <br />association <br />instance. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getRelationship(element)</strong></h3><p>Return an element’s relationship.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element  </td><td>An element. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td><span>java.util.Collection</span></td><td><span>A collection <br />of relationships.</span></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="9d481d27-0cf4-4611-9db7-c283bde94e79"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($relationship in $report.getRelationship($element))
Name: $relationship.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getRelationship(element, recursive)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of element relationships.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th> Type  </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.element </td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td>recursive</td><td>boolean</td><td>If true, performs <br />recursively.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />relationships.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getRelativeActor(element)</strong></h3><p>Return an element’s relative actor.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th> Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection <br />of actors. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getSendingOperationalNode(element) </strong></h3><p>Return the needline’s association ends.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>A needline <br />association <br />instance.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getSlotValue(element, ClassifierName, DefiningFeatureName)</strong></h3><p>Find and retrieve the values of a slot whose name and classifier’s name are <em><strong>DefiningFeatureName</strong> </em>and <em><strong>ClassifierName</strong> </em>respectively. The code enables case-sensitivity queries such that only those case-sensitive matches will return.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th> Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong><br /><br /></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element whose <br />slot value you want <br />to find. </td></tr><tr><td>ClassifierName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A classifier name.</td></tr><tr><td>DefiningFeatureName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A slot name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of <br />ValueSpecifications <br />of a slot.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="324ce986-707d-46fc-b88e-f1123850bb17"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Use the code to retrieve the value of a slot whose name and classifier’s name are Name and Personal Data respectively.</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
Slot value is $report.getSlotValue($instance, "Personal Data", "Name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ul><li>“Personal Data” is the name of the classifier.</li><li>Name” is the name of the slot.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getStereotypeProperty(element, stereotypeName, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Get a stereotype property.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)</strong><br /><br /></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype name.</td></tr><tr><td>propertyName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>A property value.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4f115e99-fd5f-455b-b824-c3cb72c13f30"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($obj = $report.getStereotypeProperty($element, "MyStereotype", "myObject"))
$obj.name]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e30582cf-edd0-4f0c-b0db-7b2ed9567d25"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning: Duplicate stereotype name</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>stereotypeName is String. Please be aware of duplicate name of stereotype that may cause to random returned result. $report.getStereotypeProperty(element, profileName, stereotypeName, propertyName) may be an option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getStereotypeProperty(element, profileName, stereotypeName, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Get a stereotype property.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element instance.</td></tr><tr><td>profileName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A profile name.</td></tr><tr><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype name.</td></tr><tr><td>propertyName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property name. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>A property value.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getStereotypePropertyString(element, stereotypeName, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Return a stereotype property as a String value.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th> Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An element instance. </td></tr><tr><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype name. </td></tr><tr><td>propertyName</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A property name.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td><br /></td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property value as String.</td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8b2568a0-f6bd-487d-b979-66e0c3617d14"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($str = $report.getStereotypePropertyString($element, "MyStereotype", "myString"))
$str]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="warning" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e7c12958-8e3c-40c9-9161-8e90b21393fb"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Warning: Duplicate stereotype name</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>stereotypeName is String. Please be aware of duplicate name of stereotype that may cause to random returned result. $report.getStereotypePropertyString(element, profileName, stereotypeName, propertyName) may be an option.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getStereotypePropertyString(element, profileName, stereotypeName, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Get a stereotype property as a String value.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="4"><strong>Parameter(s)    </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element instance.</td></tr><tr><td>profileName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A profile name.</td></tr><tr><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype name.</td></tr><tr><td>propertyName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property name. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property value as String.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getPropertyGroupsByStereotype(stereotype)</strong></h3><p>Return a collection of property groups for the given stereotype.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p align="center"><strong> </strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></p></td><td><p>stereotype</p></td><td><p>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Class</p></td><td><p>A stereotype element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Return</strong></p></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><p>java.util.Collection</p></td><td><p>A collection of com.nomagic.magicdraw.propertygroup.PropertyGroup objects or empty if the stereotype has no group defined.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="70e4d256-a819-419b-8e48-1b7c6587885f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($stereotype in $report.getStereotypes($element))
#foreach($propertyGroup in $report.getPropertyGroupsByStereotype($stereotype))
Group: $propertyGroup.name
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><em><br /></em></p><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getPropertyGroupsByElement(element, addAlwaysVisible)</strong></h3><p>Return a set of property groups for the given element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p align="center"><strong> </strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></p></td><td><p>element</p></td><td><p>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</p></td><td><p>An element that may have property groups.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>addAlwaysVisible</p></td><td><p>boolean</p></td><td><p><strong>True</strong> if using all set stereotypes and all showWhenNotApplied stereotypes that can be set.</p><p><strong>False</strong> if using only all set stereotypes.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Return</strong></p></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><p>java.util.Set</p></td><td><p>A set of com.nomagic.magicdraw.propertygroup.PropertyGroup objects that are directly associated with the element.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f2d17656-b7fa-48c8-978e-a32891ddf1c6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($propertyGroup in $report.getPropertyGroupsByStereotype($element, true))
Group: $propertyGroup.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><em><br /></em></p><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getQProperty(element, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Get a property for an element with DSL property groups, and each property group has its own property.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p align="center"><strong> </strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Name</strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><p align="center"><strong>Description</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><p><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></p></td><td><p>element</p></td><td><p>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</p></td><td><p>An element.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>propertyName</p></td><td><p>java.lang.String</p></td><td><p>A property name.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Return</strong></p></td><td><p>-</p></td><td><p>com.nomagic.magicdraw.properties.Property</p></td><td><p>A property object for a specific element and propertyName.</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="8eb33234-6fc1-4c3c-bb48-a53900d155db"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($stereotype in $report.getStereotypes($element))
Stereotype: $stereotype.name
#foreach($propertyGroup in $report.getPropertyGroupsByStereotype($stereotype))
Group: $propertyGroup.name
#foreach($propertyName in $propertyGroup.getPropertyList())
#set($property = $report.getQProperty($element, $propertyName))
Property name: $property.name
Property value: $property.value
--------------------------------
#end
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getStereotypes(element)</strong></h3><p>Return all stereotypes applied to an element. This method is replaced by <em><strong>$element.appliedStereotype</strong></em>.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An element. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.List</td><td>A list of stereotype instances. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getSupplierElement(element)</strong></h3><p>Return the supplier of a relationship.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element  </td><td>A relationship model element. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>The relationship’s supplier.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getUsageElements(usagesMap, element)</strong></h3><p>Return the usage of a specified element.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 64.4674%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.7037%;" /><col style="width: 12.2222%;" /><col style="width: 50.7407%;" /><col style="width: 23.3333%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>usagesMap </td><td>java.util.Map </td><td>The Usage Map of the modeling tool.</td></tr><tr><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An
