element. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of element usages. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getUsages(selectedObjects)</strong></h3><p>Return the Usage Map of the modeling tool. It uses the getUsageElements method to return the usage of a specified element.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 52.2258%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 16.9207%;" /><col style="width: 19.2073%;" /><col style="width: 18.75%;" /><col style="width: 45.122%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>selectedObjects </td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Map</td><td>A Map instance of element usages.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.hasStereotype(element)</strong></h3><p>Check if an element has stereotypes.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 63.3545%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.9447%;" /><col style="width: 9.04523%;" /><col style="width: 51.1307%;" /><col style="width: 25.8794%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name  </th><th>Type </th><th>  Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element to check.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>boolean </td><td>True if it has a stereotype. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.containsStereotype(element, stereotypeName)</strong></h3><p>Return true if an element contains a stereotype (including all derived stereotypes) for a specified stereotype name.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element  </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element to check. </td></tr><tr><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype name to be <br />tested.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td> -</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if the element <br />contains a stereotype <br />for the specified <br />stereotype name. </td></tr></tbody></table><p class="auto-cursor-target">For example:<strong><br /></strong></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2977a3c9-bdf4-4127-8051-a812207be02f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#if($report.containsStereotype($element, "MyStereotype"))
````html
$element.name has been applied <<MyStereotype>>.
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong><br /></strong></p><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.containsStereotype(element, stereotypeName, includeDerived)</strong></h3><p>Return true if an element contains a stereotype for a specified stereotype name.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="3"><strong>Parameter(s)  </strong><br /><br /></td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element to test. </td></tr><tr><td>stereotypeName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A stereotype name <br />to be tested. </td></tr><tr><td>includeDerived</td><td>boolean</td><td><p>True if the searched <br />target includes all <br />derived stereotypes; <br />otherwise, false.</p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if the element <br />contains a stereotype <br />for the specified <br />stereotype name.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.isDerivedClassifier(parent, child)</strong></h3><p>Check if a child is derived from the parent by generalization.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 60.9698%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.9687%;" /><col style="width: 8.22454%;" /><col style="width: 53.9164%;" /><col style="width: 23.8903%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th> Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td rowspan="2"><strong>Parameter(s)</strong></td><td>parent</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier</td><td>A parent. </td></tr><tr><td>child</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Classifier</td><td>A possible child.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if it is derived from <br />the parent by generalization. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.isNamedElement(element)</strong></h3><p>Return whether an element is a NamedElement.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 60.8903%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 14.5098%;" /><col style="width: 9.41176%;" /><col style="width: 53.2026%;" /><col style="width: 22.8758%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element <br />to test.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>-</td><td>boolean </td><td><p>True if the given element is a NamedElement; otherwise, false</p></td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.isNull(obj)</strong></h3><p>Test and return true if an object is null.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>obj </td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An object being tested.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td>- </td><td>boolean</td><td>True if the object is null.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.isRelationship(element)</strong></h3><p>Test and return true if an element is a relationship.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th> Type</th><th> Description </th></tr><tr><td><strong>Parameter(s) </strong></td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element being tested.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Return</strong></td><td><p>-</p></td><td>boolean</td><td>True if the object is a <br />relationship.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.serialize(hyperlink)</strong></h3><p>Convert <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink</em> to <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.magicreport.helper.Hyperlink</em>. Report Wizard needs the <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.magicreport.helper.Hyperlink</em> class wrapper to return <em>com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink</em> data.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th> Type</th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>hyperlink</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.hyperlinks.Hyperlink </td><td>A modeling tool<br />hyperlink.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.plugins.impl.magicreport.helper.Hyperlink</td><td>A Report Wizard <br />hyperlink instance.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getUsedBy(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a list of element(s) used by this element (except diagrams).</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th> Type  </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td> element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An element of which <br />to determine the usage. </td></tr><tr><td> Return</td><td><br /></td><td> java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of elements <br />used by an input element.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.hasProperty(element, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Return true when a property with a given name is specified in this element, otherwise returns false.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th> Description</th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>propertyName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property name.</td></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td>  -</td><td><span>java.lang.Boolean</span></td><td>Return true when <br />a property with a <br />given name is specified <br />in this element, <br />otherwise returns false. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fe60122f-208a-4f82-8c07-a35b18952695"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $elements)
$report.hasProperty($element, "name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li>$element is the element.</li><li>data is the name of the property.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getProperty(element, propertyName)</strong></h3><p>Get element property.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 63.0366%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.2576%;" /><col style="width: 14.1414%;" /><col style="width: 51.3889%;" /><col style="width: 21.2121%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>propertyName</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A property name.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.Object </td><td>A property value. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d8d968e0-2a8e-4106-882e-d9dcc7485dd5"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.getProperty(element, propertyName)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($element in $elements)
$report.getProperty($element, "name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li>$element is the element.</li><li>name is the name of the property.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.findElementByName(source, regex)</strong></h3><p>Search and return elements that match the name using a regular expression.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 54.213%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 16.0059%;" /><col style="width: 9.39794%;" /><col style="width: 20.4112%;" /><col style="width: 54.185%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type  </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>source</td><td>java.util.Collection</td><td>A collection of elements.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>regex</td><td>java.lang.String </td><td>A regular expression with which the name is to be matched.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>-</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of matching elements.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be6d2c83-099b-411f-be23-3cb9310f79e4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.findElementByName(source, regex)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($ele in $report.findElementByName($elements, "[A]+.*"))
$ele.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li>$elements is a collection of elements to be found.</li><li>“[A]+.*” is a regular expression to find which element matches the name. In this example, the following are the names that match, such as Auxiliary, AppServer, and Alternative Fragment.</li></ul><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getPresentationElements(diagram)</strong></h3><p>Get a presentation element in a diagram. This method is equivalent to $report.getPresentationDiagramElements(diagram).</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 60.6518%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 13.7795%;" /><col style="width: 9.58005%;" /><col style="width: 53.6745%;" /><col style="width: 22.9659%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>diagram</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Diagram</td><td>A diagram instance.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.util.Collection </td><td>A collection of elements.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c3342fdf-8f18-4a80-907c-16792d1cec6b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.getPresentationElements(diagram)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($diagram in $Diagram)
$foreach($d in $report.getPresentationElements($diagram))
$d.name
#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>  </p><ul><li><strong>$diagram</strong> is the diagram instance.</li></ul><p><br /></p><h3><strong>$report.getUsageRepresentationText(baseElement, bool)</strong></h3><p>Format the usage subject. The output string is the same as the Result column of Used by table in Magic Draw.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th> Type  </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>baseElement</td><td>com.nomagic.magicdraw.uml.BaseElement</td><td>A model element <br />to be formatted.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>bool</td><td>java.lang.Boolean </td><td>True if a full path <br />is used, otherwise <br />false.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A formatted element.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="be400404-e6b1-426e-95e2-fe817e64f8e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">$report.getUsageRepresentationText(baseElement, bool)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($baseElement in $Elements)
$report.getUsageRepresentationText($baseElement, false)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><ul><li>$baseElement is the model element to be formatted.</li><li>false if a full path is not used. In this example, a full path is not used.</li></ul><h3><strong>$report.getUseCaseNumber(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a use case number of an element.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name</th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)  </td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>A model element. </td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>A use case number.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>  </p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bdb0873-446a-4cf9-bcc1-1897266adbaf"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.getUseCaseNumber(element)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($uc in $RequirementsUseCase)
$report.getUseCaseNumber($uc) $uc.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getElementURL(element)</strong></h3><p>Return a modeling tool's element URL.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th> Name </th><th>Type  </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>element </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element </td><td>An element.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>elementURL </td><td>java.lang.String</td><td>An element URL.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="244189d9-9966-46f5-b945-1d04c9299dc8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.getElementURL(element)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach ($element in $elements)
$report.getElementURL($element)
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.isEmpty(obj)</strong></h3><p>Test and return true if an object is null, empty string, or empty collection.</p><table class="relative-table wrapped" style="width: 46.6614%;"><colgroup><col style="width: 18.6325%;" /><col style="width: 11.453%;" /><col style="width: 21.0256%;" /><col style="width: 48.8889%;" /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name  </th><th>Type  </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>obj</td><td>java.lang.Object</td><td>An object being tested.</td></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td> -</td><td>boolean</td><td>True if the object is null, empty string, <br />or empty collection.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="263b28df-4ca1-4b69-847a-b89c5b39e310"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.isEmpty(obj)</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#if ($report.isEmpty($var))
Object is null, empty string or empty collection
#end ]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="717a1966-e305-41b9-88d0-6347213fd4e3"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>When checking HTML String format, <strong>$report.isEmpty</strong> ignores all <strong>&amp;nbsp</strong>, spaces, and line feeds in the returned tag value and returns <strong>false</strong> when there is one or more characters.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getBasicFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List&lt;FlowStep&gt;</strong></h3><p>Find and return a list of basic flows from a given use case. If a use case scenario is not being used, it will retrieve the value from &quot;requirementUseCase&quot; stereotype tags.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>usecase </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.UseCase </td><td>A targeted Use Case.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td>- </td><td>List&lt;com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep&gt;</td><td>A list of flow steps. <br />A Flow Step is an <br />object representing <br />each step of a Use <br />Case flow.</td></tr></tbody></table><p>The &lt;FlowStep&gt; is a &quot;com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep&quot; class.</p><p><br /></p><p>The important properties of &lt;FlowStep&gt; are as follows:</p><ul><li>name prints a name of a current step, for example: $flowStep.name.</li><li>element returns an element associates with a current step, for example: #set ($e = $flowStep.element).</li><li>alternativeConditions returns a list of alternative steps &lt;AlternativeCondition&gt; from a current step, for example: #foreach ($alter in $flowStep.alternativeConditions) #end.</li><li>exceptionTypes returns a list of exception steps &lt;ExceptionType&gt; from a current step, for example: #foreach ($alter in $flowStep.exceptionTypes) #end.</li></ul><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="94499c52-bdee-45a4-ad9b-5cedef83d391"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.getBasicFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List&lt;FlowStep&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($basicFlowList = $report.getBasicFlows($useCase))
#foreach ($flowStep in $basicFlowList)
   $flowStep.name
   #foreach ($alter in $flowStep.alternativeConditions)
      - $alter.name
   #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getAlternativeFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List&lt;FlowStep&gt;</strong></h3><p>Find and return all alternative flows from a given use case. The alternative flows will be listed from all possible branches of basic flows. If a use case scenario is not being used, it will retrieve the value from &quot;requirementUseCase&quot; stereotype tags.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th><strong>Name</strong> </th><th> Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>usecase</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.UseCase</td><td>A targeted Use Case.</td></tr><tr><td>Return </td><td> -</td><td>List&lt;com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep&gt;</td><td>A list of flow steps. <br />A Flow Step is an <br />object representing <br />each step of a Use <br />Case flow. </td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6f15065-7a8d-43ab-bffe-059b4ecd6b2d"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.getAlternativeFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List&lt;FlowStep&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($alternativeFlowList = $report.getAlternativeFlows($useCase))
$useCase.name
 #foreach ($flowStep in $alternativeFlowList)
   $flowStep.name
   #foreach ($alternative in $flowStep.alternativeConditions)
      $alternative.name
         #foreach ($alternativeFlow in $alternative.alternativeFlowSteps)
         $alternativeFlow.name
         #end
   #end
 #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getExceptionalFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List&lt;FlowStep&gt;</strong></h3><p>Find and return all exceptional flows from a given use case. The alternative flows will be listed from all possible branches of basic flows. If the use case scenario is not being used, it will retrieve the value from &quot;requirementUseCase&quot; stereotype tags.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s)</td><td>usecase </td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.mdusecases.UseCase </td><td>A targeted Use Case.</td></tr><tr><td>Return  </td><td>- </td><td>List&lt;com.nomagic.magicdraw.usecasescenarios.scenarios.FlowStep&gt; </td><td>A list of flow steps. <br />A flow step is an object <br />representing each steps <br />of a Use Case flow.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b282b5c7-e9ca-4133-9813-d855f0bb057c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample of $report.getExceptionalFlows(usecase:UseCase) : List&lt;FlowStep&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set ($exceptionFlowList = $report.getExceptionalFlows($useCase))
     #foreach ($flowStep in $exceptionFlowList)
         $flowStep.name
         #foreach (#exception in $flowStep.exceptionTypes)
             $exception.name
             #foreach ($exceptionalFlow in $exception.exceptionalFlowSteps)
                  $exceptionalFlow.name
             #end
         #end
     #end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><strong><br /></strong></p><h3><strong>$report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional(element, includePureOwned) : List&lt;Element&gt;</strong></h3><p>This code returns owned elements of a given element including additional owned elements defined in the DSL specification by additionalContentProperty.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>Name </th><th>Type </th><th>Description </th></tr><tr><td>Parameter(s) </td><td>element</td><td>com.nomagic.uml2.ext.magicdraw.classes.mdkernel.Element</td><td>A model element.</td></tr><tr><td><br /></td><td>includePureOwned</td><td>java.lang.Boolean </td><td>True if includes an <br />element owned directly <br />in the ownedElement <br />UML metaproperty. <br />Otherwise False.</td></tr><tr><td>Return</td><td> -</td><td>java.util.List </td><td>A list of owned elements <br />of a given element.</td></tr></tbody></table><p><br /></p><p>The following are the examples of using the code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="01e4c368-0758-4257-bd5f-5c5f87448232"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample 1: $report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional(element, includePureOwned) : List&lt;Element&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($e in $elements)
$e.name : $e.humanType
  #foreach($child in $report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional($e, true))
    $child.name : $child.humanType
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4a63faa6-354f-453a-a364-fe471a27e8e6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample 2: $report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional(element, includePureOwned) : List&lt;Element&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($e in $SmartPackage)
$e.name : $e.humanType
    #foreach($child in $report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional($e, true))
       $child.name : $child.humanType
    #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5b9af306-fbd6-4886-bc11-b624be470aa4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Sample 3: $report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional(element, includePureOwned) : List&lt;Element&gt;</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($packages = $array.createArray())
#set($void = $array.addCollection($packages, $Package))
#set($void = $array.addCollection($packages, $SmartPackage))
#foreach($e in $packages)
$e.name : $e.humanType
    #foreach($child in $report.getOwnedElementsIncludingAdditional($e, true))
        $child.name : $child.humanType
    #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144112 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00924: Report Wizard

- page_id: `189144112`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144112/Report+Wizard
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:42.457+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard is a report engine for the modeling tool version 14.0 and greater. It is designed to solve several problems of our legacy report engines (*XSL/XSLT* and *JPython*). 
The Report Wizard report engine is built on top of Velocity Engine (Open Source Templating engine) and is integrated with the application. To make the best of Report Wizard, you need to understand the Report Wizard UI, the Velocity Template Language (*VTL*), the application's Open API, and the helper modules. 
Report Wizard supports text-based templates to generate reports. Each report file format depends on the type of the templates. The type of template files that Report Wizard supports includes *plain text, RTF, HTML, Office Open XML* *(ISO/IEC 29500:2008)*, *OpenDocument format (ISO/IEC 26300)*, and *XML template (DocBook or FO)*. It also supports the MS Word template in VBA Editor on Windows 8.

All commercial modeling tool editions will have full use of all features within Report Wizard. The Community edition allows you to generate reports with watermarks.

Before generating a report, you need to open Report Wizard.

How to open Report Wizard

- On the Tools menu, click Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog will appear.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Report Wizard is a report engine for the modeling tool version 14.0 and greater. It is designed to solve several problems of our legacy report engines (<em>XSL/XSLT</em> and <em>JPython</em>).<br />The Report Wizard report engine is built on top of Velocity Engine (Open Source Templating engine) and is integrated with the application. To make the best of Report Wizard, you need to understand the Report Wizard UI, the Velocity Template Language (<em>VTL</em>), the application's Open API, and the helper modules.<br />Report Wizard supports text-based templates to generate reports. Each report file format depends on the type of the templates. The type of template files that Report Wizard supports includes <em>plain text, RTF, HTML, Office Open XML</em> <em>(ISO/IEC 29500:2008)</em>, <em>OpenDocument format (ISO/IEC 26300)</em>, and <em>XML template (DocBook or FO)</em>. It also supports the MS Word template in VBA Editor on Windows 8.</p><p>All commercial modeling tool editions will have full use of all features within Report Wizard. The Community edition allows you to generate reports with watermarks.</p><p>Before generating a report, you need to open Report Wizard.</p><p><br /></p><p>How to open Report Wizard</p><hr /><ul><li>On the <strong>Tools</strong> menu, click <strong>Report Wizard</strong>. The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog will appear.</li></ul>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144487 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00925: Report Wizard custom language

- page_id: `189144487`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144487/Report+Wizard+custom+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:48.465+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Report Wizard template language
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Information

1088025757

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Information

1088025760

1088025755

**On this page**

22

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Information

1088025759

#### #forrow directive

The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Velocity template language' space=''] does not support loops inside a table structure. However, the Report Wizard engine introduces a new custom syntax that allows looping inside the table structure in order to clone the table rows.

```text
The syntax is : #forrow <query data> #endrow
```

For example:

| Name | Owner |
| --- | --- |
| #forrow ($uc in $UseCase) $uc.name | $uc.owner.humanName #endrow |

**#forrow ($uc in $UseCase) $uc.name** is used for printing a use case that one row will be printed for one use case. However, there are two use cases in this example, thus there are two rows printed out. The output will be as follows:

| Name | Owner |
| --- | --- |
| UC1 | Package Requirement |
| UC2 | Package Requirement |

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

- Statements preceding the #forrow directive, but in the same row or line will execute after the #forrow directive because #forrow is a first priority directive, while statements in the preceding rows will execute in normal sequence.
- Other statements like importing a library, or creating an array variable, have to be declared in the rows preceding the #forrow directive.

#### #forpage directive

The **#forpage** directive is used to provide a loop over the codes within a page. This directive provides implementation like **#forrow**, but it creates a loop over a page instead of a row. For example:

```text

```

The report will contain a UseCase name for each document page.

#### TIP: Tip

Tip

- Statements preceding the #forpage directive, but in the same page will execute after the #forpage directive because #forpage is a first priority directive, while statements in the preceding pages will execute in normal sequence.
- Other statements like importing a library, or creating an array variable, have to be declared in the pages preceding the #forpage directive.

#### #forpage directive for OpenDocument Presentation and Spreadsheet

When this directive appears in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)' space=''], it will create a loop over all directives that are present on the current page. All directives on this page will be included inside **#forpage** as shown in the figures below.

An example of a **#forpage** directive.

```text

```

In [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet' space=''], you can also use the directives as a template. The figure below displays an example of the**#forpage** directive in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet (ODS) file. When this directive is used in an ODS template, it will create a separate spreadsheet representing each name of the use cases in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The sample of #forpage directive to create a template in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.

The figure below shows the output after including the directive code shown in the figure above. **Another Withdraw** is the name of one of the use cases in the model.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The output after including the #forpage directive in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.

#### #forcol directive for OpenDocument Spreadsheet, Excel Workbook, Word Document, and PowerPoint Presentation

This directive is designed only for the spreadsheet templates, which are OpenDocument Spreadsheet (ODS), Excel Workbook (XLSX), Word Document (DOCX), and PowerPoint Presentation (PPTX). This directive provides looping over the columns. In the figure below, the engine will generate a report with different columns for each Use Case name.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The sample of #forcol directive to create a template in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.

The output from this sample will be as shown in the figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The output after including the #forcol directive in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.

You can combine both **#forrow** and **#forcol** directives and produce a more complex report output as shown below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The sample of #forrow and #forcol directives to create a template in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.

The figure below shows an output generated from the [ATTACHMENT filename='Magic Library.mdzip'] sample project.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The output after including the #forrow and #forcol directives in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.

Since the **#forrow** syntax is similar to the **#foreach** syntax, the **#foreach** syntax can then be used in **#forrow**.

You can find more examples of using the **#forrow** and **#forcol** directives in the **Other Documents** templates in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard dialog' space=''].

To open an **Other Documents** template in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Wizard dialog' space='']**Report Wizard** dialog

1. On the main menu, click Tools > Report Wizard . The Report Wizard dialog opens.
2. In the Select Template tree, expand Other Documents .
3. Select any template and click Open .

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

#### #include and #parse directives

The original [Velocity Engine](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/1.7/user-guide.html#include) provides two include directives: **#include** and **#parse**.

**#include** allows you to import another template. The contents of the file will not be rendered through the template engine.

To include a filename using**#include($fileName)**, for example, type either:

```text

```

or

```text

```

#### INFO: Information

Information

- $fileName can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.
- $fileName can be only static or dynamic String.
- If the file is not found in the file system, $fileName would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.

**#parse** allows you to import another template. The contents of the file will be rendered through the template engine. However, the file being included will be inserted with all contents.

#### #includeSection directive

Report Wizard introduces a statement, which allows a template to include any section of a document from another template. This statement requires the template to define the beginning and the end of the section.

The logical concept of the **#includeSection** and **#parse** directives is similar. Both directives allow a template to include another template and render it through the template engine. However, **#includeSection** can be used to specify only the section that you would like to include.

To declare a section, for example, type:

```text

```

To include a section, for example, type:

```text

```

#### INFO: Information

Information

- fileName and sectionName must be static String only, e.g., #includeSection('document.docx', 'sectionA') .
- If the file is not found in the file system, fileName would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.
- **#sectionBegin** and **#sectionEnd** must be in the template body. Headers and footers are not allowed.

#### #include, #parse, and #includeSection directives: A comparison

The **#include** and **#parse** directives are built-in directives provided by Velocity. The **#includeSection** directive is a custom directive implemented by the modeling tool. The table below shows the differences among these three directives.

|  | #include | #parse | #includeSection |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Execution Time | Executed at runtime. | Executed as a separate template at runtime. | Executed at translation time. |
| Variable Scope | Variables declared in the parent template are not accessible in the included page. | Variables declared in the parent template can be accessed in the included page. | Variables declared in the parent template can be accessed in the included page. |
| Rendering | The Include template is not rendered through the template engine. | The Include template is rendered through the template engine as a separate process. | The Include template is rendered through the template engine as a single process. |
| Include only required section | No | No | Yes |
| Size of parent template | The size of the parent template remains unchanged. | The size of the parent template remains unchanged. | The size of the parent template is increased by the included section. |
| Processing overhead | The #include directive increases the processing overhead with the necessity of an additionalcall to the template engine. | The #parse directive increases the processing overhead with the necessity of an additional call to the template engine. | The #includeSection directive does not increase the processing overhead. |
| Support RTF template | No | No | Yes |
| Support ODF template | No | No | Yes |
| Support DOCX template | No | No | Yes |
| Support XLSX template | No | No | No |
| Support PPTX template | No | No | No |

1088049528

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet' space='']

````html
<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="88f610f5-f3d8-4854-8452-3e1cfb91a471"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1088025757</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="30169b36-c846-49b1-8d14-420e4569e586"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1088025760</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1be6bf89-d4ac-4984-88ea-fbc494173dcf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1088025755</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="248768c5-7ebf-48de-ac35-f8efee3be33f"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">2</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f670654a-2efc-43fa-bcf5-122ca5d0db55"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1088025759</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><h2>#forrow directive</h2><p>The <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Velocity template language" /></ac:link> does not support loops inside a table structure. However, the Report Wizard engine introduces a new custom syntax that allows looping inside the table structure in order to clone the table rows.</p><pre>The syntax is : #forrow &lt;query data&gt; #endrow</pre><p>For example:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Owner</th></tr><tr><td>#forrow ($uc in $UseCase) $uc.name</td><td>$uc.owner.humanName #endrow</td></tr></tbody></table><p><strong>#forrow ($uc in $UseCase) $<span class="external-link">uc.name</span></strong> is used for printing a use case that one row will be printed for one use case. However, there are two use cases in this example, thus there are two rows printed out. The output will be as follows:</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th>Name</th><th>Owner</th></tr><tr><td>UC1</td><td>Package Requirement</td></tr><tr><td colspan="1">UC2</td><td colspan="1">Package Requirement</td></tr></tbody></table><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4c491711-6b05-40d1-9d97-ed154c8c2646"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Statements preceding the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive, but in the same row or line will execute after the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive because <strong>#forrow</strong> is a first priority directive, while statements in the preceding rows will execute in normal sequence.</li><li>Other statements like importing a library, or creating an array variable, have to be declared in the rows preceding the <strong>#forrow</strong> directive.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>#forpage directive</h2><p>The <strong>#forpage</strong> directive is used to provide a loop over the codes within a page. This directive provides implementation like <strong>#forrow</strong>, but it creates a loop over a page instead of a row. For example:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2ae2d662-b2a6-448f-8d4c-82220a330a0f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#forpage($uc in $UseCase)
$uc.name
#endpage]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />The report will contain a UseCase name for each document page.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f62d1155-cecb-4892-96e6-a2231524c7ef"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Tip</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>Statements preceding the <strong>#forpage</strong> directive, but in the same page will execute after the <strong>#forpage</strong> directive because <strong>#forpage</strong> is a first priority directive, while statements in the preceding pages will execute in normal sequence.</li><li>Other statements like importing a library, or creating an array variable, have to be declared in the pages preceding the <strong>#forpage</strong> directive.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>#forpage directive for OpenDocument Presentation and Spreadsheet</h2><p>When this directive appears in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a template for an OpenDocument Presentation (ODP) or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX)" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OpenDocument Presentation template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, it will create a loop over all directives that are present on the current page. All directives on this page will be included inside <strong>#forpage</strong> as shown in the figures below.</p><p>An example of a <strong>#forpage</strong> directive.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6cab93df-e0d9-4737-8939-c6cda1f46369"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#forpage ($uc in $UseCase)
$uc.name
#endpage]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />In <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Creating a template for an OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[OpenDocument Spreadsheet template]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link>, you can also use the directives as a template. The figure below displays an example of the<strong> #forpage</strong> directive in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet (ODS) file. When this directive is used in an ODS template, it will create a separate spreadsheet representing each name of the use cases in the model.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ODS #forpage template.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The sample of #forpage directive to create a template in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.</h6><p>The figure below shows the output after including the directive code shown in the figure above. <strong>Another Withdraw</strong> is the name of one of the use cases in the model.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="output for ODS #forpage template.PNG" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The output after including the #forpage directive in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.</h6><h2>#forcol directive for OpenDocument Spreadsheet, Excel Workbook, Word Document, and PowerPoint Presentation</h2><p>This directive is designed only for the spreadsheet templates, which are OpenDocument Spreadsheet (ODS), Excel Workbook (XLSX), Word Document (DOCX), and PowerPoint Presentation (PPTX). This directive provides looping over the columns. In the figure below, the engine will generate a report with different columns for each Use Case name.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OpenDocument Spreadsheet #forcol template.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The sample of #forcol directive to create a template in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.</h6><p>The output from this sample will be as shown in the figure below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OpenDocument Spreadsheet #forcol output.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The output after including the #forcol directive in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.</h6><p>You can combine both <strong>#forrow</strong> and <strong>#forcol</strong> directives and produce a more complex report output as shown below.</p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OpenDocument Spreadsheet #forrow and #forcol template.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The sample of #forrow and #forcol directives to create a template in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.</h6><p>The figure below shows an output generated from the <ac:link><ri:attachment ri:filename="Magic Library.mdzip" /></ac:link> sample project.  </p><p><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="OpenDocument Spreadsheet #forrow and #forcol output report.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">The output after including the #forrow and #forcol directives in an OpenDocument Spreadsheet file.</h6><p>Since the <strong>#forrow</strong> syntax is similar to the <strong>#foreach</strong> syntax, the <strong>#foreach</strong> syntax can then be used in <strong>#forrow</strong>.</p><p>You can find more examples of using the <strong>#forrow</strong> and <strong>#forcol</strong> directives in the <strong>Other Documents</strong> templates in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard dialog" /></ac:link>.</p><p><br /></p><p>To open an <strong>Other Documents</strong> template in the <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Report Wizard dialog" /><ac:link-body><strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog</ac:link-body></ac:link></p><hr /><ol><li>On the main menu, click <strong>Tools</strong> &gt;<strong> Report Wizard</strong>. The <strong>Report Wizard</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>In the <strong>Select Template</strong> tree, expand <strong>Other Documents</strong>.</li><li>Select any template and click <strong>Open</strong>.</li></ol><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Report Wizard Other Templates.png" /></ac:image></p><h2>#include and #parse directives</h2><p>The original <a class="inline-comment-marker" href="http://velocity.apache.org/engine/1.7/user-guide.html#include">Velocity Engine</a> provides two include directives: <strong>#include</strong> and <strong>#parse</strong>.</p><p><strong>#include</strong> allows you to import another template. The contents of the file will not be rendered through the template engine.</p><p>To include a filename using<strong> #include($fileName)</strong>, for example, type either:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f9a2f443-2785-443d-95f0-aad6aa9347da"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#set($fileName = $attachedFile.file)
#include($fileName)]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target">or</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b053916d-d218-4c5c-a5e9-418393a203f6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#include("document.docx")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="31c7de69-db80-4746-bf3e-e0e275fa993b"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><strong>$fileName</strong> can be either an absolute or relative path to a file in the file system.</li><li><strong>$fileName</strong> can be only static or dynamic String.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>$fileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><br /></p><p><strong>#parse</strong> allows you to import another template. The contents of the file will be rendered through the template engine. However, the file being included will be inserted with all contents.</p><h2>#includeSection directive</h2><p>Report Wizard introduces a statement, which allows a template to include any section of a document from another template. This statement requires the template to define the beginning and the end of the section.</p><p>The logical concept of the <strong>#includeSection</strong> and <strong>#parse</strong> directives is similar. Both directives allow a template to include another template and render it through the template engine. However, <strong>#includeSection</strong> can be used to specify only the section that you would like to include.</p><p><br /></p><p>To declare a section, for example, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fabdc2df-0719-49dc-8ed7-8a56b6b85aa1"><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#sectionBegin ('sectionA')
...
#sectionEnd]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br />To include a section, for example, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="a8ccc961-d4d1-45af-b8d6-5c3b3a95a8e1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#includeSection('document.docx', 'sectionA')]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="86cf92d5-a4ba-4953-b445-8758ea3aef49"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Information</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li><strong>fileName</strong> and <strong>sectionName</strong> must be static String only, e.g., <strong>#includeSection('document.docx', 'sectionA')</strong>.</li><li>If the file is not found in the file system, <strong>fileName</strong> would be searched as an Attached File Element in a project in the File property.</li><li><p><strong>#sectionBegin</strong> and <strong>#sectionEnd</strong> must be in the template body. Headers and footers are not allowed.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>#include, #parse, and #includeSection directives: A comparison</h2><p>The <strong>#include</strong> and <strong>#parse</strong> directives are built-in directives provided by Velocity. The <strong>#includeSection</strong> directive is a custom directive implemented by the modeling tool. The table below shows the differences among these three directives.</p><table class="wrapped"><colgroup><col /><col /><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody><tr><th><br /></th><th>#include </th><th>#parse </th><th>#includeSection </th></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Execution </strong><strong style="line-height: 1.42857;">Time </strong></p></td><td>Executed at runtime.</td><td>Executed as a separate template at runtime. </td><td>Executed at translation time. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Variable Scope</strong></td><td>Variables declared in the parent template <br />are not accessible in the included page. </td><td>Variables declared in the parent template can be <br />accessed in the included page.</td><td>Variables declared in the parent template can <br />be accessed in the included page. </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Rendering </strong></td><td>The Include template is not rendered through <br />the template engine. </td><td>The Include template is rendered through the <br />template engine as a separate process.</td><td><p>The Include template is rendered through the <br />template engine as a single process. </p></td></tr><tr><td><strong>Include only required section</strong></td><td>No</td><td>No </td><td>Yes </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Size of parent template</strong></td><td>The size of the parent template remains <br />unchanged.</td><td>The size of the parent template remains unchanged.</td><td>The size of the parent template is increased <br />by the included section.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Processing overhead</strong></td><td>The #include directive increases the processing <br />overhead with the necessity of an additional<br />call to the template engine.</td><td>The #parse directive increases the processing overhead with <br />the necessity of an additional <br />call to the template engine.</td><td>The #includeSection directive does not increase <br />the processing overhead.</td></tr><tr><td><strong>Support RTF<em> </em>template</strong></td><td>No</td><td>No </td><td>Yes </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Support ODF template</strong></td><td>No </td><td>No </td><td>Yes </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Support DOCX template</strong></td><td>No</td><td>No </td><td>Yes </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Support XLSX template</strong></td><td>No </td><td>No </td><td>No </td></tr><tr><td><strong>Support PPTX template</strong></td><td>No </td><td>No </td><td>No </td></tr></tbody></table></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="38334b6f-1514-4c58-834c-ef16d53efcdb"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1088049528</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ## PAGE 00926: Report Wizard dialog
````

- page_id: `189144113`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144113/Report+Wizard+dialog

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1193232567

1193232570

1193232569

The Report Wizard dialog, see figure below, consists of 2 panes: **Control buttons** and **Content Management pane**.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Report Wizard Dialog.

#### Control buttons

There are 4 control buttons

- The [ATTACHMENT filename='back-btn.png'] button is used for proceeding to the previous content management pane.
- The [ATTACHMENT filename='next-btn.png'] button is used for proceeding to the next content management pane.
- The [ATTACHMENT filename='generate-btn.png'] button is used for generating a report.
- The [ATTACHMENT filename='cancel-btn.png'] button is used for cancelling the report generation process.

#### Content Management pane

This pane is used for managing the template content and includes the following sub-panes:

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Template Management Pane' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Report Data Management Pane' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Select Element Scope pane' space='']

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Generate Output pane' space='']

Click the **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** or **[IMAGE alt='' src='']** button to go to a specific pane.

1193232566

**Related pages**

1357591841

1357591840

Report Wizard introduces three directives: **#forrow**, **#forcol**, and **#forpage**. These directives allow you to use Velocity to generate outputs other than web documents. With these directives, you can eliminate problems that usually occur when using Velocity to generate an rtf document, for example, **#foreach** generates broken rows in a table.

**#forrow** 
 This directive is used in the Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text, and OpenDocument spreadsheet, Microsoft Word Document (DOCX), and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX) documents. Whenever #forrow iterates through a list, it creates a row in a table.

**#forcol** 
 This directive is used only in Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX), OpenDocument spreadsheet, and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX). Whenever #forcol iterates through a list, it creates a column in a table.

**#forpage** 
 This directive is used in Rich Text Format document, OpenDocument Text, OpenDocument spreadsheet, OpenDocument presentation, Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), and Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX). Whenever #forpage iterates through a list, it creates a new page in Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text or Microsoft Word (DOCX). It also creates a new sheet in OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), or a new slide in OpenDocument presentation or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX).

1357591839

**Related pages**

These directives allow you to use Velocity to generate outputs other than web documents. With these directives, you can eliminate problems that usually occur when using Velocity to generate an rtf document, for example, <strong>#foreach</strong> generates broken rows in a table.</p><p><strong>#forrow</strong><br /> This directive is used in the Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text, and OpenDocument spreadsheet, Microsoft Word Document (DOCX), and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX) documents. Whenever #forrow iterates through a list, it creates a row in a table.</p><p><strong>#forcol</strong><br /> This directive is used only in Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX), OpenDocument spreadsheet, and Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX). Whenever #forcol iterates through a list, it creates a column in a table.</p><p><strong>#forpage</strong><br /> This directive is used in Rich Text Format document, OpenDocument Text, OpenDocument spreadsheet, OpenDocument presentation, Microsoft Word document (DOCX), Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), and Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX). Whenever #forpage iterates through a list, it creates a new page in Rich Text Format, OpenDocument text or Microsoft Word (DOCX). It also creates a new sheet in OpenDocument spreadsheet or Microsoft Excel spreadsheet (XLSX), or a new slide in OpenDocument presentation or Microsoft PowerPoint presentation (PPTX).</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ca5360a2-51d0-433c-bbfd-bb15fb10902a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1357591839</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="8a118dac-10d5-49c2-8c92-e5f75d8cabab" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144848 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00928: Report Wizard Environment options

- page_id: `189144848`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144848/Report+Wizard+Environment+options
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:12.473+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Note

1334042562

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Note

1334042564

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Note

1334042564

The Report Wizard Environment options in the **Environment Options** dialog allow you to configure the report engine. There are three things that you can configure: (i) Report engine properties, (ii) Template mappings, and (iii) Template folder.

**(i) Report engine properties**

Report engine properties can be configured as follows:

- Template process size (template.process.size) This integer value defines the size of allocated memory for processing a template. If the template is larger than the allocated size, the engine will create temporary files on a local disk and process the template from these files.
- Template pool size (template.pool.size) This integer value defines the number of processing threads for evaluating a template. An increase in the number of threads may improve the performance of the engine, which will also depend on your hardware and JVM.
- Velocity properties These properties are a key-value pair. You can add any number for these properties. You will need a basic knowledge of Velocity to enter the value. (See [http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/apidocs/org/apache/velocity/runtime/RuntimeConstants.html](http://velocity.apache.org/engine/devel/apidocs/org/apache/velocity/runtime/RuntimeConstants.html) )
  - velocimacro.max.depth
  - velocimacro.context.localscope
- Debug report template . You can configure this value to enable or disable any invalid properties, references, or exception messages on the message window. See [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Debug report template' space=''] for more details.
  - template.warn.invalid.property
  - template.warn.invalid.reference
  - template.warn.invalid.method
  - template.warn.exception
- rememberOutputOptions . This value determines whether Report Wizard will remember output options of the report file when generating report. Yes if this value is true, and No if this value is false. See the last figure on this page.
- synchronizeAttachedTemplate . This value is for synchronizing an attached local report with the latest report in the server project. This property is used every time a report is generated from the attached template. Other properties that are not in the Configure report properties table are as follows:
  - template.rtf.newline Specify the new line character for the RTF documentation. The values are line (by default) and paragraph .
  - template.docx.ignorechildstyle A key for ignoring styles from included templates in the generated report. When False (by default), the styles from included templates are included in the generated report. When True , the styles from included templates are not included in the generated report, and matching styles in the main template are applied instead.
  - template.xlsx.html.link A key for selecting the first or last hyperlink in the HTML string of any cell and applying the selected hyperlink to the cell. The values are first , last , and skip .
  - element.additional.include Use an additional element and owned element to get children of the element.
  - template.odp.use.virtual.name A key for enabling the engine to replace an ODP hyperlink with a page name.

**(ii) Template mappings**

Template mappings associate a template type with the template engine. The Report engine allows you to determine how to handle different types of files, such as JS or DOCBOOK files. All you have to do is specify a filename extension and select an engine name. The Report engine also allows developers to add a user custom engine to this option.

**(iii) Template folder**

You can either select or clear the **Monitor template folder** check box in the Report Wizard Environment options in the **Environments Options** dialog to enable or disable the option to deploy an MRZIP template file. If you select the check box, Report Wizard will automatically deploy the MRZIP template file to the Report Wizard dialog whenever the file is added to your folder.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Report Wizard GUI configuration in the Environment Options dialog. 
 
[IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
The Report properties dialog in the Report Wizard Environment options.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

After updating the Report Wizard Environment options, restart the application to take effect of the changes.

1334042561

**Related pages**

This property is used every time a report is generated from the attached template.<br /><br />Other properties that are not in the <strong>Configure report properties</strong> table are as follows:<br /><br /><ul><li>template.rtf.newline<br />Specify the new line character for the RTF documentation. The values are <strong>line</strong> (by default) and <strong>paragraph</strong>.<br /><br /></li><li>template.docx.ignorechildstyle<br />A key for ignoring styles from included templates in the generated report. When <strong>False</strong> (by default), the styles from included templates are included in the generated report. The Report engine also allows developers to add a user custom engine to this option.</p><p><strong>(iii) Template folder</strong></p><p>You can either select or clear the <strong>Monitor template folder</strong> check box in the Report Wizard Environment options in the <strong>Environments Options</strong> dialog to enable or disable the option to deploy an MRZIP template file. If you select the check box, Report Wizard will automatically deploy the MRZIP template file to the Report Wizard dialog whenever the file is added to your folder.</p><h6 style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:title="Report Wizard GUI Configuration in MagicDraw Environment Options Dialog" ac:alt="Report Wizard GUI Configuration in MagicDraw Environment Options Dialog"><ri:attachment ri:filename="env-options-a.png" /></ac:image><br />Report Wizard GUI configuration in the Environment Options dialog.<br /><br /><ac:image ac:title="Report Wizard Environment Options" ac:alt="Report Wizard Environment Options"><ri:attachment ri:filename="env-options-b.png" /></ac:image><br />The Report properties dialog in the Report Wizard Environment options.</h6><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f3a65857-f818-49ee-ac9b-46bfee8ceecf"><ac:parameter ac:name="title">Note</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>After updating the Report Wizard Environment options, restart the application to take effect of the changes.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="757a94e9-cebd-4f05-894b-23c18f1e1053"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1334042561</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="children" ac:schema-version="2" ac:macro-id="591c186a-5723-46a1-9b1e-6e23c77fede7" /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144478 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00929: Report Wizard Quick Print

- page_id: `189144478`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144478/Report+Wizard+Quick+Print
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:47.473+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Report Wizard provides five keyboard shortcuts to generate a report, with last used options, from a recently-used template. The table below lists the predefined keyboard shortcuts:

| Keyboard Shortcut | Quick Print Template |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + 1 | Template name A |
| Alt + 2 | Template name B |
| Alt + 3 | Template name C |
| Alt + 4 | Template name D |
| Alt + 5 | Template name E |

To change a keyboard shortcut

1. On the main menu, click Options > Environment to open the Environment Options dialog.
2. Select Keyboard from the tree list.
3. Select Tools from the Category drop-down menu.
4. Press the new shortcut keys on your keyboard, and click [ATTACHMENT filename='Assign_button.png'] .

To use Report Wizard Quick Print

1. After a report has been generated, a new menu will appear below the **Report Wizard** menu. The name of the new menu is the name of the recently generated template. [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Newly generated reports are listed on the **Tools** main menu.
2. Either click the template name, or press **Alt+1**, **Alt+2**, or**Alt+3**to generate this template with the last used options.
3. Select the saved location and filename.
4. A message dialog will open, asking if you want to open the report in the default viewer once the generating process has completed.

#### INFO: Info

Info

- The next time you generate a report, a new menu will create and map the keyboard shortcuts until the keyboard shortcuts list is fully filled. The number of keyboard shortcuts is limited to five.

- If the keyboard shortcuts list is full, and the new template has been generated, the keyboard shortcuts will be reused.

The table below lists some of the keyboard shortcuts provided by Report Wizard Quick Print:

| Shortcut Key | Template Name |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + 1 | Tutorial 01 – Print Element |
| Alt + 2 | Tutorial 02 – Print Diagram |
| Alt + 3 | Tutorial 03 – Print Project |
| Alt + 4 | Tutorial 04 – Print Report Data |
| Alt + 5 | Tutorial 05 – Macro |

The table above shows that the Tutorial 01 – Print Element keyboard shortcut is the most outdated template, and the following report has been completed using the template. Report Wizard Quick Print will reuse the keyboard shortcut of the most outdated template.

The table below lists commonly re-used keyboard shortcuts:

| Shortcut Key | Template Name |
| --- | --- |
| Alt + 1 | Business Process Diagram |
| Alt + 2 | Tutorial 02 – Print Diagram |
| Alt + 3 | Tutorial 03 – Print Project |
| Alt + 4 | Tutorial 04 – Print Report Data |
| Alt + 5 | Tutorial 05 – Macro |

If the next template to be generated has already been in the Quick Print list, the list will not change.

#### Glossary

- Template A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.

#### More information about Report Wizard options

To learn more about Report Wizard environment options, you can visit [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Customizing environment options' space=''].

Report Wizard Quick Print will reuse the keyboard shortcut of the most outdated template.</p><p>The table below lists commonly re-used keyboard shortcuts:</p><table style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><colgroup><col /><col /></colgroup><tbody style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><th>Shortcut Key</th><th>Template Name</th></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 1 </td><td>Business Process Diagram</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 2</td><td>Tutorial 02 – Print Diagram</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 3 </td><td>Tutorial 03 – Print Project</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 4</td><td>Tutorial 04 – Print Report Data</td></tr><tr style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><td>Alt + 5</td><td>Tutorial 05 – Macro</td></tr></tbody></table><p>If the next template to be generated has already been in the Quick Print list, the list will not change.</p><h2>Glossary</h2><ul><li><strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">Template</span></strong><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><br />A file that serves as a starting point for a new report.<br /></span></li></ul><h2>More information about Report Wizard options</h2><p>To learn more about Report Wizard environment options, you can visit <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Customizing environment options" /></ac:link><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">.<br /></span></p>

## PAGE 00931: Report Wizard template language

- page_id: `189144485`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144485/Report+Wizard+template+language
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:47.731+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1266456373

1266456374

1266456374

Report Wizard is built on Velocity Engine. Knowledge of the Velocity Template Language and the Report Wizard Custom Language used within the Report Wizard template is necessary for understanding, editing, or creating templates.

1266456372

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189139695 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00932: Reporting Issues

- page_id: `189139695`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189139695/Reporting+Issues
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:36:19.124+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Getting started
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

1428101707

INLINE

1428101711

INLINE

1428137241

INLINE

**On this page**

5

1428101710

INLINE

INLINE

Need help? Send notifications of a problem, suggest improvements for our modeling tools, or ask about new features.

###### offlineReport an issue when the program is unresponsive

If you encounter an issue or the modeling tool becomes unresponsive, a separately executable tool is provided for analyzing the status of the process to aid in bug submission.In these situations, manually start the *submit_issue.exe* file (located in the *<modeling tool installation directory>\bin folder*).After you start *submit_issue.exe*, the**Report an Issue** dialog opens.

In this dialog, you can easily dump threads, memory heap, or memory histogram into files and provide those files when reporting an issue to the support team.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Report an Issue dialog in the offline mode

First of all, select the specific instance of the modeling tool to dump reports from. Then, click one of the following:

- **Dump Threads to Log File** - threads will be dumped into a Log file.
- **Dump Memory Heap to File** - memory heap will be dumped to a new .hprof file.
- **Dump Memory Histogram to File** - a memory histogram will be dumped to a new .txt file. To use the **Dump Memory Histogram to File** functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.

###### View and Submit Internal Errors

Internal errors are displayed as notifications by default. The internal error does not necessarily mean damage to your data but can be a reason for unexpected tool behavior. We highly recommend submitting internal errors to No Magic for further investigation. We strongly recommend attaching the log file. An internal error message appears at the bottom of the modeling tool.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Example of Internal Error message in the modeling tool

To view internal errors

To view internal errors you must open the **Internal Errors** dialog, using one of the two methods outlined below:

- Click the View internal errors button in the Notification Window.
- Click the notification icon on the status line.

To submit an error

1. Open the Internal Errors dialog.
2. Click the Export To File button. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
3. Specify the name and location of the error log file and click Export .
4. [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC'] and attach the exported error log file.

Internal errors will not be shown for 24 hours (or until you restart your modeling tool) after clicking the **Clear And Close** buttons.

To Display Internal Errors

1. From the Options menu, select Environment . The Environment options dialog will open.
2. In the General options group , click Internal Errors.
3. In the Internal Errors options list change the Display Internal Errors option value to true to display internal errors.

1428101705

INLINE

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Support_obsolete' space='NMDOC']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Configuration files' space='']
