Send notifications of a problem, suggest improvements for our modeling tools, or ask about new features. </span></p><h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="anchor" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5bb4ad24-062c-4a01-8ff1-2f0000496544"><ac:parameter ac:name="">offline</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro>Report an issue when the program is unresponsive</h4><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">If you encounter an issue or the modeling tool becomes unresponsive, a separately executable tool is provided for analyzing the status of the process to aid in bug submission. </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In these situations, manually start the <em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">submit_issue.exe</span></em> file (located in the <em>&lt;modeling tool installation directory&gt;\bin folder</em>). </span><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">After you start <em><span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">submit_issue.exe</span></em>, the </span><strong>Report an Issue</strong> dialog <span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">opens. </span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">In this dialog, you can easily dump threads, memory heap, or memory histogram into files and provide those files when reporting an issue to the support team.</span></p><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="report_an_issue_offline_dialog.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">The Report an Issue dialog in the offline mode</span></h6><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);">First of all, select the specific instance of the modeling tool to dump reports from. Then, click one of the following:</span></p><ul><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Dump Threads to Log File</strong> - threads will be dumped into a Log file.</span></li><li><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Dump Memory Heap to File</strong> - memory heap will be dumped to a new .hprof file.</span></li><li><p class="auto-cursor-target"><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"><strong>Dump Memory Histogram to File</strong> - a memory histogram will be dumped to a new .txt file. <br /></span></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="fd25920a-a29b-47bd-815e-fca56b11154a"><ac:rich-text-body><p>To use the <strong>Dump Memory Histogram to File</strong> functionality, the application must run with the JDK or JRE that comes with the installation.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></li></ul><h4>View and Submit Internal Errors</h4><p>Internal errors are displayed as notifications by default. The internal error does not necessarily mean damage to your data but can be a reason for unexpected tool behavior. We highly recommend submitting internal errors to No Magic for further investigation. We strongly recommend attaching the log file. An internal error message appears at the bottom of the modeling tool. </p><p style="text-align: center;"><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_error_message_.png" /></ac:image></span></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">Example of Internal Error message in the modeling tool</h6><p>To view internal errors</p><hr /><p>To view internal errors you must open the <strong>Internal Errors</strong> dialog, using one of the two methods outlined below:</p><ul><li>Click the <strong>View</strong> <strong>internal errors</strong> button in the Notification Window.</li><li>Click the <strong>notification</strong> icon on the status line.<br /><br /></li></ul><p>To submit an error</p><hr /><ol><li>Open the <strong>Internal Errors </strong>dialog.</li><li>Click the <strong>Export To File</strong> button.<br /><span class="confluence-embedded-file-wrapper"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="internal_errors.png" /></ac:image></span></li><li>Specify the name and location of the error log file and click<strong> Export</strong>.</li><li class="ancestor-link parent-link"><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Report an issue to the customer support]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> and attach the exported error log file. </li></ol><p>Internal errors will not be shown for 24 hours (or until you restart your modeling tool) after clicking the <strong>Clear And Close</strong> buttons.</p><p><br /></p><p>To Display Internal Errors</p><hr /><ol><li>From the <strong>Options</strong> menu, select <strong>Environment</strong>. The <strong>Environment options </strong>dialog will open.</li><li>In the <strong>General</strong> options group<strong>, </strong>click <strong>Internal Errors.</strong></li><li>In the <strong>Internal Errors</strong> options list change the <strong>Display Internal Errors</strong> option value to <em>true</em> to display internal errors.</li></ol></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="03742df4-e780-4c06-9cb7-879ea15dd4af"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">1428101705</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Support_obsolete" ri:space-key="NMDOC" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Configuration files" /></ac:link></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143831 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00933: Representation of Teamwork Cloud users

- page_id: `189143831`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143831/Representation+of+Teamwork+Cloud+users
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:58.613+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You need to have the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Permissions' space='TWCloud2024xR2']**List All Users** permission assigned to change the user representation.

You can customize how users are represented when, for example, they lock model elements by changing the value of the**Representation of Users**environment option.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Changing the user representation in the Teamwork Cloud project.

A *User Name* is shown by default in the following cases:

- the corresponding fields (e.g., Full name) are not filled in the Web Application Platform.
- the List All Users permission is not assigned to the user.

To choose how to represent users

1. In the main menu, click Options > Environment .
2. In the panel on the left, select Collaboration .
3. Under General , click Representation of Users . [ATTACHMENT filename='representation_of_users_environment_option.png']
4. Select to represent users by displaying one of the following:
  - User Name
  - Full Name
  - Email
5. When you are done, click OK .
6. Reload an open server project.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<strong>Environment</strong>.</li><li>In the panel on the left, select <strong>Collaboration</strong>.</li><li>Under <strong>General</strong>, click <strong>Representation of Users</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="representation_of_users_environment_option.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Select to represent users by displaying one of the following:<ul><li>User Name </li><li>Full Name</li><li>Email</li></ul></li><li>When you are done, click <strong>OK</strong>.</li><li><ac:inline-comment-marker ac:ref="3e47549c-9a73-4665-9f16-79a42f4344d8">Reload an open server project.</ac:inline-comment-marker> </li></ol>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189141859 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00934: Representing Use Case scenarios in Activity diagrams

- page_id: `189141859`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189141859/Representing+Use+Case+scenarios+in+Activity+diagrams
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:38:38.115+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > UML elements > Use Case > Use Case Scenario sketch
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

879905382

879905384

879905383

After you have created a Use Case scenario, you can represent this scenario in an Activity diagram. The following procedure describes how to represent the Use Case scenario in the Activity diagram and how to turn the automatic layout on or off in the Activity diagram when representing the Use Case scenario.

To represent a Use Case scenario in an Activity diagram

1. Open the Use Case Specification window and click the Use Case Scenario Sketch property group.
2. Click the Open Activity Diagram button [ATTACHMENT filename='open_and_update_button.png'] . The Activity diagram opens with the Use Case scenario represented on it.

You need to reopen the Activity diagram each time after you make modifications to the Use Case scenario in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space='']. Modifications to the Activity diagram where the Use Case scenario is represented automatically appear in the textual Use Case scenario.

For Use Cases having read-only accessibility, the use Case Scenario cannot be represented on the Activity diagram.

In the Activity diagram, all symbols are laid out automatically every time the diagram is opened.

To leave symbols in the same place while opening the Activity diagram after modifying the Use Case scenario in the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Specification window' space=''], change the **Layout use case scenario activity diagram**option value to *false*. This option appears in the **Project Options**dialog, in the **General project options**group.

Your modeling tool might not be able to interpret the Use Case scenario in some cases. These are the most common reasons why:

| There is no merge node or call behavior action after the element. |
| --- |
| There is no initial node in Activity. |
| There is no final node in Activity. |
| There are no outgoing control flows from the element. |
| There is no merge node after the element. |
| There are no outgoing flows from the element. |
| There is no alternative flow step after the element. |
| Cyclic scenario. |

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Use Case Scenario cannot be interpreted

879905381

This option appears in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">Project Options </strong>dialog, in the <strong style="line-height: 1.4285715;">General project options </strong>group.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><p><br /></p><p>Your modeling tool might not be able to interpret the Use Case scenario in some cases. <!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144854 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00935: Reset to defaults option

- page_id: `189144854`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144854/Reset+to+defaults+option
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:41:13.575+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Configuring Report Wizard > Report Wizard Environment options
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

Click the **Reset to Defaults** button to reset data to the default settings, see figure below.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Reset to Defaults button.

- The modeling tool allows you to configure the report engine settings in three levels: (i) Environment option, (ii) global config.xml , and (iii) template config.xml .
- The template config.xml configuration settings will override the environment option configuration settings, which will override the global config.xml configuration settings.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p>Click the <strong>Reset to Defaults</strong> button to reset data to the default settings, see figure below.</p><p style="margin-left: 30.0px;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="Reset to Defaults Button" ac:alt="Reset to Defaults Button"><ri:attachment ri:filename="ReportOption9.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-left: 30.0px;">The Reset to Defaults button.</h6><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6c6b50cc-101c-4966-a939-4cc55e249b80"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>The modeling tool allows you to configure the report engine settings in three levels: (i) Environment option, (ii) global <em>config.xml</em>, and (iii) template <em>config.xml</em>.</li><li>The template<em> config.xml</em> configuration settings will override the environment option configuration settings, which will override the global <em>config.xml</em> configuration settings.</li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144556 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00936: Resizing fixed-measurement images

- page_id: `189144556`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144556/Resizing+fixed-measurement+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:57.906+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050277646

2050277651

2050277644

**On this page**

43

2050277650

There are six methods to specify the resulting dimensions in terms of measurements to resize an image, for example, in inch (in), centimeter (cm), millimeter (mm), point (pt), or pixel (px)], and specifying the dot-per-inch (*DPI*) value. Every measurement parameter must be specified in a pair of quote marks.

###### **$image.setSize(image, measureWidth, measureHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in terms of measurements.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
| Parameter(s) | measureWidth | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | measureHeight | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, measureSize)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height in terms of measurement while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, measureSize, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The height of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, measureSize)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width in a term of measurement, while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, measureSize, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (a term of measurement), and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | measureSize | java.lang.String | The width of the image in a term of measurement. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setDPI(dotsPerInches)**

This method is used to set the resolution (dpi) of images. The default value is 96 dpi. The dpi value will only affect the methods that take inches, centimeters, and millimeters as their parameters.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | dotsPerInches | java.lang.Integer | Image resolution (dpi). |
| Return | - | - | - |

Use **$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘1.5in’, ‘2in’)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 1.5 inches and height to 2 inches. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set size function.

Use either **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’)** or **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, true)**, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set height function.

Use **$image.setHeight($diagram.image, ‘45mm’, false)**, for example, to resize the image’s height to 45 millimeters and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set height function.

Use **$image.setDPI(192)** and then **$image.setSize($diagram.image, ‘0.9in’, ‘1.1in’)**, for example, to set the resolution (dpi) of the image to 192 dots per inch, and then resize the image width to 0.9 inches and height to 1.1 inches. The resulting image size will become 173x211 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set DPI function.

Instead of using **$image.setDPI(192)**, if you set the resolution (dpi) to 144 using **$image.setDPI(144)**, the resulting image size will become 130x158 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set DPI function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144552 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00937: Resizing fixed-pixel images

- page_id: `189144552`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144552/Resizing+fixed-pixel+images
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:57.615+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Helper tools > Image tool
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

2050270131

2050270136

2050270129

**On this page**

43

2050270135

There are five methods to specify the resulting dimensions for an image to be resized in pixel size (the size parameters must be positive numbers or -1 number). If the value is -1, it will resize an image to the document paper dimensions. For example:

```text
$image.setWidth($diagram.image, -1)
```

As shown by the example, the image will be resized to a paper width while maintaining the aspect ratio. The value -1 applies only to certain template types such as *RTF*, *ODT*, and *DOCX*. If the value is -2, it will resize an image to document paper bounds if and only if image bounds are larger than paper bounds. For example:

```text
$image.setWidth($diagram.image, -2)
```

Using the value -2 also maintains the image aspect ratio. However, it can only be applied to certain template types such as *RTF*, *ODT*, and *DOCX*.

****

###### **$image.setSize(image, sizeWidth, sizeHeight)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to an exact size; the width and height in pixels.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | sizeWidth | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
|  | sizeHeight | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, size)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (in pixels), while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setHeight(image, size, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific height (in pixels) and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The height of the image in pixels. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, size)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (in pixels), while maintaining the image aspect ratio.

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

****

###### **$image.setWidth(image, size, keepRatio)**

Return an image icon for an element. This method is used to resize the image to a specific width (in pixels) and to specify whether the image aspect ratio is to be maintained or not (depending on the keepRatio parameter).

|  | Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Parameter(s) | image | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | An image object for the element icon. |
|  | size | java.lang.Integer | The width of the image in pixels. |
|  | keepRatio | java.lang.Boolean | A flag to maintain the image aspect ratio |
| Return | - | com.nomagic.magicreport.Image | The resized image object for the element icon. |

Use **$image.setSize($diagram.image, 200, 200)**, for example, to resize the image’s width and height to 200 pixels. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set size function.

Use either **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150)** or **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150, true)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 150 pixels and maintain the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set width function.

Use **$image.setWidth($diagram.image, 150, false)**, for example, to resize the image’s width to 150 pixels and ignore the image aspect ratio. The following photos show the result.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Results of using the image set width function.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142276 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00938: Resolving not synchronized elements

- page_id: `189142276`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142276/Resolving+not+synchronized+elements
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:39:08.532+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with model elements > Parameters synchronization
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

The active validation functionality marks the not synchronized elements in the Model Browser and highlights the shapes on the diagram pane. For more information about the active validation and solving the incorrect model, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space=''].

In addition, you can solve the broken parameters synchronization by using the **Synchronization** dialog.

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<ac:layout><ac:layout-section ac:type="two_right_sidebar"><ac:layout-cell><p>The active validation functionality marks the not synchronized elements in the Model Browser and highlights the shapes on the diagram pane. For more information about the active validation and solving the incorrect model, see <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Validation" /></ac:link>.</p><p>In addition, you can solve the broken parameters synchronization by using the <strong>Synchronization</strong> dialog.</p></ac:layout-cell><ac:layout-cell><p> </p></ac:layout-cell></ac:layout-section></ac:layout>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189140002 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00939: Resolving unconfirmed usages of packages

- page_id: `189140002`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189140002/Resolving+unconfirmed+usages+of+packages
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:16.177+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Working with projects > Project partitioning > Maintaining decomposed model integrity
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

#### CONTENT-LAYER: Model Usages

1020795580

#### CONTENT-COLUMN: Model Usages

1020795582

#### CONTENT-BLOCK: Model Usages

1020795581

For the overview of automated project usages and situations when they can become unconfirmed, see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Controlling dependency creation between used projects' space=''].

Unconfirmed project usages are caught by special [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Automated project usages' space=''] and displayed as a warning or error validation result. The severity depends on the exact model composition situation, but the solution methods are the same in both cases.

#### INFO: Model Usages

Model Usages

Model

#### WARNING: Model Usages

Model Usages

These recovered elements are in turn flagged as errors. Hence single unconfirmed usage can cause a lot of error validation results: one for the unconfirmed usage itself (rule abbreviation - NCAMU) and one for each recovered element (rule abbreviation - REF). In this situation, the unconfirmed usage result should be examined first, because solving it may automatically solve all other results.

There are two ways to resolve an unconfirmed used project usage. The usage can be either confirmed or rejected.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### Two ways for resolving unconfirmed used project usages.

###### Confirming usages

If the used project usage A → B is good and necessary according to the user policy, it can be confirmed, i.e., the user-defined usage is to be created instead of the current unconfirmed automated used project usage.

You can confirm an automated usage

- By using the specific command
- Manually

To confirm an automated usage by using the specific command

1. Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.
2. Select Confirm and use the used project into <used project_name> from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']**Used project** wizard opens. The required used project is already selected in the first step of the wizard.
3. Click Finish. The necessary user-defined used project usage will be created.

To confirm an automated usage manually

#### INFO: Example

Example

In this case, you can better control the usage creation process. So if you need, for example, to use not only a required used project but some other one that brings the required used project as a part of it, be advised to confirm the usage manually.

For example, let’s say there are three unconfirmed usages from the project *RadioSet* to used projects *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances.* Instead of confirming each usage, you can create just one user-defined usage *RadioSet → ElectricComponents*, since the used project *ElectricComponents* brings in *Capacitors, Inductances,* and *Resistances* as sub-used projects.

#### WARNING: Important!

Important!

However, this is the case when you must be sure that the user-defined usage will be created in the right place.

1. In the main menu, click Options > Used Projects . The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Managing used projects' space='']**U****sed Projects** dialog opens.
2. Select a used project that is the usage target resource.
3. Click the Used project button. The [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']**Used Project** wizard opens.
4. Follow the steps of the usual procedure (select a required used project and finish or continue to the second wizard step to provide more configuration options for used project usage and then finish).

****

**Rejecting usages**

If the usage A → B is not good according to the user policy, it must be rejected and removed.

You can reject an automated usage

- By using the specific command (for removing underlying model level references)
- Manually (by removing or redirecti ng underlying model level references to different elements)

To reject an automated usage by using the specific command

#### INFO: Model Usages

Model Usages

Use this way to reject a model-level usage that, for example, was created inadvertently.

1. Right-click the automated usage in the validation results.
2. Select Remove underlying model references from the shortcut menu (see the preceding figure). All underlying model-level references (the ones that cause this automated usage) will be removed.

Clear Recovered Element Usages

To remove an automated usage manually

In more complex cases, you may want to address each usage individually. Use dependency analysis means to examine what dependencies are there and review them on a case-by-case basis (see [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Displaying related elements' space=''], [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Dependency Matrix' space=''], and so forth).

**Useful commands for preparing to resolve unconfirmed used project usages**

When you need to explore your project composition in detail before attempting to resolve unconfirmed usages, use the following commands for navigating:

- Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Used project management' space='']
- Open [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Resource Usage Map' space='TWCloud2024xR2'] for collaboration projects

1020807231

**Related pages**

- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Analyzing usages and dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Identifying Package Dependencies' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Using other projects in a project' space='']
- [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Validation' space='']

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189142488 space=MD2024xR2 version=2 -->
## PAGE 00940: Resource Manager

- page_id: `189142488`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189142488/Resource+Manager
- version_number: 2
- version_date: `2024-10-06T19:45:41.810+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Tools
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

7929296

INLINE

7929299

INLINE

319023586

**On this page**

**43**

7929297

INLINE

Resource Manager functionality allows you to manage local resources (installed with a modeling tool or downloaded separately) and the resources available on the server/shared repository.

With the Resource Manager, you can manage different types of resources, such as Profiles, Plugins, Templates, Language resources, Case studies/Samples, Custom diagrams, and others.

The Resource Manager functionality allows you to:

- Install the resources
- Update the resources
- Distribute needed resources internally (including the company's developed resources)
- Check dependency of a resource

The **Resource/Plugin Manager** helps you manage the resources. You can add the resources to your modeling tool from the following locations:

- Local file system/network share
- Webserver

##### Working with the Resource/Plugin Manager

To open the Resource/Plugin Manager dialog

- From the **Help** menu, select **Resource/Plugin Manager**. [IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### The Resource/Plugin manager dialog.

To add resources from an *.rdzip* file on a local file system, network share, or a web server

The *core.rdzip* file containing all available resources is added automatically to the **Resource/Plugin Manager** by default.

1. In the Resource Location section, click the [...] button. The Manage Resource Locations dialog opens. Click one of the following:
  1. Add button to add an .rdzip file from a local file system or network share.
  2. Add URL button to add an .rdzip file from a web server. [IMAGE alt='' src='']
2. The file selection/URL dialog opens. Specify the location/URL of the *.rdzip* file and click **Open**.
3. Click OK . The resources of the file are loaded.
4. Select the check box of the resource you want to install.
5. Click the Download/Install button to install the selected resource. [ATTACHMENT filename='Install_a_plugin_through_the_pre-bundled_plugin_file.png']
6. Click Close and restart your modeling tool.

To add resources froma *.zip* file on a local file system or network share

1. Click the Import button. The file selection dialog opens.
2. Specify the location of the *.zip* file and click **Open**. The plugin is extracted and installed automatically.
3. Click **Close** and restart your modeling tool. 
**[IMAGE alt='' src='']**

To browse added resource locations

1. In the Resource/Plugin Manager dialog, click on the Resource Location selection.
2. Select the resource location from the drop-down menu. The resources are added to the Resource/Plugin Manager from the added resource file. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_manager_location_drop-down.png']

To remove a resource location

1. In the Resource Location section, click the [...] button. The Manage Resource Locations dialog opens.
2. Select the resource location you want to remove.
3. Click the Remove button. Click OK . [IMAGE alt='' src='']

****

To uninstall a resource

1. Select the check box of the resource you want to uninstall.
2. Click the Remove button.
3. Click Close and restart the modeling tool. [ATTACHMENT filename='uninstall_a_resource.png']

To see the resource detailsand/or check the dependency of a resource

1. Select the check box of the resource whose details you want to view.
2. Click Details . The dialog expands with the resource description at the bottom. [ATTACHMENT filename='resource_manager_details.png']
3. If the resource has dependencies, they are displayed in the Required compartment. [ATTACHMENT filename='check_the_dependency_of_a_resource.png']

##### Adding resources from a file

The pre-bundled resource distribution file *core*.*rdzip* is bundled with every modeling tool. It is located at < *installation_directory>/data/resourcemanager/distribution* >. The file is automatically added to the **Resource/Plugin Manager**by default.

There are multiple ways to install resources using the **Resource/Plugin Manager**, which depends on your needs and preferences. See the image below.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Visualization of the Resource/Plugin Manager workflow.

To learn more about plugin installation options, see the [CONFLUENCE_PAGE title='Installing modeling tools and plugins' space='IL2024xR2'] page.

##### Distributing resources using *.rdzip*

Once you have the resource distribution file (*.rdzip*), you can share resources internally, e.g., on a company level.

The resource distribution file (.*rdzip*) contains the resources and their descriptor file. If you are using the web server for resource distribution, make sure that the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*) is extracted into the intended server location.

##### Building your own custom resources file

You can bundle your resources into one *.rdzip* file and then distribute that file on a local file system, network share, or web server.

To build the resources file, you need to install the **Development Tools** plugin. The **Development Tools** plugincan be downloaded individually and then installed using **Resource/Plugin Manager**, or it can be found in the resource distribution file (.*rdzip*).

7929295

INLINE

The resources of the file are loaded.</li><li>Select the check box of the resource you want to install.</li><li>Click the <strong>Download/Install</strong> button to install the selected resource.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="Install_a_plugin_through_the_pre-bundled_plugin_file.png" /></ac:image></li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> and restart your modeling tool.</li></ol><p><span style="color: rgb(0,0,0);"> <br /></span></p><p><span>To add resources from</span><span> </span>a <em style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">.zip</em> <span> file on a local file system or network share</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Click the <strong>Import</strong> button. The file selection dialog opens.</li><li><p>Specify the location of the <em>.zip</em> file and click <strong>Open</strong>. The plugin is extracted and installed automatically.</p></li><li><p>Click <strong>Close</strong> and restart your modeling tool.<br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="import_resource.png" /></ac:image> <br /></strong></p></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To browse added resource locations</p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong> dialog, click on the <strong>Resource Location</strong> selection.</li><li>Select the resource location from the drop-down menu. The resources are added to the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager </strong>from the added resource file.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_manager_location_drop-down.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p><span> <span>To remove a resource location</span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>In the <strong>Resource Location</strong> section, click the [...] button. The <strong>Manage Resource Locations</strong> dialog opens.</li><li>Select the resource location you want to remove.</li><li>Click the <strong>Remove</strong> button. Click <strong>OK</strong>.<br /><strong> <ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="remove_a_resource_location.png" /></ac:image> <br /></strong></li></ol><p><strong> <br /></strong></p><p><span>To uninstall a resource</span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select the check box of the resource you want to uninstall.</li><li>Click the <strong>Remove</strong> button.</li><li>Click <strong>Close</strong> and restart the modeling tool.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="uninstall_a_resource.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><p><br /></p><p>To see the resource details<span><span> and/or check the dependency of a resource</span> </span></p><hr /><ol><li>Select the check box of the resource whose details you want to view.</li><li>Click <strong>Details</strong>. The dialog expands with the resource description at the bottom.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_manager_details.png" /></ac:image></li><li>If the resource has dependencies, they are displayed in the <strong>Required</strong> compartment.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="check_the_dependency_of_a_resource.png" /></ac:image></li></ol><h3>Adding resources from a file</h3><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="09363c79-db5b-44f3-ab1b-04b7f56ab351"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The pre-bundled resource distribution file <em>core</em>.<em>rdzip</em> is bundled with every modeling tool. It is located at <span>&lt;</span> <em>installation_directory&gt;/data/resourcemanager/distribution</em> <span>&gt;. The file is automatically added to the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager </strong>by default.</span></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>There are multiple ways to install resources using the <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>, which depends on your needs and preferences. See the image below.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;margin-bottom: 30.0px;"><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="resource_manager_visualization.png" /></ac:image> <br />Visualization of the Resource/Plugin Manager workflow.</h6><p>To learn more about plugin installation options, see the <ac:link><ri:page ri:space-key="IL2024xR2" ri:content-title="Installing modeling tools and plugins" /><ac:plain-text-link-body><![CDATA[Installing plugins]]></ac:plain-text-link-body></ac:link> page.</p><h3>Distributing resources using <em style="font-size: 16.0px;letter-spacing: -0.006em;">.rdzip </em></h3><p>Once you have the resource distribution file (<em>.rdzip</em>), you can share resources internally, e.g., on a company level. </p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b6e1c583-d6c6-4aa4-9584-6692569bb3a2"><ac:rich-text-body><p>The resource distribution file (.<em>rdzip</em>) contains the resources and their descriptor file. If you are using the web server for resource distribution, make sure that the resource distribution file (.<em>rdzip</em>) is extracted into the intended server location.</p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Building your own custom resources file</h3><p>You can bundle your resources into one <em>.rdzip</em> file and then distribute that file on a local file system, network share, or web server.</p><p>To build the resources file, you need to install the <strong>Development Tools</strong> plugin. <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">The</span> <strong>Development Tools </strong> <span style="color: rgb(62,63,64);">plugin </span>can be downloaded individually and then installed using <strong>Resource/Plugin Manager</strong>, or it can be found in the resource distribution file (.<em>rdzip</em>).</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="info" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="06f3c676-7b41-4b90-8f00-3a0c0f02e65f"><ac:rich-text-body>For more information about building the resource distribution file, read <ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building a resource distribution file" /></ac:link>.</ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="974e6b48-d65f-4339-aa9f-f6e4bb8c82be"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">7929295</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="atlassian-macro-output-type">INLINE</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p class="auto-cursor-target"><strong>Related pages</strong></p><ul><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Distributing custom diagrams" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Distributing Resources" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Distributing Profiles" /></ac:link></li><li><ac:link><ri:page ri:content-title="Building a resource distribution file" /></ac:link><br /><br /></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p class="auto-cursor-target"><br /></p>
````

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189144508 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00941: Retrieving Slot Values

- page_id: `189144508`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189144508/Retrieving+Slot+Values

- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:50.021+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Report Wizard > Template variables
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

32

126605376

Instead of using Stereotype and Tag, some projects may use Instance Specification and Slot to define new attributes of an element.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
Instance Specification and Slot defining element attributes.

You can retrieve a slot value by either using a normal UML Specification structure, or by using a slot property.

#### Using normal UML Specification to Retrieve Slot Values

For example, to retrieve a Name slot value type:

```text
0)
				#set($v = $slot.value.get(0).text)
			#else
				#set($v = "")
			#end
			Slot value is $v
		#end
	#end
#end]]>
```

#### Using Slot Property to Retrieve Slot Values

You can use a slot property as a shortcut to get the following information from a slot.

##### Retrieving slot information from an element

The following code retrieves slot information from an element.

```text

```

In which:

- $element is an element.
- slots is a property for getting slot variables.

For example, if you want to obtain a collection of slot variables of an element, type:

```text

```

###### Retrieving slot information from an element and a classifier’s name

The following code retrieves slot information from an element and a classifier’s name.

```text

```

Where:

- $element is an element.
- slots is a property for getting the slots variable.
- ClassifierName is the classifier’s name.

The code returns:

- A list of slot variables whose classifier names are ClassifierName.
- If there are multiple classifiers that match the ClassifierName, the code returns the slot property of the first classifier.
- If there is no classifier that matches the ClassifierName, the code returns null.

For example, if you want to retrieve all slot values from the “Communicate” classifier, type:

```text

```

###### Retrieving slot value from an element, a classifier’s Name, and a defining feature name

```text

```

Where:

- **$element** is an element.
- **slots** is a property for getting slots variable.
- **ClassifierName** is the classifier’s name.
- **DefiningFeatureName** is the name of a slot.

The code returns:

- A slot value whose name is DefiningFeatureName and a classifier whose name is ClassifierName.
- If the slot multiplicity is < =1 (less than or equal to 1), it will return the value in ValueSpecification.
- If the slot multiplicity is > 1, it will return the value in array of ValueSpecification.

For example, if you want to retrieve the values of a slot whose name is “Address” and those of a classifier whose name is “Communicate”, type:

```text

```

- ClassifierName and DefiningFeatureName are case-sensitive properties.
- You can use: $report.getSlotValue($element, ClassifierName, DefiningFeatureName) to retrieve the slot value. See $report below for further details.

##### Code examples to retrieve slot values

This section explains how to get the value of an instance slot from a classifier. The figure below shows the elements of a slot variable in the Containment tree. You can see the values of the element **Member** in the tree, or you can right-click on it and open the Specification dialog to see the values in the dialog.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
An example of elements in the Containment tree.

The following figure shows the Instance Specification dialog of the element Member of the slot variable. As you can see in the dialog, the element Member of the slot variable contains slot names and values.

###### [IMAGE alt='' src=''] 
All slots of Instance Specification.

The following are examples of using the code to retrieve a value of a slot.

Example 1.

If you want to print the value of a slot **Address** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Communicate,**you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity = 1.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is :

```text

```

Example 2.

If you want to print the value of a slot **Telephone** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Communicate**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is > 1, [1..*].

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 3.

If you want to print the value of a slot Name of the element Member from the classifier Personal Data, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity = 1.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 4.

If you want to print the value of a slot **Nickname** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Personal Data**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is > 1, [0..*].

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 5.

If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot **Name** of the element **Member** from the classifier**Personal Data**, you can use the following template code.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 6.

If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot **Nickname** of the element **Member** from the classifier **Personal Data**, you can use the following template code.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 7.

If you want to print the names and values of all classifiers and the slot properties of all instance elements, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

```text

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1d06e539-0d58-446f-833f-a51625c663e5"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605373</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="096acd2b-02ca-46a2-835c-c2a3d730d9bc"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605376</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4154f92b-3c0f-4bb0-be68-81013ec6f373"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605371</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="bf58dd29-bc69-4a3c-bf4d-8ece4bda8e35"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d39e1f93-7c8a-4fa2-a5f2-5690a286f3d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">126605376</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p>Instead of using Stereotype and Tag, some projects may use Instance Specification and Slot to define new attributes of an element.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Ins_spec_slot.png" /></ac:image><br />Instance Specification and Slot defining element attributes.</h6><p>You can retrieve a slot value by either using a normal UML Specification structure, or by using a slot property.</p><h2>Using normal UML Specification to Retrieve Slot Values</h2><p>For example, to retrieve a Name slot value type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3b4989e1-d02f-4ff1-b5ed-8b8ad7f9159a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
	#foreach($slot in $instance.slot)
		#if($slot.definingFeature.name == 'Name')
			#if($slot.value.size() > 0)
				#set($v = $slot.value.get(0).text)
			#else
				#set($v = "")
			#end
			Slot value is $v
		#end
	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using Slot Property to Retrieve Slot Values</h2><p>You can use a slot property as a shortcut to get the following information from a slot.</p><h3>Retrieving slot information from an element</h3><p>The following code retrieves slot information from an element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b39cf1fe-654a-4b5e-b671-91481733c9d0"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.slots]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>In which:</p><ul><li>$element is an element.</li><li>slots is a property for getting slot variables.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to obtain a collection of slot variables of an element, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3eac2eb2-b0cc-47f3-9ed1-c8b041d4f4d8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($slot in $instanceSpecification.slots)
	$slot.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Retrieving slot information from an element and a classifier’s name</h4><p>The following code retrieves slot information from an element and a classifier’s name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c455d57f-b3d8-4973-b2be-72be49ccc4b6"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.slots.ClassifierName
or
$element.slots.get("ClassifierName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Where:</p><ul><li>$element is an element.</li><li>slots is a property for getting the slots variable.</li><li>ClassifierName is the classifier’s name.</li></ul><p>The code returns:</p><ul><li>A list of slot variables whose classifier names are ClassifierName.</li><li>If there are multiple classifiers that match the ClassifierName, the code returns the slot property of the first classifier.</li><li>If there is no classifier that matches the ClassifierName, the code returns null.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to retrieve all slot values from the “Communicate” classifier, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cb72fff2-3360-41d6-ac20-1765a8cf551c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$instanceSpecification.slots.Communicate
$instanceSpecification.slots.get("Communicate")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h4>Retrieving slot value from an element, a classifier’s Name, and a defining feature name</h4><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="5432ff95-f5fb-4799-8564-bcc19ee954c2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.slots.ClassifierName.DefiningFeatureName

or

$element.slots.get("ClassifierName").get("DefiningFeatureName")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Where:</p><ul><li><em><strong>$element</strong></em> <br />is an element.</li><li><em><strong>slots</strong></em> <br />is a property for getting slots variable.</li><li><em><strong>ClassifierName</strong></em> <br />is the classifier’s name.</li><li><em><strong>DefiningFeatureName</strong></em> <br />is the name of a slot.</li></ul><p>The code returns:</p><ul><li>A slot value whose name is DefiningFeatureName and a classifier whose name is ClassifierName.</li><li>If the slot multiplicity is &lt; =1 (less than or equal to 1), it will return the value in ValueSpecification.</li><li>If the slot multiplicity is &gt; 1, it will return the value in array of ValueSpecification.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to retrieve the values of a slot whose name is “Address” and those of a classifier whose name is “Communicate”, type:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4bf54286-94b7-4664-a620-cf6d758e3dd8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$instanceSpecification.slots.Communicate.Address
$instanceSpecification.slots.Communicate.get("Address")
$instanceSpecification.slots.get("Communicate").Address
$instanceSpecification.slots.get("Communicate").get("Address")]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="tip" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e75cb621-b6ac-4177-9752-236ea0dea673"><ac:rich-text-body><ul><li>ClassifierName and DefiningFeatureName are case-sensitive properties.</li><li><p>You can use:</p><pre>$report.getSlotValue($element, ClassifierName, DefiningFeatureName)</pre><p>to retrieve the slot value. See $report below for further details.</p></li></ul></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Code examples to retrieve slot values</h3><p>This section explains how to get the value of an instance slot from a classifier. The figure below shows the elements of a slot variable in the Containment tree. As you can see in the dialog, the element Member of the slot variable contains slot names and values.</p><h6 style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center" ac:title="All Slots of Instance Specification" ac:alt="All Slots of Instance Specification"><ri:attachment ri:filename="insspec_slot.png" /></ac:image><br />All slots of Instance Specification.</h6><p>The following are examples of using the code to retrieve a value of a slot.</p><p><br /></p><p>Example 1.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot <strong>Address</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Communicate, </strong>you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity = 1.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0b922420-d26b-442a-bda9-f81a968391d4"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $instance.slots.Communicate.Address
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is :</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3c2639a0-e006-4173-ab7d-371915f632d1"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is US]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 2.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot <strong>Telephone</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Communicate</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is &gt; 1, [1..*].</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="219a764c-2699-401d-a7e2-bdf9865f469c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $instance.slots.Communicate.Telephone
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ea85fd57-1362-4570-91e9-26604f9f5efc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 3.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot Name of the element Member from the classifier Personal Data, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity = 1.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="835d8231-1391-43a5-9daf-bf5a9a91e8f8"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $instance.slots.get("Personal Data").get("Name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="db1e3d50-e33f-4396-b2fc-4cc92e690571"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[	Slot value is Peter]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Example 4.</p><p>If you want to print the value of a slot <strong>Nickname</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Personal Data</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut. Note that the slot multiplicity is &gt; 1, [0..*].</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="011960e3-c451-4bd2-972a-b0c80d35d260"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $instance.slots.get("Personal Data").get("Nickname")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ba67d40e-fdee-48c3-ba22-15479f2c2610"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is [Peet, P]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 5.</p><p>If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot <strong>Name</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier<strong> Personal Data</strong>, you can use the following template code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="d5202526-2083-47a6-b173-021d5420ae96"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $report.getSlotValue($instance, "Personal Data", "Name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b5170855-ce3d-472d-a861-c14836fe612f"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Slot value is [Peter]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 6.</p><p>If you want to use $Report method to print the value of a slot <strong>Nickname</strong> of the element <strong>Member</strong> from the classifier <strong>Personal Data</strong>, you can use the following template code.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd460782-44cb-49a4-86ff-66ee419a0224"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  	Slot value is $report.getSlotValue($instance, "Personal Data", "Nickname")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3217cea0-b93a-4f1b-9c06-ca007a8c966e"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[ Slot value is [Peet, P]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 7.</p><p>If you want to print the names and values of all classifiers and the slot properties of all instance elements, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="ae0ce5a7-fc3f-4c91-950b-a438ca841ce2"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($instance in $InstanceSpecification)
  #foreach($classifier in $instance.slots)
	Classifier name: $classifier.name
	Slots:
      #foreach($slot in $classifier)
	   $slot.name : $slot.value
      #end
 
  #end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><p><br /></p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4225d8a6-12a2-4e96-a28b-386d75d8d4ab"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Classifier name: Personal Data
	Slots:
  	   Name : Peter
  	   Age : 20
  	   Nickname : [Peet, P]
  	   Gender : Male
  	   ID : 1234
 
	Classifier name: Communicate
	Slots:
  	   Telephone : [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]
  	   E-mail : [a@example.com, b@example.com]
  	   Address : US
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>
````

###### An example of elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree and all tags of a Class.

#### Using normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values

The sample code of usage of normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values is as follows:

```text
0)
        		#set($t = $tag.value.get(0))
        	#else
            	#set($t = "")
        	#end
        	Tag value is $t
        #end
	#end
#end]]>
```

#### Using tag properties to retrieve tag values

You can use tag properties as a shortcut to get the following information from tags.

##### Retrieving tag information from elements

The following code retrieves tag information from an element.

```text

```

Given that:

- $element is an element.
- tags is a property for getting the tag variable.

For example, if you want to obtain a collection of tag properties of an element, type the following line:

```text

```

##### Retrieving tag information from elements and stereotype names

The following code retrieves tag information from an element and a stereotype name.

```text

```

or

```text

```

Given that:

- $element is an element.
- tags is a property for getting the tag variable.
- StereotypeName is the name of a stereotype.

The code returns a list of tag variables whose tag stereotype names are **StereotypeName**.

#### NOTE: Note

Note

- If there are multiple tag stereotype names that match StereotypeName , the code returns the tag property of the first tag stereotype name.
- If there is no tag stereotype name that matches StereotypeName , the code returns null.

For example, if you want to retrieve all tag values from the **Communicate** tag stereotype, type the following:

```text

```

`or`

```text

```

##### Retrieving tag values from elements, tag stereotype names, and tag names

Type one of the following lines:

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

```text

```

Given that:

- $element is an element.
- tags is a property for getting the tag variable.
- StereotypeName is the name of a stereotype.
- TagName is the name of a tag.

The code returns the following:

- A tag value named TagName and a tag stereotype named StereotypeName .
- If the tag multiplicity is < =1 (less than or equal to 1), it will return a value.
- If the tag multiplicity is > 1, it will return an array of values.

For example, if you want to retrieve the values of a tag named **Address** and those of a tag stereotype named **Communicate**, type the following lines:

```text

```

#### NOTE: Note

Note

**StereotypeName** and **TagName** are case-sensitive properties.

#### Code examples to retrieve tag values

This section explains how to get the value of a tag from a class. The figure below shows the elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree. You can see the values of the **Member** element in the tree, or you can right-click it and select **Specification** to open the **Specification** dialog to see the values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### An example of elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree.

The following figure shows the **Specification** dialog of the **Member** element of the tag variable. The **Member** element of the tag variable contains tag names and values.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

###### All tags of a Class.

The following code examples are for retrieving the value of a tag.

Example 1:

If you want to print the **Address** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Communicate stereotype,**you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity *=* *1*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 2:

If you want to print the **Telephone** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Communicate stereotype**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity is *> 1, [1..*]*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 3:

If you want to print the **Name** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Personal Data**stereotype, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity *=* *1*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 4:

If you want to print the **Nickname** tag value of the **Member** element from the **Personal Data stereotype**, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

Note that the tag multiplicity is *> 1, [0..*]*.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

Example 5:

If you want to print the names and values of all stereotypes and the tag properties of all class elements, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.

```text

```

The output of the code shown above is:

```text

```

<ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-layer" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="2d26bfa2-8c6a-428d-81e6-900e0a79980f"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887862</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-column" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="f5b88173-958f-4b73-a833-ff158e3bddbf"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887866</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="c0f684f8-4123-4c24-a9d9-49a7a5b57f5a"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887858</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p><strong>On this page</strong></p><p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="toc" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="b48e59ae-c7c8-4a44-88df-4753946893e7"><ac:parameter ac:name="maxLevel">3</ac:parameter><ac:parameter ac:name="minLevel">2</ac:parameter></ac:structured-macro></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><ac:structured-macro ac:name="content-block" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="71ad8cc2-a627-499c-aec0-f35f887fc2de"><ac:parameter ac:name="id">556887865</ac:parameter><ac:rich-text-body><p style="text-align: center;"><ac:image ac:align="center"><ri:attachment ri:filename="Tag Values.png" /></ac:image></p><h6 style="text-align: center;">An example of elements of a tag variable in the Containment tree and all tags of a Class.</h6><h2>Using normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values</h2><p>The sample code of usage of normal UML specifications to retrieve tag values is as follows:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1f051079-0cde-4e27-b32b-acee013cb77c"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
	#foreach($tag in $class.taggedValue)
		#if($tag.tagDefinition.name == "Name")
			#if($tag.value.size() > 0)
        		#set($t = $tag.value.get(0))
        	#else
            	#set($t = "")
        	#end
        	Tag value is $t
        #end
	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h2>Using tag properties to retrieve tag values</h2><p>You can use tag properties as a shortcut to get the following information from tags.</p><h3>Retrieving tag information from elements</h3><p>The following code retrieves tag information from an element.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="20a664f6-a7b1-449f-b897-bd9d19316188"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[$element.tags]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>Given that:</p><ul><li><strong>$element</strong> is an element.</li><li><strong>tags</strong> is a property for getting the tag variable.</li></ul><p>For example, if you want to obtain a collection of tag properties of an element, type the following line:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="3437bad3-8dd8-4e06-bedb-3dc54d781b16"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($tagProperty in $class.tags)
    $tagProperty.name
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><h3>Retrieving tag information from elements and stereotype names</h3><p>The following code retrieves tag information from an element and a stereotype name.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="85987d6e-0b26-48ed-a718-2514a8571703"><ac:parameter     Tag value is $class.tags.Communicate.Address
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="84eed87e-6920-453d-b013-673b42886303"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is US]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 2:</p><p>If you want to print the <strong>Telephone</strong> tag value of the <strong>Member</strong> element from the <strong>Communicate stereotype</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><p>Note that the tag multiplicity is <em>&gt; 1, [1..*]</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="6e632674-c73e-4b80-aa4c-7787c2b15ebc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
    Tag value is $class.tags.Communicate.Telephone
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="557721da-40ab-4d0b-b890-2374e914f29b"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 3:</p><p>If you want to print the <strong>Name</strong> tag value of the <strong>Member</strong> element from the <strong>Personal Data </strong>stereotype, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><p>Note that the tag multiplicity <em>=</em> <em>1</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="1eed33e1-343b-43bc-81e0-0e29999b3737"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
    Tag value is $class.tags.get("Personal Data").get("Name")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="4997521f-3e20-4bf0-8df9-d56686eb0dfb"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is Peter]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 4:</p><p>If you want to print the <strong>Nickname</strong> tag value of the <strong>Member</strong> element from the <strong>Personal Data stereotype</strong>, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><p>Note that the tag multiplicity is <em>&gt; 1, [0..*]</em>.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="cd2e623e-a161-4716-8579-656959277b4a"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
    Tag value is $class.tags.get("Personal Data").get("Nickname")
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="412e8b35-348c-4ba7-9fc9-9daf722145dc"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Tag value is [Peet, P]]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>Example 5:</p><p>If you want to print the names and values of all stereotypes and the tag properties of all class elements, you can use the following template code as a shortcut.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="0eca2d30-f2cd-4d24-aff4-2c9fb8ce53f3"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[#foreach($class in $Class)
	#foreach($tagProperty in $class.tags)
    	Stereotype name: $tagProperty.name
    	Tags:
      		#foreach($tag in $tagProperty)
       			$tag.name : $tag.value
      		#end
  	#end
#end]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p>The output of the code shown above is:</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="code" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="36c598e4-9fe1-44f6-aac7-a615af43a442"><ac:parameter ac:name="language">text</ac:parameter><ac:plain-text-body><![CDATA[Stereotype name: Communicate
Tags:
	Address : US
	Telephone : [0899999999, 0811111111, 02222222]
	E-mail : [a@example.com, b@example.com]
Stereotype name: Personal Data
Tags:
 	Gender : Male
	ID : 1234
	Age : 20
	Name : Peter
	Nickname : [Peet, P]
]]></ac:plain-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro>

<!--NOMAGIC_PAGE id=189143901 space=MD2024xR2 version=1 -->
## PAGE 00943: Returning to Merge dialog

- page_id: `189143901`
- space_key: `MD2024xR2`
- source_url: https://docs.nomagic.com/spaces/MD2024xR2/pages/189143901/Returning+to+Merge+dialog
- version_number: 1
- version_date: `2024-10-03T20:40:30.679+02:00`
- ancestors: MagicDraw Documentation > User Guide > Collaborative modeling > Using Teamwork Cloud > Model merge in Teamwork Cloud
- labels: []

### NORMALIZED CONTENT

You can return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes, e.g., rejected the element creation, by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.

Returning to the **Merge** dialog is only possible if you have not committed the changes to the server or reloaded the server project yet.

Also, you need to discard the changes you have already introduced to the project before you can return to the **Merge** dialog.

[IMAGE alt='' src='']

To return to the Merge dialog

- Click Return to Merge in the notification window that appears after clicking Finish Merging in the Merged Result panel. [ATTACHMENT filename='model_successfully_merged.png']

Alternatively, you can return to the **Merge** dialog by performing the merge operation again. This works only if the project has not been reloaded or committed to the server yet.

To initiate the merge operation

1. Do one of the following:
  - In the main menu, go to Collaborate > Merge From . [ATTACHMENT filename='collaborate_merge_from.png']

- 
  - In the main menu, go to Tools > Project Merge . [ATTACHMENT filename='tools_project_merge.png']

### EXACT CONFLUENCE STORAGE SOURCE

````html
<p class="auto-cursor-target">You can return to the previously performed merge operation if you notice that you have accepted or rejected the changes, e.g., rejected the element creation, by mistake and need to change your merge decisions.</p><ac:structured-macro ac:name="note" ac:schema-version="1" ac:macro-id="e4a65075-8897-4973-b77d-b2caf4cdac41"><ac:rich-text-body><p>Returning to the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog is only possible if you have not committed the changes to the server or reloaded the server project yet.</p><p>Also, you need to discard the changes you have already introduced to the project before you can return to the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog.</p><p><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="discard_changes_to_return_to_merge_dialog.png" /></ac:image></p></ac:rich-text-body></ac:structured-macro><p><br /></p><p>To return to the Merge dialog</p><hr /><ul><li><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Click </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Return to Merge</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> in the notification window that appears after clicking </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Finish Merging</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> in the </span><strong style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;">Merged Result</strong><span style="letter-spacing: 0.0px;"> panel.</span><br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="model_successfully_merged.png" /></ac:image><br /><br /></li></ul><p>Alternatively, you can return to the <strong>Merge</strong> dialog by performing the merge operation again. This works only if the project has not been reloaded or committed to the server yet.</p><p>To initiate the merge operation</p><hr /><ol><li>Do one of the following:<ul><li>In the main menu, go to<strong> Collaborate</strong> &gt;<strong> Merge From</strong>.<br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="collaborate_merge_from.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ol><ul><li style="list-style-type: none;background-image: none;"><ul><li>In the main menu, go to <strong>Tools</strong> &gt; <strong>Project Merge</strong>. <br /><ac:image><ri:attachment ri:filename="tools_project_merge.png" /></ac:image></li></ul></li></ul>
````
